provides the set of paths to show at /\\n154: \\tlistedPathProvider routes.ListedPathProvider\\n155: \\n156: \\t// DiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an unaggregated form.\\n157: \\tDiscoveryGroupManager discovery.GroupManager\\n158: \\n159: \\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\\n160: \\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\\n161: \\n162: \\t// PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager serves /apis aggregated from all peer apiservers.\\n163: \\tPeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager discoveryendpoint.PeerAggregatedResourceManager\\n164: \\n165: \\t// AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager serves /api in an aggregated form.\\n166: \\tAggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\\n167: \\n168: \\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI if these configs are non-nil.\\n169: \\topenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\\n170: \\n171: \\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI V3 if these configs are non-nil.\\n172: \\topenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\\n173: \\n174: \\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation indicates not to install the OpenAPI handler\\n175: \\t// during PrepareRun.\\n176: \\t// Set this to true when the specific API Server has its own OpenAPI handler\\n177: \\t// (e.g. kube-aggregator)\\n178: \\tskipOpenAPIInstallation bool\\n179: \\n180: \\t// OpenAPIVersionedService controls the /openapi/v2 endpoint, and can be used to update the served spec.\\n181: \\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\\n182: \\tOpenAPIVersionedService *handler.OpenAPIService\\n183: \\n184: \\t// OpenAPIV3VersionedService controls the /openapi/v3 endpoint and can be used to update the served spec.\\n185: \\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\\n186: \\tOpenAPIV3VersionedService *handler3.OpenAPIService\\n187: \\n188: \\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the restful container endpoints.\\n189: \\t// It is set during PrepareRun.\\n190: \\tStaticOpenAPISpec *spec.Swagger\\n191: \\n192: \\t// PostStartHooks are each called after the server has started listening, in a separate go func for each\\n193: \\t// with no guarantee of ordering between them.  The map key is a name used for error reporting.\\n194: \\t// It may kill the process with a panic if it wishes to by returning an error.\\n195: \\tpostStartHookLock      sync.Mutex\\n196: \\tpostStartHooks         map[string]postStartHookEntry\\n197: \\tpostStartHooksCalled   bool\\n198: \\tdisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\\n199: \\n200: \\tpreShutdownHookLock    sync.Mutex\\n201: \\tpreShutdownHooks       map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\\n202: \\tpreShutdownHooksCalled bool\\n203: \\n204: \\t// healthz checks\\n205: \\thealthzRegistry healthCheckRegistry\\n206: \\treadyzRegistry  healthCheckRegistry\\n207: \\tlivezRegistry   healthCheckRegistry\\n208: \\n209: \\tlivezGracePeriod time.Duration\\n210: \\n211: \\t// auditing. The backend is started before the server starts listening.\\n212: \\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\\n213: \\n214: \\t// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary\\n215: \\t// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in\\n216: \\t// the create-on-update case\\n217: \\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\\n218: \\n219: \\t// EquivalentResourceRegistry provides information about resources equivalent to a given resource,\\n220: \\t// and the kind associated with a given resource. As resources are installed, they are registered here.\\n221: \\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\\n222: \\n223: \\t// delegationTarget is the next delegate in the chain. This is never nil.\\n224: \\tdelegationTarget DelegationTarget\\n225: \\n226: \\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\\n227: \\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\\n228: \\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\\n229: \\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\\n230: \\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\\n231: \\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\\n232: \\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\\n233: \\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\\n234: \\n235: \\t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\\n236: \\t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\\n237: \\t// but /readyz will return failure.\\n238: \\tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\\n239: \\n240: \\t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\\n241: \\t// 0 means no limit.\\n242: \\tmaxRequestBodyBytes int64\\n243: \\n244: \\t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\\n245: \\tAPIServerID string\\n246: \\n247: \\t// StorageReadinessHook implements post-start-hook functionality for checking readiness\\n248: \\t// of underlying storage for registered resources.\\n249: \\tStorageReadinessHook *StorageReadinessHook\\n250: \\n251: \\t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\\n252: \\tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\\n253: \\n254: \\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\\n255: \\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\\n256: \\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\\n257: \\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\\n258: \\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\\n259: \\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\\n260: \\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\\n261: \\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\\n262: \\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n263: \\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\\n264: \\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the through if you have them.\\n269: \\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\\n270: \\n271: \\t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals that happen during the life cycle of the apiserver.\\n272: \\tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\\n273: \\n274: \\t// destroyFns contains a list of functions that should be called on shutdown to clean up resources.\\n275: \\tdestroyFns []func()\\n276: \\n277: \\t// muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals holds signals that indicate all known HTTP paths have been registered.\\n278: \\t// it exists primarily to avoid returning a 404 response when a resource actually exists but we haven't installed the path to a handler.\\n279: \\t// it is exposed for easier composition of the individual servers.\\n280: \\t// the primary users of this field are the WithMuxCompleteProtection filter and the NotFoundHandler\\n281: \\tmuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals map[string]<-chan struct{}\\n282: \\n283: \\t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to value,\\n293: \\t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\\n294: \\t// watch request(s) to drain.\\n295: \\t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\\n296: \\t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\\n297: \\t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\\n298: \\t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\\n299: \\t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\\n300: \\t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\\n301: \\t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\\n302: \\t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\\n303: \\t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\\n304: \\t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\\n305: \\t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\\n306: \\t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\\n307: \\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\\n308: }\\n309: \\n310: // DelegationTarget is an interface which allows for composition of API servers with top level handling that works\\n311: // as expected.\\n312: type DelegationTarget interface {\\n313: \\t// UnprotectedHandler returns a handler that is NOT protected by a normal chain\\n314: \\tUnprotectedHandler() http.Handler\\n315: \\n316: \\t// PostStartHooks returns the post-start hooks that need to be combined\\n317: \\tPostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry\\n318: \\n319: \\t// PreShutdownHooks returns the pre-stop hooks that need to be combined\\n320: \\tPreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\\n321: \\n322: \\t// HealthzChecks returns the healthz checks that need to be combined\\n323: \\tHealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker\\n324: \\n325: \\t// ListedPaths returns the paths for supporting an index\\n326: \\tListedPaths() []string\\n327: \\n328: \\t// NextDelegate returns the next delegationTarget in the chain of delegations\\n329: \\tNextDelegate() DelegationTarget\\n330: \\n331: \\t// PrepareRun does post API installation setup steps. It calls recursively the same function of the delegates.\\n332: \\tPrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer\\n333: \\n334: \\t// MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals exposes registered signals that indicate if all known HTTP paths have been installed.\\n335: \\tMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{}\\n336: \\n337: \\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\\n338: \\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\\n339: \\t// for being called more than once.\\n340: \\tDestroy()\\n341: }\\n342: \\n343: func (s *GenericAPIServer) UnprotectedHandler() http.Handler {\\n344: \\t// when we delegate, we need the server we're delegating to choose whether or not to use gorestful\\n345: \\treturn s.Handler.Director\\n346: }\\n347: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry {\\n348: \\treturn s.postStartHooks\\n349: }\\n350: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry {\\n351: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL150-350\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "150: \t// not protected by authentication/authorization.\n151: \tUnprotectedDebugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\n152: \n153: \t// listedPathProvider is a lister which provides the set of paths to show at /\n154: \tlistedPathProvider routes.ListedPathProvider\n155: \n156: \t// DiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an unaggregated form.\n157: \tDiscoveryGroupManager discovery.GroupManager\n158: \n159: \t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\n160: \tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n161: \n162: \t// PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager serves /apis aggregated from all peer apiservers.\n163: \tPeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager discoveryendpoint.PeerAggregatedResourceManager\n164: \n165: \t// AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager serves /api in an aggregated form.\n166: \tAggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n167: \n168: \t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI if these configs are non-nil.\n169: \topenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\n170: \n171: \t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI V3 if these configs are non-nil.\n172: \topenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\n173: \n174: \t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation indicates not to install the OpenAPI handler\n175: \t// during PrepareRun.\n176: \t// Set this to true when the specific API Server has its own OpenAPI handler\n177: \t// (e.g. kube-aggregator)\n178: \tskipOpenAPIInstallation bool\n179: \n180: \t// OpenAPIVersionedService controls the /openapi/v2 endpoint, and can be used to update the served spec.\n181: \t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n182: \tOpenAPIVersionedService *handler.OpenAPIService\n183: \n184: \t// OpenAPIV3VersionedService controls the /openapi/v3 endpoint and can be used to update the served spec.\n185: \t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n186: \tOpenAPIV3VersionedService *handler3.OpenAPIService\n187: \n188: \t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the restful container endpoints.\n189: \t// It is set during PrepareRun.\n190: \tStaticOpenAPISpec *spec.Swagger\n191: \n192: \t// PostStartHooks are each called after the server has started listening, in a separate go func for each\n193: \t// with no guarantee of ordering between them.  The map key is a name used for error reporting.\n194: \t// It may kill the process with a panic if it wishes to by returning an error.\n195: \tpostStartHookLock      sync.Mutex\n196: \tpostStartHooks         map[string]postStartHookEntry\n197: \tpostStartHooksCalled   bool\n198: \tdisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n199: \n200: \tpreShutdownHookLock    sync.Mutex\n201: \tpreShutdownHooks       map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\n202: \tpreShutdownHooksCalled bool\n203: \n204: \t// healthz checks\n205: \thealthzRegistry healthCheckRegistry\n206: \treadyzRegistry  healthCheckRegistry\n207: \tlivezRegistry   healthCheckRegistry\n208: \n209: \tlivezGracePeriod time.Duration\n210: \n211: \t// auditing. The backend is started before the server starts listening.\n212: \tAuditBackend audit.Backend\n213: \n214: \t// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary\n215: \t// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in\n216: \t// the create-on-update case\n217: \tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\n218: \n219: \t// EquivalentResourceRegistry provides information about resources equivalent to a given resource,\n220: \t// and the kind associated with a given resource. As resources are installed, they are registered here.\n221: \tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\n222: \n223: \t// delegationTarget is the next delegate in the chain. This is never nil.\n224: \tdelegationTarget DelegationTarget\n225: \n226: \t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\n227: \t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\n228: \t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\n229: \tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\n230: \t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\n231: \t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\n232: \t// complete while the server is shuting down.\n233: \tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\n234: \n235: \t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\n236: \t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\n237: \t// but /readyz will return failure.\n238: \tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\n239: \n240: \t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\n241: \t// 0 means no limit.\n242: \tmaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n243: \n244: \t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\n245: \tAPIServerID string\n246: \n247: \t// StorageReadinessHook implements post-start-hook functionality for checking readiness\n248: \t// of underlying storage for registered resources.\n249: \tStorageReadinessHook *StorageReadinessHook\n250: \n251: \t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\n252: \tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\n253: \n254: \t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\n255: \t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\n256: \tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\n257: \t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\n258: \t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\n259: \t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\n260: \t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\n261: \t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\n262: \tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n263: \t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\n264: \t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\n265: \t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\n266: \tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n267: \n268: \t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\n269: \tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\n270: \n271: \t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals that happen during the life cycle of the apiserver.\n272: \tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\n273: \n274: \t// destroyFns contains a list of functions that should be called on shutdown to clean up resources.\n275: \tdestroyFns []func()\n276: \n277: \t// muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals holds signals that indicate all known HTTP paths have been registered.\n278: \t// it exists primarily to avoid returning a 404 response when a resource actually exists but we haven't installed the path to a handler.\n279: \t// it is exposed for easier composition of the individual servers.\n280: \t// the primary users of this field are the WithMuxCompleteProtection filter and the NotFoundHandler\n281: \tmuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals map[string]<-chan struct{}\n282: \n283: \t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\n284: \t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\n285: \t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\n286: \t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\n287: \t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\n288: \t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\n289: \t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\n290: \tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\n291: \n292: \t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\n293: \t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\n294: \t// watch request(s) to drain.\n295: \t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\n296: \t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\n297: \t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\n298: \t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\n299: \t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\n300: \t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\n301: \t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\n302: \t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\n303: \t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\n304: \t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\n305: \t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\n306: \t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\n307: \tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\n308: }\n309: \n310: // DelegationTarget is an interface which allows for composition of API servers with top level handling that works\n311: // as expected.\n312: type DelegationTarget interface {\n313: \t// UnprotectedHandler returns a handler that is NOT protected by a normal chain\n314: \tUnprotectedHandler() http.Handler\n315: \n316: \t// PostStartHooks returns the post-start hooks that need to be combined\n317: \tPostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry\n318: \n319: \t// PreShutdownHooks returns the pre-stop hooks that need to be combined\n320: \tPreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\n321: \n322: \t// HealthzChecks returns the healthz checks that need to be combined\n323: \tHealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker\n324: \n325: \t// ListedPaths returns the paths for supporting an index\n326: \tListedPaths() []string\n327: \n328: \t// NextDelegate returns the next delegationTarget in the chain of delegations\n329: \tNextDelegate() DelegationTarget\n330: \n331: \t// PrepareRun does post API installation setup steps. It calls recursively the same function of the delegates.\n332: \tPrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer\n333: \n334: \t// MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals exposes registered signals that indicate if all known HTTP paths have been installed.\n335: \tMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{}\n336: \n337: \t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\n338: \t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\n339: \t// for being called more than once.\n340: \tDestroy()\n341: }\n342: \n343: func (s *GenericAPIServer) UnprotectedHandler() http.Handler {\n344: \t// when we delegate, we need the server we're delegating to choose whether or not to use gorestful\n345: \treturn s.Handler.Director\n346: }\n347: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry {\n348: \treturn s.postStartHooks\n349: }\n350: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry {\n351: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL150-350"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"600: \\tif hook == nil {\\n601: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"hook func may not be nil: %q\\\", name)\\n602: \\t}\\n603: \\tif c.DisabledPostStartHooks.Has(name) {\\n604: \\t\\tklog.V(1).Infof(\\\"skipping %q because it was explicitly disabled\\\", name)\\n605: \\t\\treturn nil\\n606: \\t}\\n607: \\n608: \\tif postStartHook, exists := c.PostStartHooks[name]; exists {\\n609: \\t\\t// this is programmer error, but it can be hard to debug\\n610: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"unable to add %q because it was already registered by: %s\\\", name, postStartHook.originatingStack)\\n611: \\t}\\n612: \\tc.PostStartHooks[name] = PostStartHookConfigEntry{hook: hook, originatingStack: string(debug.Stack())}\\n613: \\n614: \\treturn nil\\n615: }\\n616: \\n617: // AddPostStartHookOrDie allows you to add a PostStartHook, but dies on failure.\\n618: func (c *Config) AddPostStartHookOrDie(name string, hook PostStartHookFunc) {\\n619: \\tif err := c.AddPostStartHook(name, hook); err != nil {\\n620: \\t\\tklog.Fatalf(\\\"Error registering PostStartHook %q: %v\\\", name, err)\\n621: \\t}\\n622: }\\n623: \\n624: func completeOpenAPI(config *openapicommon.Config, version *version.Version) {\\n625: \\tif config == nil {\\n626: \\t\\treturn\\n627: \\t}\\n628: \\tif config.SecurityDefinitions != nil {\\n629: \\t\\t// Setup OpenAPI security: all APIs will have the same authentication for now.\\n630: \\t\\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = []map[string][]string{}\\n631: \\t\\tkeys := []string{}\\n632: \\t\\tfor k := range *config.SecurityDefinitions {\\n633: \\t\\t\\tkeys = append(keys, k)\\n634: \\t\\t}\\n635: \\t\\tsort.Strings(keys)\\n636: \\t\\tfor _, k := range keys {\\n637: \\t\\t\\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = append(config.DefaultSecurity, map[string][]string{k: {}})\\n638: \\t\\t}\\n639: \\t\\tif config.CommonResponses == nil {\\n640: \\t\\t\\tconfig.CommonResponses = map[int]spec.Response{}\\n641: \\t\\t}\\n642: \\t\\tif _, exists := config.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized]; !exists {\\n643: \\t\\t\\tconfig.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized] = spec.Response{\\n644: \\t\\t\\t\\tResponseProps: spec.ResponseProps{\\n645: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDescription: \\\"Unauthorized\\\",\\n646: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n647: \\t\\t\\t}\\n648: \\t\\t}\\n649: \\t}\\n650: \\t// make sure we populate info, and info.version, if not manually set\\n651: \\tif config.Info == nil {\\n652: \\t\\tconfig.Info = &spec.Info{}\\n653: \\t}\\n654: \\tif config.Info.Version == \\\"\\\" {\\n655: \\t\\tif version != nil {\\n656: \\t\\t\\tconfig.Info.Version = strings.Split(version.String(), \\\"-\\\")[0]\\n657: \\t\\t} else {\\n658: \\t\\t\\tconfig.Info.Version = \\\"unversioned\\\"\\n659: \\t\\t}\\n660: \\t}\\n661: }\\n662: \\n663: func completeOpenAPIV3(config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config, version *version.Version) {\\n664: \\tif config == nil {\\n665: \\t\\treturn\\n666: \\t}\\n667: \\tif config.SecuritySchemes != nil {\\n668: \\t\\t// Setup OpenAPI security: all APIs will have the same authentication for now.\\n669: \\t\\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = []map[string][]string{}\\n670: \\t\\tkeys := []string{}\\n671: \\t\\tfor k := range config.SecuritySchemes {\\n672: \\t\\t\\tkeys = append(keys, k)\\n673: \\t\\t}\\n674: \\t\\tsort.Strings(keys)\\n675: \\t\\tfor _, k := range keys {\\n676: \\t\\t\\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = append(config.DefaultSecurity, map[string][]string{k: {}})\\n677: \\t\\t}\\n678: \\t\\tif config.CommonResponses == nil {\\n679: \\t\\t\\tconfig.CommonResponses = map[int]*spec3.Response{}\\n680: \\t\\t}\\n681: \\t\\tif _, exists := config.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized]; !exists {\\n682: \\t\\t\\tconfig.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized] = &spec3.Response{\\n683: \\t\\t\\t\\tResponseProps: spec3.ResponseProps{\\n684: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDescription: \\\"Unauthorized\\\",\\n685: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n686: \\t\\t\\t}\\n687: \\t\\t}\\n688: \\t}\\n689: \\t// make sure we populate info, and info.version, if not manually set\\n690: \\tif config.Info == nil {\\n691: \\t\\tconfig.Info = &spec.Info{}\\n692: \\t}\\n693: \\tif config.Info.Version == \\\"\\\" {\\n694: \\t\\tif version != nil {\\n695: \\t\\t\\tconfig.Info.Version = strings.Split(version.String(), \\\"-\\\")[0]\\n696: \\t\\t} else {\\n697: \\t\\t\\tconfig.Info.Version = \\\"unversioned\\\"\\n698: \\t\\t}\\n699: \\t}\\n700: }\\n701: \\n702: // DrainedNotify returns a lifecycle signal of genericapiserver already drained while shutting down.\\n703: func (c *Config) DrainedNotify() <-chan struct{} {\\n704: \\treturn c.lifecycleSignals.InFlightRequestsDrained.Signaled()\\n705: }\\n706: \\n707: // ShutdownInitiated returns a lifecycle signal of apiserver shutdown having been initiated.\\n708: func (c *Config) ShutdownInitiatedNotify() <-chan struct{} {\\n709: \\treturn c.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated.Signaled()\\n710: }\\n711: \\n712: // Complete fills in any fields not set that are required to have valid data and can be derived\\n713: // from other fields. If you're going to `ApplyOptions`, do that first. It's mutating the receiver.\\n714: func (c *Config) Complete(informers informers.SharedInformerFactory) CompletedConfig {\\n715: \\tif c.FeatureGate == nil {\\n716: \\t\\tc.FeatureGate = utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate\\n717: \\t}\\n718: \\tif len(c.ExternalAddress) == 0 && c.PublicAddress != nil {\\n719: \\t\\tc.ExternalAddress = c.PublicAddress.String()\\n720: \\t}\\n721: \\n722: \\t// if there is no port, and we listen on one securely, use that one\\n723: \\tif _, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(c.ExternalAddress); err != nil {\\n724: \\t\\tif c.SecureServing == nil {\\n725: \\t\\t\\tklog.Fatalf(\\\"cannot derive external address port without listening on a secure port.\\\")\\n726: \\t\\t}\\n727: \\t\\t_, port, err := c.SecureServing.HostPort()\\n728: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n729: \\t\\t\\tklog.Fatalf(\\\"cannot derive external address from the secure port: %v\\\", err)\\n730: \\t\\t}\\n731: \\t\\tc.ExternalAddress = net.JoinHostPort(c.ExternalAddress, strconv.Itoa(port))\\n732: \\t}\\n733: \\tcompleteOpenAPI(c.OpenAPIConfig, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\\n734: \\tcompleteOpenAPIV3(c.OpenAPIV3Config, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\\n735: \\n736: \\tif c.DiscoveryAddresses == nil {\\n737: \\t\\tc.DiscoveryAddresses = discovery.DefaultAddresses{DefaultAddress: c.ExternalAddress}\\n738: \\t}\\n739: \\n740: \\tAuthorizeClientBearerToken(c.LoopbackClientConfig, &c.Authentication, &c.Authorization)\\n741: \\n742: \\tif c.RequestInfoResolver == nil {\\n743: \\t\\tc.RequestInfoResolver = NewRequestInfoResolver(c)\\n744: \\t}\\n745: \\n746: \\tif c.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil {\\n747: \\t\\tif c.RESTOptionsGetter == nil {\\n748: \\t\\t\\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistry()\\n749: \\t\\t} else {\\n750: \\t\\t\\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistryWithIdentity(func(groupResource schema.GroupResource) string {\\n751: \\t\\t\\t\\t// use the storage prefix as the key if possible\\n752: \\t\\t\\t\\tif opts, err := c.RESTOptionsGetter.GetRESTOptions(groupResource, nil); err == nil {\\n753: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn opts.ResourcePrefix\\n754: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n755: \\t\\t\\t\\t// otherwise return \\\"\\\" to use the default key (parent GV name)\\n756: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\"\\n757: \\t\\t\\t})\\n758: \\t\\t}\\n759: \\t}\\n760: \\n761: \\treturn CompletedConfig{&completedConfig{c, informers}}\\n762: }\\n763: \\n764: // Complete fills in any fields not set that are required to have valid data and can be derived\\n765: // from other fields. If you're going to `ApplyOptions`, do that first. It's mutating the receiver.\\n766: func (c *RecommendedConfig) Complete() CompletedConfig {\\n767: \\treturn c.Config.Complete(c.SharedInformerFactory)\\n768: }\\n769: \\n770: var defaultAllowedMediaTypes = []string{\\n771: \\truntime.ContentTypeJSON,\\n772: \\truntime.ContentTypeYAML,\\n773: \\truntime.ContentTypeProtobuf,\\n774: }\\n775: \\n776: // New creates a new server which logically combines the handling chain with the passed server.\\n777: // name is used to differentiate for logging. The handler chain in particular can be difficult as it starts delegating.\\n778: // delegationTarget may not be nil.\\n779: func (c completedConfig) New(name string, delegationTarget DelegationTarget) (*GenericAPIServer, error) {\\n780: \\tif c.Serializer == nil {\\n781: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.Serializer == nil\\\")\\n782: \\t}\\n783: \\tallowedMediaTypes := defaultAllowedMediaTypes\\n784: \\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.CBORServingAndStorage) {\\n785: \\t\\tallowedMediaTypes = append(allowedMediaTypes, runtime.ContentTypeCBOR)\\n786: \\t}\\n787: \\tfor _, info := range c.Serializer.SupportedMediaTypes() {\\n788: \\t\\tvar ok bool\\n789: \\t\\tfor _, mt := range allowedMediaTypes {\\n790: \\t\\t\\tif info.MediaType == mt {\\n791: \\t\\t\\t\\tok = true\\n792: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n793: \\t\\t\\t}\\n794: \\t\\t}\\n795: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n796: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"refusing to create new apiserver %q with support for media type %q (allowed media types are: %s)\\\", name, info.MediaType, strings.Join(allowedMediaTypes, \\\", \\\"))\\n797: \\t\\t}\\n798: \\t}\\n799: \\tif c.LoopbackClientConfig == nil {\\n800: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.LoopbackClientConfig == nil\\\")\\n801: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL600-800\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "600: \tif hook == nil {\n601: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"hook func may not be nil: %q\", name)\n602: \t}\n603: \tif c.DisabledPostStartHooks.Has(name) {\n604: \t\tklog.V(1).Infof(\"skipping %q because it was explicitly disabled\", name)\n605: \t\treturn nil\n606: \t}\n607: \n608: \tif postStartHook, exists := c.PostStartHooks[name]; exists {\n609: \t\t// this is programmer error, but it can be hard to debug\n610: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to add %q because it was already registered by: %s\", name, postStartHook.originatingStack)\n611: \t}\n612: \tc.PostStartHooks[name] = PostStartHookConfigEntry{hook: hook, originatingStack: string(debug.Stack())}\n613: \n614: \treturn nil\n615: }\n616: \n617: // AddPostStartHookOrDie allows you to add a PostStartHook, but dies on failure.\n618: func (c *Config) AddPostStartHookOrDie(name string, hook PostStartHookFunc) {\n619: \tif err := c.AddPostStartHook(name, hook); err != nil {\n620: \t\tklog.Fatalf(\"Error registering PostStartHook %q: %v\", name, err)\n621: \t}\n622: }\n623: \n624: func completeOpenAPI(config *openapicommon.Config, version *version.Version) {\n625: \tif config == nil {\n626: \t\treturn\n627: \t}\n628: \tif config.SecurityDefinitions != nil {\n629: \t\t// Setup OpenAPI security: all APIs will have the same authentication for now.\n630: \t\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = []map[string][]string{}\n631: \t\tkeys := []string{}\n632: \t\tfor k := range *config.SecurityDefinitions {\n633: \t\t\tkeys = append(keys, k)\n634: \t\t}\n635: \t\tsort.Strings(keys)\n636: \t\tfor _, k := range keys {\n637: \t\t\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = append(config.DefaultSecurity, map[string][]string{k: {}})\n638: \t\t}\n639: \t\tif config.CommonResponses == nil {\n640: \t\t\tconfig.CommonResponses = map[int]spec.Response{}\n641: \t\t}\n642: \t\tif _, exists := config.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized]; !exists {\n643: \t\t\tconfig.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized] = spec.Response{\n644: \t\t\t\tResponseProps: spec.ResponseProps{\n645: \t\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Unauthorized\",\n646: \t\t\t\t},\n647: \t\t\t}\n648: \t\t}\n649: \t}\n650: \t// make sure we populate info, and info.version, if not manually set\n651: \tif config.Info == nil {\n652: \t\tconfig.Info = &spec.Info{}\n653: \t}\n654: \tif config.Info.Version == \"\" {\n655: \t\tif version != nil {\n656: \t\t\tconfig.Info.Version = strings.Split(version.String(), \"-\")[0]\n657: \t\t} else {\n658: \t\t\tconfig.Info.Version = \"unversioned\"\n659: \t\t}\n660: \t}\n661: }\n662: \n663: func completeOpenAPIV3(config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config, version *version.Version) {\n664: \tif config == nil {\n665: \t\treturn\n666: \t}\n667: \tif config.SecuritySchemes != nil {\n668: \t\t// Setup OpenAPI security: all APIs will have the same authentication for now.\n669: \t\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = []map[string][]string{}\n670: \t\tkeys := []string{}\n671: \t\tfor k := range config.SecuritySchemes {\n672: \t\t\tkeys = append(keys, k)\n673: \t\t}\n674: \t\tsort.Strings(keys)\n675: \t\tfor _, k := range keys {\n676: \t\t\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = append(config.DefaultSecurity, map[string][]string{k: {}})\n677: \t\t}\n678: \t\tif config.CommonResponses == nil {\n679: \t\t\tconfig.CommonResponses = map[int]*spec3.Response{}\n680: \t\t}\n681: \t\tif _, exists := config.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized]; !exists {\n682: \t\t\tconfig.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized] = &spec3.Response{\n683: \t\t\t\tResponseProps: spec3.ResponseProps{\n684: \t\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Unauthorized\",\n685: \t\t\t\t},\n686: \t\t\t}\n687: \t\t}\n688: \t}\n689: \t// make sure we populate info, and info.version, if not manually set\n690: \tif config.Info == nil {\n691: \t\tconfig.Info = &spec.Info{}\n692: \t}\n693: \tif config.Info.Version == \"\" {\n694: \t\tif version != nil {\n695: \t\t\tconfig.Info.Version = strings.Split(version.String(), \"-\")[0]\n696: \t\t} else {\n697: \t\t\tconfig.Info.Version = \"unversioned\"\n698: \t\t}\n699: \t}\n700: }\n701: \n702: // DrainedNotify returns a lifecycle signal of genericapiserver already drained while shutting down.\n703: func (c *Config) DrainedNotify() <-chan struct{} {\n704: \treturn c.lifecycleSignals.InFlightRequestsDrained.Signaled()\n705: }\n706: \n707: // ShutdownInitiated returns a lifecycle signal of apiserver shutdown having been initiated.\n708: func (c *Config) ShutdownInitiatedNotify() <-chan struct{} {\n709: \treturn c.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated.Signaled()\n710: }\n711: \n712: // Complete fills in any fields not set that are required to have valid data and can be derived\n713: // from other fields. If you're going to `ApplyOptions`, do that first. It's mutating the receiver.\n714: func (c *Config) Complete(informers informers.SharedInformerFactory) CompletedConfig {\n715: \tif c.FeatureGate == nil {\n716: \t\tc.FeatureGate = utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate\n717: \t}\n718: \tif len(c.ExternalAddress) == 0 && c.PublicAddress != nil {\n719: \t\tc.ExternalAddress = c.PublicAddress.String()\n720: \t}\n721: \n722: \t// if there is no port, and we listen on one securely, use that one\n723: \tif _, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(c.ExternalAddress); err != nil {\n724: \t\tif c.SecureServing == nil {\n725: \t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"cannot derive external address port without listening on a secure port.\")\n726: \t\t}\n727: \t\t_, port, err := c.SecureServing.HostPort()\n728: \t\tif err != nil {\n729: \t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"cannot derive external address from the secure port: %v\", err)\n730: \t\t}\n731: \t\tc.ExternalAddress = net.JoinHostPort(c.ExternalAddress, strconv.Itoa(port))\n732: \t}\n733: \tcompleteOpenAPI(c.OpenAPIConfig, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\n734: \tcompleteOpenAPIV3(c.OpenAPIV3Config, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\n735: \n736: \tif c.DiscoveryAddresses == nil {\n737: \t\tc.DiscoveryAddresses = discovery.DefaultAddresses{DefaultAddress: c.ExternalAddress}\n738: \t}\n739: \n740: \tAuthorizeClientBearerToken(c.LoopbackClientConfig, &c.Authentication, &c.Authorization)\n741: \n742: \tif c.RequestInfoResolver == nil {\n743: \t\tc.RequestInfoResolver = NewRequestInfoResolver(c)\n744: \t}\n745: \n746: \tif c.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil {\n747: \t\tif c.RESTOptionsGetter == nil {\n748: \t\t\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistry()\n749: \t\t} else {\n750: \t\t\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistryWithIdentity(func(groupResource schema.GroupResource) string {\n751: \t\t\t\t// use the storage prefix as the key if possible\n752: \t\t\t\tif opts, err := c.RESTOptionsGetter.GetRESTOptions(groupResource, nil); err == nil {\n753: \t\t\t\t\treturn opts.ResourcePrefix\n754: \t\t\t\t}\n755: \t\t\t\t// otherwise return \"\" to use the default key (parent GV name)\n756: \t\t\t\treturn \"\"\n757: \t\t\t})\n758: \t\t}\n759: \t}\n760: \n761: \treturn CompletedConfig{&completedConfig{c, informers}}\n762: }\n763: \n764: // Complete fills in any fields not set that are required to have valid data and can be derived\n765: // from other fields. If you're going to `ApplyOptions`, do that first. It's mutating the receiver.\n766: func (c *RecommendedConfig) Complete() CompletedConfig {\n767: \treturn c.Config.Complete(c.SharedInformerFactory)\n768: }\n769: \n770: var defaultAllowedMediaTypes = []string{\n771: \truntime.ContentTypeJSON,\n772: \truntime.ContentTypeYAML,\n773: \truntime.ContentTypeProtobuf,\n774: }\n775: \n776: // New creates a new server which logically combines the handling chain with the passed server.\n777: // name is used to differentiate for logging. The handler chain in particular can be difficult as it starts delegating.\n778: // delegationTarget may not be nil.\n779: func (c completedConfig) New(name string, delegationTarget DelegationTarget) (*GenericAPIServer, error) {\n780: \tif c.Serializer == nil {\n781: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.Serializer == nil\")\n782: \t}\n783: \tallowedMediaTypes := defaultAllowedMediaTypes\n784: \tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.CBORServingAndStorage) {\n785: \t\tallowedMediaTypes = append(allowedMediaTypes, runtime.ContentTypeCBOR)\n786: \t}\n787: \tfor _, info := range c.Serializer.SupportedMediaTypes() {\n788: \t\tvar ok bool\n789: \t\tfor _, mt := range allowedMediaTypes {\n790: \t\t\tif info.MediaType == mt {\n791: \t\t\t\tok = true\n792: \t\t\t\tbreak\n793: \t\t\t}\n794: \t\t}\n795: \t\tif !ok {\n796: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"refusing to create new apiserver %q with support for media type %q (allowed media types are: %s)\", name, info.MediaType, strings.Join(allowedMediaTypes, \", \"))\n797: \t\t}\n798: \t}\n799: \tif c.LoopbackClientConfig == nil {\n800: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.LoopbackClientConfig == nil\")\n801: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL600-800"
  }
}

emptyDelegate) Destroy() {\\n436: }\\n437: \\n438: // preparedGenericAPIServer is a private wrapper that enforces a call of PrepareRun() before Run can be invoked.\\n439: type preparedGenericAPIServer struct {\\n440: \\t*GenericAPIServer\\n441: }\\n442: \\n443: // PrepareRun does post API installation setup steps. It calls recursively the same function of the delegates.\\n444: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer {\\n445: \\ts.delegationTarget.PrepareRun()\\n446: \\n447: \\tif s.openAPIConfig != nil && !s.skipOpenAPIInstallation {\\n448: \\t\\ts.OpenAPIVersionedService, s.StaticOpenAPISpec = routes.OpenAPI{\\n449: \\t\\t\\tConfig: s.openAPIConfig,\\n450: \\t\\t}.InstallV2(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\\n451: \\t}\\n452: \\n453: \\tif s.openAPIV3Config != nil && !s.skipOpenAPIInstallation {\\n454: \\t\\ts.OpenAPIV3VersionedService = routes.OpenAPI{\\n455: \\t\\t\\tV3Config: s.openAPIV3Config,\\n456: \\t\\t}.InstallV3(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\\n457: \\t}\\n458: \\n459: \\ts.installHealthz()\\n460: \\ts.installLivez()\\n461: \\n462: \\t// as soon as shutdown is initiated, readiness should start failing\\n463: \\treadinessStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated.Signaled()\\n464: \\terr := s.addReadyzShutdownCheck(readinessStopCh)\\n465: \\tif err != nil {\\n466: \\t\\tklog.Errorf(\\\"Failed to install readyz shutdown check %s\\\", err)\\n467: \\t}\\n468: \\ts.installReadyz()\\n469: \\n470: \\tcomponentName := \\\"apiserver\\\"\\n471: \\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentStatusz) {\\n472: \\t\\tstatusz.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, componentName, statusz.NewRegistry(s.EffectiveVersion, statusz.WithListedPaths(s.ListedPaths())))\\n473: \\t}\\n474: \\n475: \\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentFlagz) {\\n476: \\t\\tif s.Flagz != nil {\\n477: \\t\\t\\tflagz.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, componentName, s.Flagz)\\n478: \\t\\t}\\n479: \\t}\\n480: \\n481: \\treturn preparedGenericAPIServer{s}\\n482: }\\n483: \\n484: // Run spawns the secure http server. It only returns if stopCh is closed\\n485: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\\n486: //\\n487: // Deprecated: use RunWithContext instead. Run will not get removed to avoid\\n488: // breaking consumers, but should not be used in new code.\\n489: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error {\\n490: \\tctx := wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh)\\n491: \\treturn s.RunWithContext(ctx)\\n492: }\\n493: \\n494: // RunWithContext spawns the secure http server. It only returns if ctx is canceled\\n495: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\\n496: // This is the diagram of what contexts/channels/signals are dependent on each other:\\n497: //\\n498: // |                                   ctx\\n499: // |                                    |\\n500: // |           ---------------------------------------------------------\\n501: // | ctx.Done()\\n538: \\tdelayedStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.AfterShutdownDelayDuration\\n539: \\tshutdownInitiatedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated\\n540: \\n541: \\t// Clean up resources on map[string]<-chan struct{}{}\n434: }\n435: func (s emptyDelegate) Destroy() {\n436: }\n437: \n438: // preparedGenericAPIServer is a private wrapper that enforces a call of PrepareRun() before Run can be invoked.\n439: type preparedGenericAPIServer struct {\n440: \t*GenericAPIServer\n441: }\n442: \n443: // PrepareRun does post API installation setup steps. It calls recursively the same function of the delegates.\n444: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer {\n445: \ts.delegationTarget.PrepareRun()\n446: \n447: \tif s.openAPIConfig != nil && !s.skipOpenAPIInstallation {\n448: \t\ts.OpenAPIVersionedService, s.StaticOpenAPISpec = routes.OpenAPI{\n449: \t\t\tConfig: s.openAPIConfig,\n450: \t\t}.InstallV2(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n451: \t}\n452: \n453: \tif s.openAPIV3Config != nil && !s.skipOpenAPIInstallation {\n454: \t\ts.OpenAPIV3VersionedService = routes.OpenAPI{\n455: \t\t\tV3Config: s.openAPIV3Config,\n456: \t\t}.InstallV3(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n457: \t}\n458: \n459: \ts.installHealthz()\n460: \ts.installLivez()\n461: \n462: \t// as soon as shutdown is initiated, readiness should start failing\n463: \treadinessStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated.Signaled()\n464: \terr := s.addReadyzShutdownCheck(readinessStopCh)\n465: \tif err != nil {\n466: \t\tklog.Errorf(\"Failed to install readyz shutdown check %s\", err)\n467: \t}\n468: \ts.installReadyz()\n469: \n470: \tcomponentName := \"apiserver\"\n471: \tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentStatusz) {\n472: \t\tstatusz.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, componentName, statusz.NewRegistry(s.EffectiveVersion, statusz.WithListedPaths(s.ListedPaths())))\n473: \t}\n474: \n475: \tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentFlagz) {\n476: \t\tif s.Flagz != nil {\n477: \t\t\tflagz.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, componentName, s.Flagz)\n478: \t\t}\n479: \t}\n480: \n481: \treturn preparedGenericAPIServer{s}\n482: }\n483: \n484: // Run spawns the secure http server. It only returns if stopCh is closed\n485: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\n486: //\n487: // Deprecated: use RunWithContext instead. Run will not get removed to avoid\n488: // breaking consumers, but should not be used in new code.\n489: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error {\n490: \tctx := wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh)\n491: \treturn s.RunWithContext(ctx)\n492: }\n493: \n494: // RunWithContext spawns the secure http server. It only returns if ctx is canceled\n495: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\n496: // This is the diagram of what contexts/channels/signals are dependent on each other:\n497: //\n498: // |                                   ctx\n499: // |                                    |\n500: // |           ---------------------------------------------------------\n501: // |           |                                                       |\n502: // |    ShutdownInitiated (shutdownInitiatedCh)                        |\n503: // |           |                                                       |\n504: // | (ShutdownDelayDuration)                                    (PreShutdownHooks)\n505: // |           |                                                       |\n506: // |  AfterShutdownDelayDuration (delayedStopCh)   PreShutdownHooksStopped (preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh)\n507: // |           |                                                       |\n508: // |           |-------------------------------------------------------|\n509: // |                                    |\n510: // |                                    |\n511: // |               NotAcceptingNewRequest (notAcceptingNewRequestCh)\n512: // |                                    |\n513: // |                                    |\n514: // |           |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\n515: // |           |                        |              |                                          |\n516: // |        [without                 [with             |                                          |\n517: // | ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  |                                          |\n518: // |           |                        |              |                                          |\n519: // |           |                        ---------------|                                          |\n520: // |           |                                       |                                          |\n521: // |           |                      |----------------|-----------------------|                  |\n522: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |\n523: // |           |         (NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   (WatchRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   |\n524: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |\n525: // |           |                      |------------------|---------------------|                  |\n526: // |           |                                         |                                        |\n527: // |           |                         InFlightRequestsDrained (drainedCh)                      |\n528: // |           |                                         |                                        |\n529: // |           |-------------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------|\n530: // |                               |                     |\n531: // |                       stopHttpServerCtx     (AuditBackend::Shutdown())\n532: // |                               |\n533: // |                       listenerStoppedCh\n534: // |                               |\n535: // |      HTTPServerStoppedListening (httpServerStoppedListeningCh)\n536: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) RunWithContext(ctx context.Context) error {\n537: \tstopCh := ctx.Done()\n538: \tdelayedStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.AfterShutdownDelayDuration\n539: \tshutdownInitiatedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated\n540: \n541: \t// Clean up resources on shutdown.\n542: \tdefer s.Destroy()\n543: \n544: \t// If UDS profiling is enabled, start a local http server listening on that socket\n545: \tif s.UnprotectedDebugSocket != nil {\n546: \t\tgo func() {\n547: \t\t\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrashWithContext(ctx)\n548: \t\t\tklog.Error(s.UnprotectedDebugSocket.RunWithContext(ctx))\n549: \t\t}()\n550: \t}\n551: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL350-550"
  }
}

\"priority-and-fairness-filter\"\n952: \t\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(priorityAndFairnessFilterHookName) {\n953: \t\t\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(priorityAndFairnessFilterHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n954: \t\t\t\tgenericfilters.StartPriorityAndFairnessWatermarkMaintenance(hookContext.Done())\n955: \t\t\t\treturn nil\n956: \t\t\t})\n957: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n958: \t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n959: \t\t\t}\n960: \t\t}\n961: \t} else {\n962: \t\tconst maxInFlightFilterHookName = \"max-in-flight-filter\"\n963: \t\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(maxInFlightFilterHookName) {\n964: \t\t\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(maxInFlightFilterHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n965: \t\t\t\tgenericfilters.StartMaxInFlightWatermarkMaintenance(hookContext.Done())\n966: \t\t\t\treturn nil\n967: \t\t\t})\n968: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n969: \t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n970: \t\t\t}\n971: \t\t}\n972: \t}\n973: \n974: \t// Add PostStartHook for maintenaing the object count tracker.\n975: \tif c.StorageObjectCountTracker != nil {\n976: \t\tconst storageObjectCountTrackerHookName = \"storage-object-count-tracker-hook\"\n977: \t\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(storageObjectCountTrackerHookName) {\n978: \t\t\tif err := s.AddPostStartHook(storageObjectCountTrackerHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n979: \t\t\t\tgo c.StorageObjectCountTracker.RunUntil(hookContext.Done())\n980: \t\t\t\treturn nil\n981: \t\t\t}); err != nil {\n982: \t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n983: \t\t\t}\n984: \t\t}\n985: \t}\n986: \n987: \tfor _, delegateCheck := range delegationTarget.HealthzChecks() {\n988: \t\tskip := false\n989: \t\tfor _, existingCheck := range c.HealthzChecks {\n990: \t\t\tif existingCheck.Name() == delegateCheck.Name() {\n991: \t\t\t\tskip = true\n992: \t\t\t\tbreak\n993: \t\t\t}\n994: \t\t}\n995: \t\tif skip {\n996: \t\t\tcontinue\n997: \t\t}\n998: \t\ts.AddHealthChecks(delegateCheck)\n999: \t}\n1000: \ts.RegisterDestroyFunc(func() {\n1001: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"550: \\t}\\n551: \\n552: \\t// spawn a new goroutine for closing the MuxAndDiscoveryComplete signal\\n553: \\t// registration happens during construction of the generic api server\\n554: \\t// the last server in the chain aggregates signals from the previous instances\\n555: \\tgo func() {\\n556: \\t\\tfor _, muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal := range s.GenericAPIServer.MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() {\\n557: \\t\\t\\tselect {\\n558: \\t\\t\\tcase <-muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal:\\n559: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n560: \\t\\t\\tcase <-stopCh:\\n561: \\t\\t\\t\\tklog.V(1).Infof(\\\"haven't completed %s, stop requested\\\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\\n562: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n563: \\t\\t\\t}\\n564: \\t\\t}\\n565: \\t\\ts.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signal()\\n566: \\t\\tklog.V(1).Infof(\\\"%s has all endpoints registered and discovery information is complete\\\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\\n567: \\t}()\\n568: \\n569: \\tgo func() {\\n570: \\t\\tdefer delayedStopCh.Signal()\\n571: \\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", delayedStopCh.Name())\\n572: \\n573: \\t\\t<-stopCh\\n574: \\n575: \\t\\t// As soon as shutdown is initiated, /readyz should start returning failure.\\n576: \\t\\t// This gives the load balancer a window defined by ShutdownDelayDuration to detect that /readyz is red\\n577: \\t\\t// and stop sending traffic to this server.\\n578: \\t\\tshutdownInitiatedCh.Signal()\\n579: \\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", shutdownInitiatedCh.Name())\\n580: \\n581: \\t\\ttime.Sleep(s.ShutdownDelayDuration)\\n582: \\t}()\\n583: \\n584: \\t// close socket after delayed stopCh\\n585: \\tshutdownTimeout := s.ShutdownTimeout\\n586: \\tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\\n587: \\t\\t// when this mode is enabled, we do the following:\\n588: \\t\\t// - the server will continue to listen until all existing requests in flight\\n589: \\t\\t//   (not including active long running requests) have been drained.\\n590: \\t\\t// - once drained, http Server Shutdown is invoked with a timeout of 2s,\\n591: \\t\\t//   net/http waits for 1s for the peer to respond to a GO_AWAY frame, so\\n592: \\t\\t//   we should wait for a minimum of 2s\\n593: \\t\\tshutdownTimeout = 2 * time.Second\\n594: \\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] using HTTP Server shutdown timeout\\\", \\\"shutdownTimeout\\\", shutdownTimeout)\\n595: \\t}\\n596: \\n597: \\tnotAcceptingNewRequestCh := s.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest\\n598: \\tdrainedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.InFlightRequestsDrained\\n599: \\t// Canceling the parent context does not immediately cancel the HTTP server.\\n600: \\t// We only inherit context values here and deal with cancellation ourselves.\\n601: \\tstopHTTPServerCtx, stopHTTPServer := context.WithCancelCause(context.WithoutCancel(ctx))\\n602: \\tgo func() {\\n603: \\t\\tdefer stopHTTPServer(errors.New(\\\"time to stop HTTP server\\\"))\\n604: \\n605: \\t\\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh := notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\\n606: \\t\\tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\\n607: \\t\\t\\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh = drainedCh.Signaled()\\n608: \\t\\t}\\n609: \\n610: \\t\\t<-timeToStopHttpServerCh\\n611: \\t}()\\n612: \\n613: \\t// Start the audit backend before any request comes in. This means we must call Backend.Run\\n614: \\t// before http server start serving. Otherwise the Backend.ProcessEvents call might block.\\n615: \\t// AuditBackend.Run will stop as soon as all in-flight requests are drained.\\n616: \\tif s.AuditBackend != nil {\\n617: \\t\\tif err := s.AuditBackend.Run(drainedCh.Signaled()); err != nil {\\n618: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to run the audit backend: %v\\\", err)\\n619: \\t\\t}\\n620: \\t}\\n621: \\n622: \\tstoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err := s.NonBlockingRunWithContext(stopHTTPServerCtx, shutdownTimeout)\\n623: \\tif err != nil {\\n624: \\t\\treturn err\\n625: \\t}\\n626: \\n627: \\thttpServerStoppedListeningCh := s.lifecycleSignals.HTTPServerStoppedListening\\n628: \\tgo func() {\\n629: \\t\\t<-listenerStoppedCh\\n630: \\t\\thttpServerStoppedListeningCh.Signal()\\n631: \\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", httpServerStoppedListeningCh.Name())\\n632: \\t}()\\n633: \\n634: \\t// we don't accept new request as soon as both ShutdownDelayDuration has\\n635: \\t// elapsed and preshutdown hooks have completed.\\n636: \\tpreShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.PreShutdownHooksStopped\\n637: \\tgo func() {\\n638: \\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Name())\\n639: \\t\\tdefer notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signal()\\n640: \\n641: \\t\\t// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain\\n642: \\t\\t<-delayedStopCh.Signaled()\\n643: \\n644: \\t\\t// Additionally wait for preshutdown hooks to also be finished, as some of them need\\n645: \\t\\t// to send API calls to clean up after themselves (e.g. lease reconcilers removing\\n646: \\t\\t// itself from the active servers).\\n647: \\t\\t<-preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Signaled()\\n648: \\t}()\\n649: \\n650: \\t// wait for all in-flight non-long running requests to finish\\n651: \\tnonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})\\n652: \\tgo func() {\\n653: \\t\\tdefer close(nonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh)\\n654: \\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).Info(\\\"[graceful-termination] in-flight non long-running request(s) have drained\\\")\\n655: \\n656: \\t\\t// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain (it rejects everything after Wait has been called).\\n657: \\t\\t<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\\n658: \\n659: \\t\\t// Wait for all requests to finish, which are bounded by the RequestTimeout variable.\\n660: \\t\\t// once NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait is invoked, the apiserver is\\n661: \\t\\t// expected to reject any incoming request with a {503, Retry-After}\\n662: \\t\\t// response via the WithWaitGroup filter. On the contrary, we observe\\n663: \\t\\t// that incoming request(s) get a 'connection refused' error, this is\\n664: \\t\\t// because, at this point, we have called 'Server.Shutdown' and\\n665: \\t\\t// net/http server has stopped listening. This causes incoming\\n666: \\t\\t// request to get a 'connection refused' error.\\n667: \\t\\t// On the other hand, if 'ShutdownSendRetryAfter' is enabled incoming\\n668: \\t\\t// requests will be rejected with a {429, Retry-After} since\\n669: \\t\\t// 'Server.Shutdown' will be invoked only after in-flight requests\\n670: \\t\\t// have been drained.\\n671: \\t\\t// TODO: can we consolidate these two modes of graceful termination?\\n672: \\t\\ts.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait()\\n673: \\t}()\\n674: \\n675: \\t// wait for all in-flight watches to finish\\n676: \\tactiveWatchesDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})\\n677: \\tgo func() {\\n678: \\t\\tdefer close(activeWatchesDrainedCh)\\n679: \\n680: \\t\\t<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\\n681: \\t\\tif s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod <= time.Duration(0) {\\n682: \\t\\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] not going to wait for active watch request(s) to drain\\\")\\n683: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n684: \\t\\t}\\n685: \\n686: \\t\\t// Wait for all active watches to finish\\n687: \\t\\tgrace := s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod\\n688: \\t\\tactiveBefore, activeAfter, err := s.WatchRequestWaitGroup.Wait(func(count int) (utilwaitgroup.RateLimiter, context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {\\n689: \\t\\t\\tqps := float64(count) / grace.Seconds()\\n690: \\t\\t\\t// TODO: we don't want the QPS (max requests drained per second) to\\n691: \\t\\t\\t//  get below a certain floor value, since we want the server to\\n692: \\t\\t\\t//  drain the active watch requests as soon as possible.\\n693: \\t\\t\\t//  For now, it's hard coded to 200, and it is subject to change\\n694: \\t\\t\\t//  based on the result from the scale testing.\\n695: \\t\\t\\tif qps < 200 {\\n696: \\t\\t\\t\\tqps = 200\\n697: \\t\\t\\t}\\n698: \\n699: \\t\\t\\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), grace)\\n700: \\t\\t\\t// We don't expect more than one token to be consumed\\n701: \\t\\t\\t// in a single Wait call, so setting burst to 1.\\n702: \\t\\t\\treturn rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(qps), 1), ctx, cancel\\n703: \\t\\t})\\n704: \\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] active watch request(s) have drained\\\",\\n705: \\t\\t\\t\\\"duration\\\", grace, \\\"activeWatchesBefore\\\", activeBefore, \\\"activeWatchesAfter\\\", activeAfter, \\\"error\\\", err)\\n706: \\t}()\\n707: \\n708: \\tgo func() {\\n709: \\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", drainedCh.Name())\\n710: \\t\\tdefer drainedCh.Signal()\\n711: \\n712: \\t\\t<-nonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh\\n713: \\t\\t<-activeWatchesDrainedCh\\n714: \\t}()\\n715: \\n716: \\tklog.V(1).Info(\\\"[graceful-termination] waiting for shutdown to be initiated\\\")\\n717: \\t<-stopCh\\n718: \\n719: \\t// run shutdown hooks directly. This includes deregistering from\\n720: \\t// the kubernetes endpoint in case of kube-apiserver.\\n721: \\tfunc() {\\n722: \\t\\tdefer func() {\\n723: \\t\\t\\tpreShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Signal()\\n724: \\t\\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] pre-shutdown hooks completed\\\", \\\"name\\\", preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Name())\\n725: \\t\\t}()\\n726: \\t\\terr = s.RunPreShutdownHooks()\\n727: \\t}()\\n728: \\tif err != nil {\\n729: \\t\\treturn err\\n730: \\t}\\n731: \\n732: \\t// Wait for all requests in flight to drain, bounded by the RequestTimeout variable.\\n733: \\t<-drainedCh.Signaled()\\n734: \\n735: \\tif s.AuditBackend != nil {\\n736: \\t\\ts.AuditBackend.Shutdown()\\n737: \\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] audit backend shutdown completed\\\")\\n738: \\t}\\n739: \\n740: \\t// wait for stoppedCh that is closed when the graceful termination (server.Shutdown) is finished.\\n741: \\t<-listenerStoppedCh\\n742: \\t<-stoppedCh\\n743: \\n744: \\tklog.V(1).Info(\\\"[graceful-termination] apiserver is exiting\\\")\\n745: \\treturn nil\\n746: }\\n747: \\n748: // NonBlockingRun spawns the secure http server. An error is\\n749: // returned if the secure port cannot be listened on.\\n750: // The returned channel is closed when the (asynchronous) termination is finished.\\n751: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL550-750\"}",

    "content": "550: \t}\n551: \n552: \t// spawn a new goroutine for closing the MuxAndDiscoveryComplete signal\n553: \t// registration happens during construction of the generic api server\n554: \t// the last server in the chain aggregates signals from the previous instances\n555: \tgo func() {\n556: \t\tfor _, muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal := range s.GenericAPIServer.MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() {\n557: \t\t\tselect {\n558: \t\t\tcase <-muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal:\n559: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n560: \t\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n561: \t\t\t\tklog.V(1).Infof(\"haven't completed %s, stop requested\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\n562: \t\t\t\treturn\n563: \t\t\t}\n564: \t\t}\n565: \t\ts.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signal()\n566: \t\tklog.V(1).Infof(\"%s has all endpoints registered and discovery information is complete\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\n567: \t}()\n568: \n569: \tgo func() {\n570: \t\tdefer delayedStopCh.Signal()\n571: \t\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", delayedStopCh.Name())\n572: \n573: \t\t<-stopCh\n574: \n575: \t\t// As soon as shutdown is initiated, /readyz should start returning failure.\n576: \t\t// This gives the load balancer a window defined by ShutdownDelayDuration to detect that /readyz is red\n577: \t\t// and stop sending traffic to this server.\n578: \t\tshutdownInitiatedCh.Signal()\n579: \t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", shutdownInitiatedCh.Name())\n580: \n581: \t\ttime.Sleep(s.ShutdownDelayDuration)\n582: \t}()\n583: \n584: \t// close socket after delayed stopCh\n585: \tshutdownTimeout := s.ShutdownTimeout\n586: \tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\n587: \t\t// when this mode is enabled, we do the following:\n588: \t\t// - the server will continue to listen until all existing requests in flight\n589: \t\t//   (not including active long running requests) have been drained.\n590: \t\t// - once drained, http Server Shutdown is invoked with a timeout of 2s,\n591: \t\t//   net/http waits for 1s for the peer to respond to a GO_AWAY frame, so\n592: \t\t//   we should wait for a minimum of 2s\n593: \t\tshutdownTimeout = 2 * time.Second\n594: \t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] using HTTP Server shutdown timeout\", \"shutdownTimeout\", shutdownTimeout)\n595: \t}\n596: \n597: \tnotAcceptingNewRequestCh := s.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest\n598: \tdrainedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.InFlightRequestsDrained\n599: \t// Canceling the parent context does not immediately cancel the HTTP server.\n600: \t// We only inherit context values here and deal with cancellation ourselves.\n601: \tstopHTTPServerCtx, stopHTTPServer := context.WithCancelCause(context.WithoutCancel(ctx))\n602: \tgo func() {\n603: \t\tdefer stopHTTPServer(errors.New(\"time to stop HTTP server\"))\n604: \n605: \t\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh := notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\n606: \t\tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\n607: \t\t\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh = drainedCh.Signaled()\n608: \t\t}\n609: \n610: \t\t<-timeToStopHttpServerCh\n611: \t}()\n612: \n613: \t// Start the audit backend before any request comes in. This means we must call Backend.Run\n614: \t// before http server start serving. Otherwise the Backend.ProcessEvents call might block.\n615: \t// AuditBackend.Run will stop as soon as all in-flight requests are drained.\n616: \tif s.AuditBackend != nil {\n617: \t\tif err := s.AuditBackend.Run(drainedCh.Signaled()); err != nil {\n618: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to run the audit backend: %v\", err)\n619: \t\t}\n620: \t}\n621: \n622: \tstoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err := s.NonBlockingRunWithContext(stopHTTPServerCtx, shutdownTimeout)\n623: \tif err != nil {\n624: \t\treturn err\n625: \t}\n626: \n627: \thttpServerStoppedListeningCh := s.lifecycleSignals.HTTPServerStoppedListening\n628: \tgo func() {\n629: \t\t<-listenerStoppedCh\n630: \t\thttpServerStoppedListeningCh.Signal()\n631: \t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", httpServerStoppedListeningCh.Name())\n632: \t}()\n633: \n634: \t// we don't accept new request as soon as both ShutdownDelayDuration has\n635: \t// elapsed and preshutdown hooks have completed.\n636: \tpreShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.PreShutdownHooksStopped\n637: \tgo func() {\n638: \t\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Name())\n639: \t\tdefer notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signal()\n640: \n641: \t\t// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain\n642: \t\t<-delayedStopCh.Signaled()\n643: \n644: \t\t// Additionally wait for preshutdown hooks to also be finished, as some of them need\n645: \t\t// to send API calls to clean up after themselves (e.g. lease reconcilers removing\n646: \t\t// itself from the active servers).\n647: \t\t<-preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Signaled()\n648: \t}()\n649: \n650: \t// wait for all in-flight non-long running requests to finish\n651: \tnonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})\n652: \tgo func() {\n653: \t\tdefer close(nonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh)\n654: \t\tdefer klog.V(1).Info(\"[graceful-termination] in-flight non long-running request(s) have drained\")\n655: \n656: \t\t// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain (it rejects everything after Wait has been called).\n657: \t\t<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\n658: \n659: \t\t// Wait for all requests to finish, which are bounded by the RequestTimeout variable.\n660: \t\t// once NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait is invoked, the apiserver is\n661: \t\t// expected to reject any incoming request with a {503, Retry-After}\n662: \t\t// response via the WithWaitGroup filter. On the contrary, we observe\n663: \t\t// that incoming request(s) get a 'connection refused' error, this is\n664: \t\t// because, at this point, we have called 'Server.Shutdown' and\n665: \t\t// net/http server has stopped listening. This causes incoming\n666: \t\t// request to get a 'connection refused' error.\n667: \t\t// On the other hand, if 'ShutdownSendRetryAfter' is enabled incoming\n668: \t\t// requests will be rejected with a {429, Retry-After} since\n669: \t\t// 'Server.Shutdown' will be invoked only after in-flight requests\n670: \t\t// have been drained.\n671: \t\t// TODO: can we consolidate these two modes of graceful termination?\n672: \t\ts.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait()\n673: \t}()\n674: \n675: \t// wait for all in-flight watches to finish\n676: \tactiveWatchesDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})\n677: \tgo func() {\n678: \t\tdefer close(activeWatchesDrainedCh)\n679: \n680: \t\t<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\n681: \t\tif s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod <= time.Duration(0) {\n682: \t\t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] not going to wait for active watch request(s) to drain\")\n683: \t\t\treturn\n684: \t\t}\n685: \n686: \t\t// Wait for all active watches to finish\n687: \t\tgrace := s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod\n688: \t\tactiveBefore, activeAfter, err := s.WatchRequestWaitGroup.Wait(func(count int) (utilwaitgroup.RateLimiter, context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {\n689: \t\t\tqps := float64(count) / grace.Seconds()\n690: \t\t\t// TODO: we don't want the QPS (max requests drained per second) to\n691: \t\t\t//  get below a certain floor value, since we want the server to\n692: \t\t\t//  drain the active watch requests as soon as possible.\n693: \t\t\t//  For now, it's hard coded to 200, and it is subject to change\n694: \t\t\t//  based on the result from the scale testing.\n695: \t\t\tif qps < 200 {\n696: \t\t\t\tqps = 200\n697: \t\t\t}\n698: \n699: \t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), grace)\n700: \t\t\t// We don't expect more than one token to be consumed\n701: \t\t\t// in a single Wait call, so setting burst to 1.\n702: \t\t\treturn rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(qps), 1), ctx, cancel\n703: \t\t})\n704: \t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] active watch request(s) have drained\",\n705: \t\t\t\"duration\", grace, \"activeWatchesBefore\", activeBefore, \"activeWatchesAfter\", activeAfter, \"error\", err)\n706: \t}()\n707: \n708: \tgo func() {\n709: \t\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", drainedCh.Name())\n710: \t\tdefer drainedCh.Signal()\n711: \n712: \t\t<-nonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh\n713: \t\t<-activeWatchesDrainedCh\n714: \t}()\n715: \n716: \tklog.V(1).Info(\"[graceful-termination] waiting for shutdown to be initiated\")\n717: \t<-stopCh\n718: \n719: \t// run shutdown hooks directly. This includes deregistering from\n720: \t// the kubernetes endpoint in case of kube-apiserver.\n721: \tfunc() {\n722: \t\tdefer func() {\n723: \t\t\tpreShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Signal()\n724: \t\t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] pre-shutdown hooks completed\", \"name\", preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Name())\n725: \t\t}()\n726: \t\terr = s.RunPreShutdownHooks()\n727: \t}()\n728: \tif err != nil {\n729: \t\treturn err\n730: \t}\n731: \n732: \t// Wait for all requests in flight to drain, bounded by the RequestTimeout variable.\n733: \t<-drainedCh.Signaled()\n734: \n735: \tif s.AuditBackend != nil {\n736: \t\ts.AuditBackend.Shutdown()\n737: \t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] audit backend shutdown completed\")\n738: \t}\n739: \n740: \t// wait for stoppedCh that is closed when the graceful termination (server.Shutdown) is finished.\n741: \t<-listenerStoppedCh\n742: \t<-stoppedCh\n743: \n744: \tklog.V(1).Info(\"[graceful-termination] apiserver is exiting\")\n745: \treturn nil\n746: }\n747: \n748: // NonBlockingRun spawns the secure http server. An error is\n749: // returned if the secure port cannot be listened on.\n750: // The returned channel is closed when the (asynchronous) termination is finished.\n751: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL550-750"
  }
}

Input
{
  "endLine": 200,
