24. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 350,
  "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go",
  "repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes",
  "startLine": 200
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"200: \\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\\n201: \\tGet(ctx context.Context, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error\\n202: \\n203: \\t// GetList unmarshalls objects found at key into a *List api object (an object\\n204: \\t// that satisfies runtime.IsList definition).\\n205: \\t// If 'opts.Recursive' is false, 'key' is used as an exact match. If `opts.Recursive'\\n206: \\t// is true, 'key' is used as a prefix.\\n207: \\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\\n208: \\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\\n209: \\tGetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error\\n210: \\n211: \\t// GuaranteedUpdate keeps calling 'tryUpdate()' to update key 'key' (of type 'destination')\\n212: \\t// retrying the update until success if there is index conflict.\\n213: \\t// Note that object passed to tryUpdate may change across invocations of tryUpdate() if\\n214: \\t// other writers are simultaneously updating it, so tryUpdate() needs to take into account\\n215: \\t// the current contents of the object when deciding how the update object should look.\\n216: \\t// If the key doesn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error if ignoreNotFound=false\\n217: \\t// else `destination` will be set to the zero value of it's type.\\n218: \\t// If the eventual successful invocation of `tryUpdate` returns an output with the same serialized\\n219: \\t// contents as the input, it won't perform any update, but instead set `destination` to an object with those\\n220: \\t// contents.\\n221: \\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\\n222: \\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\\n223: \\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\\n224: \\t//\\n225: \\t// Example:\\n226: \\t//\\n227: \\t// s := /* implementation of Interface */\\n228: \\t// err := s.GuaranteedUpdate(\\n229: \\t//     \\\"myKey\\\", &MyType{}, true, preconditions,\\n230: \\t//     func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (runtime.Object, *uint64, error) {\\n231: \\t//       // Before each invocation of the user defined function, \\\"input\\\" is reset to\\n232: \\t//       // current contents for \\\"myKey\\\" in database.\\n233: \\t//       curr := input.(*MyType)  // Guaranteed to succeed.\\n234: \\t//\\n235: \\t//       // Make the modification\\n236: \\t//       curr.Counter++\\n237: \\t//\\n238: \\t//       // Return the modified object - return an error to stop iterating. Return\\n239: \\t//       // a uint64 to alter the TTL on the object, or nil to keep it the same value.\\n240: \\t//       return cur, nil, nil\\n241: \\t//    }, cachedExistingObject\\n242: \\t// )\\n243: \\tGuaranteedUpdate(\\n244: \\t\\tctx context.Context, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,\\n245: \\t\\tpreconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error\\n246: \\n247: \\t// Stats returns storage stats.\\n248: \\tStats(ctx context.Context) (Stats, error)\\n249: \\n250: \\t// ReadinessCheck checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\\n251: \\tReadinessCheck() error\\n252: \\n253: \\t// RequestWatchProgress requests the a watch stream progress status be sent in the\\n254: \\t// watch response stream as soon as possible.\\n255: \\t// Used for monitor watch progress even if watching resources with no changes.\\n256: \\t//\\n257: \\t// If watch is lagging, progress status might:\\n258: \\t// * be pointing to stale resource version. Use etcd KV request to get linearizable resource version.\\n259: \\t// * not be delivered at all. It's recommended to poll request progress periodically.\\n260: \\t//\\n261: \\t// Note: Only watches with matching context grpc metadata will be notified.\\n262: \\t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/9325a57125e8502941d1b0c7379c4bb80a678d5c/vendor/go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/watch.go#L1037-L1042\\n263: \\t//\\n264: \\t// TODO: Remove when storage.Interface will be separate from etc3.store.\\n265: \\t// Deprecated: Added temporarily to simplify exposing RequestProgress for watch cache.\\n266: \\tRequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error\\n267: \\n268: \\t// GetCurrentResourceVersion gets the current resource version from etcd.\\n269: \\t// This method issues an empty list request and reads only the ResourceVersion from the object metadata\\n270: \\tGetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)\\n271: \\n272: \\t// EnableResourceSizeEstimation enables estimating resource size by providing function get keys from storage.\\n273: \\tEnableResourceSizeEstimation(KeysFunc) error\\n274: \\n275: \\t// CompactRevision returns latest observed revision that was compacted.\\n276: \\t// Without ListFromCacheSnapshot enabled only locally executed compaction will be observed.\\n277: \\t// Returns 0 if no compaction was yet observed.\\n278: \\tCompactRevision() int64\\n279: }\\n280: \\n281: // KeysFunc is a function prototype to fetch keys from storage.\\n282: type KeysFunc func(context.Context) ([]string, error)\\n283: \\n284: // GetOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage get operations.\\n285: type GetOptions struct {\\n286: \\t// IgnoreNotFound determines what is returned if the requested object is not found. If\\n287: \\t// true, a zero object is returned. If false, an error is returned.\\n288: \\tIgnoreNotFound bool\\n289: \\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the get operation\\n290: \\t// as a \\\"not older than\\\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\\n291: \\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\\n292: \\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\\n293: \\tResourceVersion string\\n294: }\\n295: \\n296: // ListOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage list operations.\\n297: type ListOptions struct {\\n298: \\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the list operation\\n299: \\t// as a \\\"not older than\\\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\\n300: \\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\\n301: \\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\\n302: \\tResourceVersion string\\n303: \\t// ResourceVersionMatch provides the rule for how the resource version constraint applies. If set\\n304: \\t// to the default value \\\"\\\" the legacy resource version semantic apply.\\n305: \\tResourceVersionMatch metav1.ResourceVersionMatch\\n306: \\t// Predicate provides the selection rules for the list operation.\\n307: \\tPredicate SelectionPredicate\\n308: \\t// Recursive determines whether the list or watch is defined for a single object located at the\\n309: \\t// given key, or for the whole set of objects with the given key as a prefix.\\n310: \\tRecursive bool\\n311: \\t// ProgressNotify determines whether storage-originated bookmark (progress notify) events should\\n312: \\t// be delivered to the users. The option is ignored for non-watch requests.\\n313: \\t//\\n314: \\t// Firstly, note that this field is different from the Predicate.AllowWatchBookmarks field.\\n315: \\t// Secondly, this field is intended for internal clients only such as the watch cache.\\n316: \\t//\\n317: \\t// This means that external clients do not have the ability to set this field directly.\\n318: \\t// For example by setting the allowWatchBookmarks query parameter.\\n319: \\t//\\n320: \\t// The motivation for this approach is the fact that the frequency\\n321: \\t// of bookmark events from a storage like etcd might be very high.\\n322: \\t// As the number of watch requests increases, the server load would also increase.\\n323: \\t//\\n324: \\t// Furthermore, the server is not obligated to provide bookmark events at all,\\n325: \\t// as described in https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/956-watch-bookmark#proposal\\n326: \\tProgressNotify bool\\n327: \\t// SendInitialEvents, when set together with Watch option,\\n328: \\t// begin the watch stream with synthetic init events to build the\\n329: \\t// whole state of all resources followed by a synthetic \\\"Bookmark\\\"\\n330: \\t// event containing a ResourceVersion after which the server\\n331: \\t// continues streaming events.\\n332: \\tSendInitialEvents *bool\\n333: }\\n334: \\n335: // DeleteOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage delete operations.\\n336: type DeleteOptions struct {\\n337: \\t// IgnoreStoreReadError, if enabled, will ignore store read error\\n338: \\t// such as transformation or decode failure and go ahead with the\\n339: \\t// deletion of the object.\\n340: \\t// NOTE: for normal deletion flow it should always be false, it may be\\n341: \\t// enabled by the caller only to facilitate unsafe deletion of corrupt\\n342: \\t// object which otherwise can not be deleted using the normal flow\\n343: \\tIgnoreStoreReadError bool\\n344: }\\n345: \\n346: func ValidateListOptions(keyPrefix string, versioner Versioner, opts ListOptions) (withRev int64, continueKey string, err error) {\\n347: \\tif opts.Recursive && len(opts.Predicate.Continue) > 0 {\\n348: \\t\\tcontinueKey, continueRV, err := DecodeContinue(opts.Predicate.Continue, keyPrefix)\\n349: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n350: \\t\\t\\treturn 0, \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"invalid continue token: %v\\\", err))\\n351: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go%3FL200-350\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "200: \t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\n201: \tGet(ctx context.Context, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error\n202: \n203: \t// GetList unmarshalls objects found at key into a *List api object (an object\n204: \t// that satisfies runtime.IsList definition).\n205: \t// If 'opts.Recursive' is false, 'key' is used as an exact match. If `opts.Recursive'\n206: \t// is true, 'key' is used as a prefix.\n207: \t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\n208: \t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\n209: \tGetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error\n210: \n211: \t// GuaranteedUpdate keeps calling 'tryUpdate()' to update key 'key' (of type 'destination')\n212: \t// retrying the update until success if there is index conflict.\n213: \t// Note that object passed to tryUpdate may change across invocations of tryUpdate() if\n214: \t// other writers are simultaneously updating it, so tryUpdate() needs to take into account\n215: \t// the current contents of the object when deciding how the update object should look.\n216: \t// If the key doesn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error if ignoreNotFound=false\n217: \t// else `destination` will be set to the zero value of it's type.\n218: \t// If the eventual successful invocation of `tryUpdate` returns an output with the same serialized\n219: \t// contents as the input, it won't perform any update, but instead set `destination` to an object with those\n220: \t// contents.\n221: \t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\n222: \t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\n223: \t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\n224: \t//\n225: \t// Example:\n226: \t//\n227: \t// s := /* implementation of Interface */\n228: \t// err := s.GuaranteedUpdate(\n229: \t//     \"myKey\", &MyType{}, true, preconditions,\n230: \t//     func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (runtime.Object, *uint64, error) {\n231: \t//       // Before each invocation of the user defined function, \"input\" is reset to\n232: \t//       // current contents for \"myKey\" in database.\n233: \t//       curr := input.(*MyType)  // Guaranteed to succeed.\n234: \t//\n235: \t//       // Make the modification\n236: \t//       curr.Counter++\n237: \t//\n238: \t//       // Return the modified object - return an error to stop iterating. Return\n239: \t//       // a uint64 to alter the TTL on the object, or nil to keep it the same value.\n240: \t//       return cur, nil, nil\n241: \t//    }, cachedExistingObject\n242: \t// )\n243: \tGuaranteedUpdate(\n244: \t\tctx context.Context, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,\n245: \t\tpreconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error\n246: \n247: \t// Stats returns storage stats.\n248: \tStats(ctx context.Context) (Stats, error)\n249: \n250: \t// ReadinessCheck checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\n251: \tReadinessCheck() error\n252: \n253: \t// RequestWatchProgress requests the a watch stream progress status be sent in the\n254: \t// watch response stream as soon as possible.\n255: \t// Used for monitor watch progress even if watching resources with no changes.\n256: \t//\n257: \t// If watch is lagging, progress status might:\n258: \t// * be pointing to stale resource version. Use etcd KV request to get linearizable resource version.\n259: \t// * not be delivered at all. It's recommended to poll request progress periodically.\n260: \t//\n261: \t// Note: Only watches with matching context grpc metadata will be notified.\n262: \t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/9325a57125e8502941d1b0c7379c4bb80a678d5c/vendor/go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/watch.go#L1037-L1042\n263: \t//\n264: \t// TODO: Remove when storage.Interface will be separate from etc3.store.\n265: \t// Deprecated: Added temporarily to simplify exposing RequestProgress for watch cache.\n266: \tRequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error\n267: \n268: \t// GetCurrentResourceVersion gets the current resource version from etcd.\n269: \t// This method issues an empty list request and reads only the ResourceVersion from the object metadata\n270: \tGetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)\n271: \n272: \t// EnableResourceSizeEstimation enables estimating resource size by providing function get keys from storage.\n273: \tEnableResourceSizeEstimation(KeysFunc) error\n274: \n275: \t// CompactRevision returns latest observed revision that was compacted.\n276: \t// Without ListFromCacheSnapshot enabled only locally executed compaction will be observed.\n277: \t// Returns 0 if no compaction was yet observed.\n278: \tCompactRevision() int64\n279: }\n280: \n281: // KeysFunc is a function prototype to fetch keys from storage.\n282: type KeysFunc func(context.Context) ([]string, error)\n283: \n284: // GetOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage get operations.\n285: type GetOptions struct {\n286: \t// IgnoreNotFound determines what is returned if the requested object is not found. If\n287: \t// true, a zero object is returned. If false, an error is returned.\n288: \tIgnoreNotFound bool\n289: \t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the get operation\n290: \t// as a \"not older than\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\n291: \t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\n292: \t// ResourceVersion may be served.\n293: \tResourceVersion string\n294: }\n295: \n296: // ListOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage list operations.\n297: type ListOptions struct {\n298: \t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the list operation\n299: \t// as a \"not older than\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\n300: \t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\n301: \t// ResourceVersion may be served.\n302: \tResourceVersion string\n303: \t// ResourceVersionMatch provides the rule for how the resource version constraint applies. If set\n304: \t// to the default value \"\" the legacy resource version semantic apply.\n305: \tResourceVersionMatch metav1.ResourceVersionMatch\n306: \t// Predicate provides the selection rules for the list operation.\n307: \tPredicate SelectionPredicate\n308: \t// Recursive determines whether the list or watch is defined for a single object located at the\n309: \t// given key, or for the whole set of objects with the given key as a prefix.\n310: \tRecursive bool\n311: \t// ProgressNotify determines whether storage-originated bookmark (progress notify) events should\n312: \t// be delivered to the users. The option is ignored for non-watch requests.\n313: \t//\n314: \t// Firstly, note that this field is different from the Predicate.AllowWatchBookmarks field.\n315: \t// Secondly, this field is intended for internal clients only such as the watch cache.\n316: \t//\n317: \t// This means that external clients do not have the ability to set this field directly.\n318: \t// For example by setting the allowWatchBookmarks query parameter.\n319: \t//\n320: \t// The motivation for this approach is the fact that the frequency\n321: \t// of bookmark events from a storage like etcd might be very high.\n322: \t// As the number of watch requests increases, the server load would also increase.\n323: \t//\n324: \t// Furthermore, the server is not obligated to provide bookmark events at all,\n325: \t// as described in https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/956-watch-bookmark#proposal\n326: \tProgressNotify bool\n327: \t// SendInitialEvents, when set together with Watch option,\n328: \t// begin the watch stream with synthetic init events to build the\n329: \t// whole state of all resources followed by a synthetic \"Bookmark\"\n330: \t// event containing a ResourceVersion after which the server\n331: \t// continues streaming events.\n332: \tSendInitialEvents *bool\n333: }\n334: \n335: // DeleteOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage delete operations.\n336: type DeleteOptions struct {\n337: \t// IgnoreStoreReadError, if enabled, will ignore store read error\n338: \t// such as transformation or decode failure and go ahead with the\n339: \t// deletion of the object.\n340: \t// NOTE: for normal deletion flow it should always be false, it may be\n341: \t// enabled by the caller only to facilitate unsafe deletion of corrupt\n342: \t// object which otherwise can not be deleted using the normal flow\n343: \tIgnoreStoreReadError bool\n344: }\n345: \n346: func ValidateListOptions(keyPrefix string, versioner Versioner, opts ListOptions) (withRev int64, continueKey string, err error) {\n347: \tif opts.Recursive && len(opts.Predicate.Continue) > 0 {\n348: \t\tcontinueKey, continueRV, err := DecodeContinue(opts.Predicate.Continue, keyPrefix)\n349: \t\tif err != nil {\n350: \t\t\treturn 0, \"\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid continue token: %v\", err))\n351: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go%3FL200-350"
  }
}

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package admission\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"io\\\"\\n22: \\n23: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n25: \\tauditinternal \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\\\"\\n27: )\\n28: \\n29: // Attributes is an interface used by AdmissionController to get information about a request\\n30: // that is used to make an admission decision.\\n31: type Attributes interface {\\n32: \\t// GetName returns the name of the object as presented in the request.  On a CREATE operation, the client\\n33: \\t// may omit name and rely on the server to generate the name.  If that is the case, this method will return\\n34: \\t// the empty string\\n35: \\tGetName() string\\n36: \\t// GetNamespace is the namespace associated with the request (if any)\\n37: \\tGetNamespace() string\\n38: \\t// GetResource is the name of the resource being requested.  This is not the kind.  For example: pods\\n39: \\tGetResource() schema.GroupVersionResource\\n40: \\t// GetSubresource is the name of the subresource being requested.  This is a different resource, scoped to the parent resource, but it may have a different kind.\\n41: \\t// For instance, /pods has the resource \\\"pods\\\" and the kind \\\"Pod\\\", while /pods/foo/status has the resource \\\"pods\\\", the sub resource \\\"status\\\", and the kind \\\"Pod\\\"\\n42: \\t// (because status operates on pods). The binding resource for a pod though may be /pods/foo/binding, which has resource \\\"pods\\\", subresource \\\"binding\\\", and kind \\\"Binding\\\".\\n43: \\tGetSubresource() string\\n44: \\t// GetOperation is the operation being performed\\n45: \\tGetOperation() Operation\\n46: \\t// GetOperationOptions is the options for the operation being performed\\n47: \\tGetOperationOptions() runtime.Object\\n48: \\t// IsDryRun indicates that modifications will definitely not be persisted for this request. This is to prevent\\n49: \\t// admission controllers with side effects and a method of reconciliation from being overwhelmed.\\n50: \\t// However, a value of false for this does not mean that the modification will be persisted, because it\\n51: \\t// could still be rejected by a subsequent validation step.\\n52: \\tIsDryRun() bool\\n53: \\t// GetObject is the object from the incoming request prior to default values being applied\\n54: \\tGetObject() runtime.Object\\n55: \\t// GetOldObject is the existing object. Only populated for UPDATE and DELETE requests.\\n56: \\tGetOldObject() runtime.Object\\n57: \\t// GetKind is the type of object being manipulated.  For example: Pod\\n58: \\tGetKind() schema.GroupVersionKind\\n59: \\t// GetUserInfo is information about the requesting user\\n60: \\tGetUserInfo() user.Info\\n61: \\n62: \\t// AddAnnotation sets annotation according to key-value pair. The key should be qualified, e.g., podsecuritypolicy.admission.k8s.io/admit-policy, where\\n63: \\t// \\\"podsecuritypolicy\\\" is the name of the plugin, \\\"admission.k8s.io\\\" is the name of the organization, \\\"admit-policy\\\" is the key name.\\n64: \\t// An error is returned if the format of key is invalid. When trying to overwrite annotation with a new value, an error is returned.\\n65: \\t// Both ValidationInterface and MutationInterface are allowed to add Annotations.\\n66: \\t// By default, an annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or\\n67: \\t// equal to Metadata.\\n68: \\tAddAnnotation(key, value string) error\\n69: \\n70: \\t// AddAnnotationWithLevel sets annotation according to key-value pair with additional intended audit level.\\n71: \\t// An Annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or equal to the\\n72: \\t// intended audit level.\\n73: \\tAddAnnotationWithLevel(key, value string, level auditinternal.Level) error\\n74: \\n75: \\t// GetReinvocationContext tracks the admission request information relevant to the re-invocation policy.\\n76: \\tGetReinvocationContext() ReinvocationContext\\n77: }\\n78: \\n79: // ObjectInterfaces is an interface used by AdmissionController to get object interfaces\\n80: // such as Converter or Defaulter. These interfaces are normally coming from Request Scope\\n81: // to handle special cases like CRDs.\\n82: type ObjectInterfaces interface {\\n83: \\t// GetObjectCreater is the ObjectCreator appropriate for the requested object.\\n84: \\tGetObjectCreater() runtime.ObjectCreater\\n85: \\t// GetObjectTyper is the ObjectTyper appropriate for the requested object.\\n86: \\tGetObjectTyper() runtime.ObjectTyper\\n87: \\t// GetObjectDefaulter is the ObjectDefaulter appropriate for the requested object.\\n88: \\tGetObjectDefaulter() runtime.ObjectDefaulter\\n89: \\t// GetObjectConvertor is the ObjectConvertor appropriate for the requested object.\\n90: \\tGetObjectConvertor() runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n91: \\t// GetEquivalentResourceMapper is the EquivalentResourceMapper appropriate for finding equivalent resources and expected kind for the requested object.\\n92: \\tGetEquivalentResourceMapper() runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\\n93: }\\n94: \\n95: // privateAnnotationsGetter is a private interface which allows users to get annotations from Attributes.\\n96: type privateAnnotationsGetter interface {\\n97: \\tgetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\\n98: }\\n99: \\n100: // AnnotationsGetter allows users to get annotations from Attributes. An alternate Attribute should implement\\n101: // this interface.\\n102: type AnnotationsGetter interface {\\n103: \\tGetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\\n104: }\\n105: \\n106: // ReinvocationContext provides access to the admission related state required to implement the re-invocation policy.\\n107: type ReinvocationContext interface {\\n108: \\t// IsReinvoke returns true if the current admission check is a re-invocation.\\n109: \\tIsReinvoke() bool\\n110: \\t// SetIsReinvoke sets the current admission check as a re-invocation.\\n111: \\tSetIsReinvoke()\\n112: \\t// ShouldReinvoke returns true if any plugin has requested a re-invocation.\\n113: \\tShouldReinvoke() bool\\n114: \\t// SetShouldReinvoke signals that a re-invocation is desired.\\n115: \\tSetShouldReinvoke()\\n116: \\t// AddValue set a value for a plugin name, possibly overriding a previous value.\\n117: \\tSetValue(plugin string, v interface{})\\n118: \\t// Value reads a value for a webhook.\\n119: \\tValue(plugin string) interface{}\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: // Interface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\\n123: type Interface interface {\\n124: \\t// Handles returns true if this admission controller can handle the given operation\\n125: \\t// where operation can be one of CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, or CONNECT\\n126: \\tHandles(operation Operation) bool\\n127: }\\n128: \\n129: type MutationInterface interface {\\n130: \\tInterface\\n131: \\n132: \\t// Admit makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.\\n133: \\t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\\n134: \\tAdmit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\\n135: }\\n136: \\n137: // ValidationInterface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\\n138: type ValidationInterface interface {\\n139: \\tInterface\\n140: \\n141: \\t// Validate makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.  It is NOT allowed to mutate\\n142: \\t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\\n143: \\tValidate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\\n144: }\\n145: \\n146: // Operation is the type of resource operation being checked for admission control\\n147: type Operation string\\n148: \\n149: // Operation constants\\n150: const (\\n151: \\tCreate  Operation = \\\"CREATE\\\"\\n152: \\tUpdate  Operation = \\\"UPDATE\\\"\\n153: \\tDelete  Operation = \\\"DELETE\\\"\\n154: \\tConnect Operation = \\\"CONNECT\\\"\\n155: )\\n156: \\n157: // PluginInitializer is used for initialization of shareable resources between admission plugins.\\n158: // After initialization the resources have to be set separately\\n159: type PluginInitializer interface {\\n160: \\tInitialize(plugin Interface)\\n161: }\\n162: \\n163: // InitializationValidator holds ValidateInitialization functions, which are responsible for validation of initialized\\n164: // shared resources and should be implemented on admission plugins\\n165: type InitializationValidator interface {\\n166: \\tValidateInitialization() error\\n167: }\\n168: \\n169: // ConfigProvider provides a way to get configuration for an admission plugin based on its name\\n170: type ConfigProvider interface {\\n171: \\tConfigFor(pluginName string) (io.Reader, error)\\n172: }\\n173: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go%3FL1-200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package admission\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"io\"\n22: \n23: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n24: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n25: \tauditinternal \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit\"\n26: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n27: )\n28: \n29: // Attributes is an interface used by AdmissionController to get information about a request\n30: // that is used to make an admission decision.\n31: type Attributes interface {\n32: \t// GetName returns the name of the object as presented in the request.  On a CREATE operation, the client\n33: \t// may omit name and rely on the server to generate the name.  If that is the case, this method will return\n34: \t// the empty string\n35: \tGetName() string\n36: \t// GetNamespace is the namespace associated with the request (if any)\n37: \tGetNamespace() string\n38: \t// GetResource is the name of the resource being requested.  This is not the kind.  For example: pods\n39: \tGetResource() schema.GroupVersionResource\n40: \t// GetSubresource is the name of the subresource being requested.  This is a different resource, scoped to the parent resource, but it may have a different kind.\n41: \t// For instance, /pods has the resource \"pods\" and the kind \"Pod\", while /pods/foo/status has the resource \"pods\", the sub resource \"status\", and the kind \"Pod\"\n42: \t// (because status operates on pods). The binding resource for a pod though may be /pods/foo/binding, which has resource \"pods\", subresource \"binding\", and kind \"Binding\".\n43: \tGetSubresource() string\n44: \t// GetOperation is the operation being performed\n45: \tGetOperation() Operation\n46: \t// GetOperationOptions is the options for the operation being performed\n47: \tGetOperationOptions() runtime.Object\n48: \t// IsDryRun indicates that modifications will definitely not be persisted for this request. This is to prevent\n49: \t// admission controllers with side effects and a method of reconciliation from being overwhelmed.\n50: \t// However, a value of false for this does not mean that the modification will be persisted, because it\n51: \t// could still be rejected by a subsequent validation step.\n52: \tIsDryRun() bool\n53: \t// GetObject is the object from the incoming request prior to default values being applied\n54: \tGetObject() runtime.Object\n55: \t// GetOldObject is the existing object. Only populated for UPDATE and DELETE requests.\n56: \tGetOldObject() runtime.Object\n57: \t// GetKind is the type of object being manipulated.  For example: Pod\n58: \tGetKind() schema.GroupVersionKind\n59: \t// GetUserInfo is information about the requesting user\n60: \tGetUserInfo() user.Info\n61: \n62: \t// AddAnnotation sets annotation according to key-value pair. The key should be qualified, e.g., podsecuritypolicy.admission.k8s.io/admit-policy, where\n63: \t// \"podsecuritypolicy\" is the name of the plugin, \"admission.k8s.io\" is the name of the organization, \"admit-policy\" is the key name.\n64: \t// An error is returned if the format of key is invalid. When trying to overwrite annotation with a new value, an error is returned.\n65: \t// Both ValidationInterface and MutationInterface are allowed to add Annotations.\n66: \t// By default, an annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or\n67: \t// equal to Metadata.\n68: \tAddAnnotation(key, value string) error\n69: \n70: \t// AddAnnotationWithLevel sets annotation according to key-value pair with additional intended audit level.\n71: \t// An Annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or equal to the\n72: \t// intended audit level.\n73: \tAddAnnotationWithLevel(key, value string, level auditinternal.Level) error\n74: \n75: \t// GetReinvocationContext tracks the admission request information relevant to the re-invocation policy.\n76: \tGetReinvocationContext() ReinvocationContext\n77: }\n78: \n79: // ObjectInterfaces is an interface used by AdmissionController to get object interfaces\n80: // such as Converter or Defaulter. These interfaces are normally coming from Request Scope\n81: // to handle special cases like CRDs.\n82: type ObjectInterfaces interface {\n83: \t// GetObjectCreater is the ObjectCreator appropriate for the requested object.\n84: \tGetObjectCreater() runtime.ObjectCreater\n85: \t// GetObjectTyper is the ObjectTyper appropriate for the requested object.\n86: \tGetObjectTyper() runtime.ObjectTyper\n87: \t// GetObjectDefaulter is the ObjectDefaulter appropriate for the requested object.\n88: \tGetObjectDefaulter() runtime.ObjectDefaulter\n89: \t// GetObjectConvertor is the ObjectConvertor appropriate for the requested object.\n90: \tGetObjectConvertor() runtime.ObjectConvertor\n91: \t// GetEquivalentResourceMapper is the EquivalentResourceMapper appropriate for finding equivalent resources and expected kind for the requested object.\n92: \tGetEquivalentResourceMapper() runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\n93: }\n94: \n95: // privateAnnotationsGetter is a private interface which allows users to get annotations from Attributes.\n96: type privateAnnotationsGetter interface {\n97: \tgetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\n98: }\n99: \n100: // AnnotationsGetter allows users to get annotations from Attributes. An alternate Attribute should implement\n101: // this interface.\n102: type AnnotationsGetter interface {\n103: \tGetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\n104: }\n105: \n106: // ReinvocationContext provides access to the admission related state required to implement the re-invocation policy.\n107: type ReinvocationContext interface {\n108: \t// IsReinvoke returns true if the current admission check is a re-invocation.\n109: \tIsReinvoke() bool\n110: \t// SetIsReinvoke sets the current admission check as a re-invocation.\n111: \tSetIsReinvoke()\n112: \t// ShouldReinvoke returns true if any plugin has requested a re-invocation.\n113: \tShouldReinvoke() bool\n114: \t// SetShouldReinvoke signals that a re-invocation is desired.\n115: \tSetShouldReinvoke()\n116: \t// AddValue set a value for a plugin name, possibly overriding a previous value.\n117: \tSetValue(plugin string, v interface{})\n118: \t// Value reads a value for a webhook.\n119: \tValue(plugin string) interface{}\n120: }\n121: \n122: // Interface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\n123: type Interface interface {\n124: \t// Handles returns true if this admission controller can handle the given operation\n125: \t// where operation can be one of CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, or CONNECT\n126: \tHandles(operation Operation) bool\n127: }\n128: \n129: type MutationInterface interface {\n130: \tInterface\n131: \n132: \t// Admit makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.\n133: \t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\n134: \tAdmit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\n135: }\n136: \n137: // ValidationInterface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\n138: type ValidationInterface interface {\n139: \tInterface\n140: \n141: \t// Validate makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.  It is NOT allowed to mutate\n142: \t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\n143: \tValidate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\n144: }\n145: \n146: // Operation is the type of resource operation being checked for admission control\n147: type Operation string\n148: \n149: // Operation constants\n150: const (\n151: \tCreate  Operation = \"CREATE\"\n152: \tUpdate  Operation = \"UPDATE\"\n153: \tDelete  Operation = \"DELETE\"\n154: \tConnect Operation = \"CONNECT\"\n155: )\n156: \n157: // PluginInitializer is used for initialization of shareable resources between admission plugins.\n158: // After initialization the resources have to be set separately\n159: type PluginInitializer interface {\n160: \tInitialize(plugin Interface)\n161: }\n162: \n163: // InitializationValidator holds ValidateInitialization functions, which are responsible for validation of initialized\n164: // shared resources and should be implemented on admission plugins\n165: type InitializationValidator interface {\n166: \tValidateInitialization() error\n167: }\n168: \n169: // ConfigProvider provides a way to get configuration for an admission plugin based on its name\n170: type ConfigProvider interface {\n171: \tConfigFor(pluginName string) (io.Reader, error)\n172: }\n173: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go%3FL1-200"
  }
}

It's a map from version to resource to the storage.\\n\\tVersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\\n\\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the APIVersion used for common objects in the\\n\\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\\n\\t// define a version \\\"v1beta1\\\" but want to use the Kubernetes \\\"v1\\\" internal objects.\\n\\t// If nil, defaults to groupMeta.GroupVersion.\\n\\t// TODO: Remove this when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/19018 is fixed.\\n\\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n\\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \\\"meta.k8s.io/v1\\\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\\n\\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\\n\\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\\n\\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n\\n\\t// Scheme includes all of the types used by this group and how to convert between them (or\\n\\t// to convert objects from outside of this group that are accepted in this API).\\n\\t// TODO: replace with interfaces\\n\\tScheme *runtime.Scheme\\n\\t// NegotiatedSerializer controls how this group encodes and decodes data\\n\\tNegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\\n\\t// ParameterCodec performs conversions for query parameters passed to API calls\\n\\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\\n\\n\\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the definitions of all resources installed together.\\n\\t// It is set during InstallAPIGroups, InstallAPIGroup, and InstallLegacyAPIGroup.\\n\\tStaticOpenAPISpec map[string]*spec.Schema\\n}\\n\\nfunc (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"3b8cb144-3215-4a3f-921a-0ac62572a811\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:18.261Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"370bc43b-9bfe-4c12-bb0d-cb5f9aa366cb\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"3b8cb144-3215-4a3f-921a-0ac62572a811\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01CZhXogVHkLvWhqjVh7ozVQ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Good! \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/union\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\\\"\\n\\tdiscoveryendpoint \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery/aggregated\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filterlatency\\\"\\n\\tgenericapifilters \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/impersonation\\\"\\n\\tapiopenapi \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/openapi\\\"\\n\\tapirequest \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\\\"\\n\\tgenericfeatures \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n\\tgenericregistry \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/dynamiccertificates\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/egressselector\\\"\\n\\tgenericfilters \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/flagz\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routine\\\"\\n\\tserverstore \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n\\tutilflowcontrol \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol\\\"\\n\\tflowcontrolrequest \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/request\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/informers\\\"\\n\\trestclient \\\"k8s.io/client-go/rest\\\"\\n\\tbasecompatibility \\\"k8s.io/component-base/compatibility\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/logs\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/features\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/prometheus/slis\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n\\topenapicommon \\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/spec3\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/clock\\\"\\n\\tutilsnet \\\"k8s.io/utils/net\\\"\\n\\n\\t// install apis\\n\\t_ \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/apiserver/install\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// hostnameFunc is a function to set the hostnameFunc of this apiserver.\\n// To be used for testing purpose only, to simulate scenarios where multiple apiservers\\n// exist. In such cases we want to ensure unique apiserver IDs which are a hash of hostnameFunc.\\nvar (\\n\\thostnameFunc = os.Hostname\\n)\\n\\nconst (\\n\\t// DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix is where the legacy APIs will be located.\\n\\tDefaultLegacyAPIPrefix = \\\"/api\\\"\\n\\n\\t// APIGroupPrefix is where non-legacy API group will be located.\\n\\tAPIGroupPrefix = \\\"/apis\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// Config is a structure used to configure a GenericAPIServer.\\n// Its members are sorted roughly in order of importance for composers.\\ntype Config struct {\\n\\t// SecureServing is required to serve https\\n\\tSecureServing *SecureServingInfo\\n\\n\\t// Authentication is the configuration for authentication\\n\\tAuthentication AuthenticationInfo\\n\\n\\t// Authorization is the configuration for authorization\\n\\tAuthorization AuthorizationInfo\\n\\n\\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\\n\\t// This is required for proper functioning of the PostStartHooks on a GenericAPIServer\\n\\t// TODO: move into SecureServing(WithLoopback) as soon as insecure serving is gone\\n\\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\\n\\n\\t// EgressSelector provides a lookup mechanism for dialing outbound connections.\\n\\t// It does so based on a EgressSelectorConfiguration which was read at startup.\\n\\tEgressSelector *egressselector.EgressSelector\\n\\n\\t// RuleResolver is required to get the list of rules that apply to a given user\\n\\t// in a given namespace\\n\\tRuleResolver authorizer.RuleResolver\\n\\t// AdmissionControl performs deep inspection of a given request (including content)\\n\\t// to set values and determine whether its allowed\\n\\tAdmissionControl      admission.Interface\\n\\tCorsAllowedOriginList []string\\n\\tHSTSDirectives        []string\\n\\t// FlowControl, if not nil, gives priority and fairness to request handling\\n\\tFlowControl utilflowcontrol.Interface\\n\\n\\tEnableIndex     bool\\n\\tEnableProfiling bool\\n\\tDebugSocketPath string\\n\\tEnableDiscovery bool\\n\\n\\t// Requires generic profiling enabled\\n\\tEnableContentionProfiling bool\\n\\tEnableMetrics             bool\\n\\n\\tDisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\\n\\t// done values in this values for this map are ignored.\\n\\tPostStartHooks map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry\\n\\n\\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\\n\\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\\n\\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\\n\\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\\n\\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\\n\\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\\n\\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\\n\\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\\n\\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n\\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\\n\\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\\n\\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\\n\\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n\\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\\n\\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\\n\\t// AuditBackend is where audit events are sent to.\\n\\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\\n\\t// AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator makes the decision of whether and how to audit log a request.\\n\\tAuditPolicyRuleEvaluator audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator\\n\\t// ExternalAddress is the host name to use for external (public internet) facing URLs (e.g. Swagger)\\n\\t// Will default to a value based on secure serving info and available ipv4 IPs.\\n\\tExternalAddress string\\n\\n\\t// TracerProvider can provide a tracer, which records spans for distributed tracing.\\n\\tTracerProvider tracing.TracerProvider\\n\\n\\t//===========================================================================\\n\\t// Fields you probably don't care about changing\\n\\t//===========================================================================\\n\\n\\t// BuildHandlerChainFunc allows you to build custom handler chains by decorating the apiHandler.\\n\\tBuildHandlerChainFunc func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) (secure http.Handler)\\n\\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\\n\\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\\n\\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\\n\\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\\n\\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\\n\\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\\n\\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\\n\\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\\n\\t// DiscoveryAddresses is used to build the IPs pass to discovery. If nil, the ExternalAddress is\\n\\t// always reported\\n\\tDiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\\n\\t// The default set of healthz checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\\n\\tHealthzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\\n\\t// The default set of livez checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"d7a3c28e-c3f2-46e9-8823-6f523f65d0b5\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:21.992Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"f1c8026c-84bc-4d6b-845b-63be4f77947a\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d7a3c28e-c3f2-46e9-8823-6f523f65d0b5\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01VaCiR9ygHmGdjREzQPEtzm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage server\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"bytes\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n\\trt \\\"runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n\\n\\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/mux\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// APIServerHandlers holds the different http.Handlers used by the API server.\\n// This includes the full handler chain, the director (which chooses between gorestful and nonGoRestful,\\n// the gorestful handler (used for the API) which falls through to the nonGoRestful handler on unregistered paths,\\n// and the nonGoRestful handler (which can contain a fallthrough of its own)\\n// FullHandlerChain -> Director -> {GoRestfulContainer,NonGoRestfulMux} based on inspection of registered web services\\ntype APIServerHandler struct {\\n\\t// FullHandlerChain is the one that is eventually served with.  It should include the full filter\\n\\t// chain and then call the Director.\\n\\tFullHandlerChain http.Handler\\n\\t// The registered APIs.  InstallAPIs uses this.  Other servers probably shouldn't access this directly.\\n\\tGoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\\n\\t// NonGoRestfulMux is the final HTTP handler in the chain.\\n\\t// It comes after all filters and the API handling\\n\\t// This is where other servers can attach handler to various parts of the chain.\\n\\tNonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux\\n\\n\\t// Director is here so that we can properly handle fall through and proxy cases.\\n\\t// This looks a bit bonkers, but here's what's happening.  We need to have /apis handling registered in gorestful in order to have\\n\\t// swagger generated for compatibility.  Doing that with `/apis` as a webservice, means that it forcibly 404s (no defaulting allowed)\\n\\t// all requests which are not /apis or /apis/.  We need those calls to fall through behind gorestful for proper delegation.  Trying to\\n\\t// register for a pattern which includes everything behind it doesn't work because gorestful negotiates for verbs and content encoding\\n\\t// and all those things go crazy when gorestful really just needs to pass through.  In addition, openapi enforces unique verb constraints\\n\\t// which we don't fit into and it still muddies up swagger.  Trying to switch the webservices into a route doesn't work because the\\n\\t//  containing webservice faces all the same problems listed above.\\n\\t// This leads to the crazy thing done here.  Our mux does what we need, so we'll place it in front of gorestful.  It will introspect to\\n\\t// decide if the route is likely to be handled by gorestful and route there if needed.  Otherwise, it goes to NonGoRestfulMux mux in\\n\\t// order to handle \\\"normal\\\" paths and delegation. Hopefully no API consumers will ever have to deal with this level of detail.  I think\\n\\t// we should consider completely removing gorestful.\\n\\t// Other servers should only use this opaquely to delegate to an API server.\\n\\tDirector http.Handler\\n}\\n\\n// HandlerChainBuilderFn is used to wrap the GoRestfulContainer handler using the provided handler chain.\\n// It is normally used to apply filtering like authentication and authorization\\ntype HandlerChainBuilderFn func(apiHandler http.Handler) http.Handler\\n\\nfunc NewAPIServerHandler(name string, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, handlerChainBuilder HandlerChainBuilderFn, notFoundHandler http.Handler) *APIServerHandler {\\n\\tnonGoRestfulMux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux(name)\\n\\tif notFoundHandler != nil {\\n\\t\\tnonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(notFoundHandler)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tgorestfulContainer := restful.NewContainer()\\n\\tgorestfulContainer.Router(restful.CurlyRouter{}) // e.g. for proxy/{kind}/{name}/{*}\\n\\tgorestfulContainer.RecoverHandler(func(panicReason interface{}, httpWriter http.ResponseWriter) {\\n\\t\\tlogStackOnRecover(s, panicReason, httpWriter)\\n\\t})\\n\\tgorestfulContainer.ServiceErrorHandler(func(serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, response *restful.Response) {\\n\\t\\tserviceErrorHandler(s, serviceErr, request, response)\\n\\t})\\n\\n\\tdirector := director{\\n\\t\\tname:               name,\\n\\t\\tgoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\\n\\t\\tnonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\treturn &APIServerHandler{\\n\\t\\tFullHandlerChain:   handlerChainBuilder(director),\\n\\t\\tGoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\\n\\t\\tNonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\\n\\t\\tDirector:           director,\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\n// ListedPaths returns the paths that should be shown under /\\nfunc (a *APIServerHandler) ListedPaths() []string {\\n\\tvar handledPaths []string\\n\\t// Extract the paths handled using restful.WebService\\n\\tfor _, ws := range a.GoRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\\n\\t\\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, ws.RootPath())\\n\\t}\\n\\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, a.NonGoRestfulMux.ListedPaths()...)\\n\\tsort.Strings(handledPaths)\\n\\n\\treturn handledPaths\\n}\\n\\ntype director struct {\\n\\tname               string\\n\\tgoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\\n\\tnonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux\\n}\\n\\nfunc (d director) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n\\tpath := req.URL.Path\\n\\n\\t// check to see if our webservices want to claim this path\\n\\tfor _, ws := range d.goRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\\n\\t\\tswitch {\\n\\t\\tcase ws.RootPath() == \\\"/apis\\\":\\n\\t\\t\\t// if we are exactly /apis or /apis/, then we need special handling in loop.\\n\\t\\t\\t// normally these are passed to the nonGoRestfulMux, but if discovery is enabled, it will go directly.\\n\\t\\t\\t// We can't rely on a prefix match since /apis matches everything (see the big comment on Director above)\\n\\t\\t\\tif path == \\\"/apis\\\" || path == \\\"/apis/\\\" {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tklog.V(5).Infof(\\\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\\\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\\n\\t\\t\\t\\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\tcase strings.HasPrefix(path, ws.RootPath()):\\n\\t\\t\\t// ensure an exact match or a path boundary match\\n\\t\\t\\tif len(path) == len(ws.RootPath()) || path[len(ws.RootPath())] == '/' {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tklog.V(5).Infof(\\\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\\\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\\n\\t\\t\\t\\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// if we didn't find a match, then we just skip gorestful altogether\\n\\tklog.V(5).Infof(\\\"%v: %v %q satisfied by nonGoRestful\\\", d.name, req.Method, path)\\n\\td.nonGoRestfulMux.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n}\\n\\n// TODO: Unify with RecoverPanics?\\nfunc logStackOnRecover(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, panicReason interface{}, w http.ResponseWriter) {\\n\\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\\n\\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"recover from panic situation: - %v\\\\r\\\\n\\\", panicReason))\\n\\tfor i := 2; ; i++ {\\n\\t\\t_, file, line, ok := rt.Caller(i)\\n\\t\\tif !ok {\\n\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"    %s:%d\\\\r\\\\n\\\", file, line))\\n\\t}\\n\\tklog.Errorln(buffer.String())\\n\\n\\theaders := http.Header{}\\n\\tif ct := w.Header().Get(\\\"Content-Type\\\"); len(ct) > 0 {\\n\\t\\theaders.Set(\\\"Accept\\\", ct)\\n\\t}\\n\\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusInternalServerError, \\\"\\\", schema.GroupResource{}, \\\"\\\", \\\"\\\", 0, false), s, schema.GroupVersion{}, w, &http.Request{Header: headers})\\n}\\n\\nfunc serviceErrorHandler(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, resp *restful.Response) {\\n\\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(\\n\\t\\tapierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(serviceErr.Code, \\\"\\\", schema.GroupResource{}, \\\"\\\", serviceErr.Message, 0, false),\\n\\t\\ts,\\n\\t\\tschema.GroupVersion{},\\n\\t\\tresp,\\n\\t\\trequest.Request,\\n\\t)\\n}\\n\\n// ServeHTTP makes it an http.Handler\\nfunc (a *APIServerHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n\\ta.FullHandlerChain.ServeHTTP(w, r)\\n}\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"e447226c-2d10-455c-a4b8-aa28877660d6\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:23.233Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"3e570bb1-667c-45b0-8ce8-5c9d156b1d6a\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"e447226c-2d10-455c-a4b8-aa28877660d6\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01UK5bXAxgohuX85yi3dQbj2\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroups(apiGroupInfos ...*APIGroupInfo) error {\\n934:func (s *GenericAPIServer) registerStorageReadinessCheck(groupName string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) {\\n952:func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroup(apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) error {\\n956:func (s *GenericAPIServer) getAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, groupVersion schema.GroupVersion, apiPrefix string) (*genericapi.APIGroupVersion, error) {\\n970:func (s *GenericAPIServer) newAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, groupVersion schema.GroupVersion) *genericapi.APIGroupVersion {\\n1008:func NewDefaultAPIGroupInfo(group string, scheme *runtime.Scheme, parameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec, codecs serializer.CodecFactory) APIGroupInfo {\\n1034:func (s *GenericAPIServer) getOpenAPIModels(apiPrefix string, apiGroupInfos ...*APIGroupInfo) (managedfields.TypeConverter, error) {\\n1068:func getResourceNamesForGroup(apiPrefix string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, pathsToIgnore openapiutil.Trie) ([]string, error) APIGroupInfo struct {\\n\\tPrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion\\n\\t// Info about the resources in this group. It's a map from version to resource to the storage.\\n\\tVersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\\n\\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the APIVersion used for common objects in the\\n\\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\\n\\t// define a version \\\"v1beta1\\\" but want to use the Kubernetes \\\"v1\\\" internal objects.\\n\\t// If nil, defaults to groupMeta.GroupVersion.\\n\\t// TODO: Remove this when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/19018 is fixed.\\n\\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n\\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \\\"meta.k8s.io/v1\\\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\\n\\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\\n\\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\\n\\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n\\n\\t// Scheme includes all of the types used by this group and how to convert between them (or\\n\\t// to convert objects from outside of this group that are accepted in this API).\\n\\t// TODO: replace with interfaces\\n\\tScheme *runtime.Scheme\\n\\t// NegotiatedSerializer controls how this group encodes and decodes data\\n\\tNegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\\n\\t// ParameterCodec performs conversions for query parameters passed to API calls\\n\\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\\n\\n\\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the definitions of all resources installed together.\\n\\t// It is set during InstallAPIGroups, InstallAPIGroup, and InstallLegacyAPIGroup.\\n\\tStaticOpenAPISpec map[string]*spec.Schema\\n}\\n\\nfunc (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {\\n\\tfor _, stores := range a.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\\n\\t\\tfor _, store := range stores {\\n\\t\\t\\tstore.Destroy()\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\n// GenericAPIServer contains state for a Kubernetes cluster api server.\\ntype GenericAPIServer struct {\\n\\t// discoveryAddresses is used to build cluster IPs for discovery.\\n\\tdiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\\n\\n\\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\\n\\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\\n\\n\\t// minRequestTimeout is how short the request timeout can be.  This is used to build the RESTHandler\\n\\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\\n\\n\\t// ShutdownTimeout is the timeout used for server shutdown. This specifies the timeout before server\\n\\t// gracefully shutdown returns.\\n\\tShutdownTimeout time.Duration\\n\\n\\t// legacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\\n\\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup\\n\\tlegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\\n\\n\\t// admissionControl is used to build the RESTStorage that backs an API Group.\\n\\tadmissionControl admission.Interface\\n\\n\\t// SecureServingInfo holds configuration of the TLS server.\\n\\tSecureServingInfo *SecureServingInfo\\n\\n\\t// ExternalAddress is the address (hostname or IP and port) that should be used in\\n\\t// external (public internet) URLs for this GenericAPIServer.\\n\\tExternalAddress string\\n\\n\\t// Serializer controls how common API objects not in a group/version prefix are serialized for this server.\\n\\t// Individual APIGroups may define their own serializers.\\n\\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\\n\\n\\t// \\\"Outputs\\\"\\n\\t// Handler holds the handlers being used by this API server\\n\\tHandler *APIServerHandler\\n\\n\\t// UnprotectedDebugSocket is used to serve pprof information in a unix-domain socket. This socket is\\n\\t// not protected by authentication/authorization.\\n\\tUnprotectedDebugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\\n\\n\\t// listedPathProvider is a lister which provides the set of paths to show at /\\n\\tlistedPathProvider routes.ListedPathProvider\\n\\n\\t// DiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an unaggregated form.\\n\\tDiscoveryGroupManager discovery.GroupManager\\n\\n\\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\\n\\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\\n\\n\\t// PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager serves /apis aggregated from all peer apiservers.\\n\\tPeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager discoveryendpoint.PeerAggregatedResourceManager\\n\\n\\t// AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager serves /api in an aggregated form.\\n\\tAggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\\n\\n\\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI if these configs are non-nil.\\n\\topenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\\n\\n\\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI V3 if these configs are non-nil.\\n\\topenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\\n\\n\\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation indicates not to install the OpenAPI handler\\n\\t// during PrepareRun.\\n\\t// Set this to true when the specific API Server has its own OpenAPI handler\\n\\t// (e.g. kube-aggregator)\\n\\tskipOpenAPIInstallation bool\\n\\n\\t// OpenAPIVersionedService controls the /openapi/v2 endpoint, and can be used to update the served spec.\\n\\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\\n\\tOpenAPIVersionedService *handler.OpenAPIService\\n\\n\\t// OpenAPIV3VersionedService controls the /openapi/v3 endpoint and can be used to update the served spec.\\n\\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\\n\\tOpenAPIV3VersionedService *handler3.OpenAPIService\\n\\n\\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the restful container endpoints.\\n\\t// It is set during PrepareRun.\\n\\tStaticOpenAPISpec *spec.Swagger\\n\\n\\t// PostStartHooks are each called after the server has started listening, in a separate go func for each\\n\\t// with no guarantee of ordering between them.  The map key is a name used for error reporting.\\n\\t// It may kill the process with a panic if it wishes to by returning an error.\\n\\tpostStartHookLock      sync.Mutex\\n\\tpostStartHooks         map[string]postStartHookEntry\\n\\tpostStartHooksCalled   bool\\n\\tdisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\\n\\n\\tpreShutdownHookLock    sync.Mutex\\n\\tpreShutdownHooks       map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\\n\\tpreShutdownHooksCalled bool\\n\\n\\t// healthz checks\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"4b6761ed-35b7-40db-a549-05e2a60f9529\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:28.686Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"2cc08146-ce34-4f19-9607-8abed0c4caac\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"4b6761ed-35b7-40db-a549-05e2a60f9529\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_015W6Y3bsgqrM627TxvLrn7z\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now '200,310p'\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":1808,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":52187,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":1808},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2wCeFFt1B29mxhyimU\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"1e88bf3f-b61e-48cf-a044-e7609bb4dde0\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:30.310Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"1e88bf3f-b61e-48cf-a044-e7609bb4dde0\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01FobG7dmZtDKpTnHS5qQc7K\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"\\t// healthz checks\\n\\thealthzRegistry healthCheckRegistry\\n\\treadyzRegistry  healthCheckRegistry\\n\\tlivezRegistry   healthCheckRegistry\\n\\n\\tlivezGracePeriod time.Duration\\n\\n\\t// auditing. The backend is started before the server starts listening.\\n\\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\\n\\n\\t// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary\\n\\t// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in\\n\\t// the create-on-update case\\n\\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\\n\\n\\t// EquivalentResourceRegistry provides information about resources equivalent to a given resource,\\n\\t// and the kind associated with a given resource. As resources are installed, they are registered here.\\n\\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\\n\\n\\t// delegationTarget is the next delegate in the chain. This is never nil.\\n\\tdelegationTarget DelegationTarget\\n\\n\\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\\n\\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\\n\\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\\n\\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\\n\\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\\n\\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\\n\\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\\n\\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\\n\\n\\t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\\n\\t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\\n\\t// but /readyz will return failure.\\n\\tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\\n\\n\\t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\\n\\t// 0 means no limit.\\n\\tmaxRequestBodyBytes int64\\n\\n\\t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\\n\\tAPIServerID string\\n\\n\\t// StorageReadinessHook implements post-start-hook functionality for checking readiness\\n\\t// of underlying storage for registered resources.\\n\\tStorageReadinessHook *StorageReadinessHook\\n\\n\\t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\\n\\tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\\n\\n\\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\\n\\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\\n\\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\\n\\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\\n\\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\\n\\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\\n\\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\\n\\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\\n\\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n\\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\\n\\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\\n\\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\\n\\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n\\n\\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\\n\\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\\n\\n\\t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals that happen during the life cycle of the apiserver.\\n\\tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\\n\\n\\t// destroyFns contains a list of functions that should be called on shutdown to clean up resources.\\n\\tdestroyFns []func()\\n\\n\\t// muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals holds signals that indicate all known HTTP paths have been registered.\\n\\t// it exists primarily to avoid returning a 404 response when a resource actually exists but we haven't installed the path to a handler.\\n\\t// it is exposed for easier composition of the individual servers.\\n\\t// the primary users of this field are the WithMuxCompleteProtection filter and the NotFoundHandler\\n\\tmuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals map[string]<-chan struct{}\\n\\n\\t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\\n\\t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\\n\\t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\\n\\t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\\n\\t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\\n\\t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\\n\\t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\\n\\tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\\n\\n\\t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\\n\\t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\\n\\t// watch request(s) to drain.\\n\\t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\\n\\t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\\n\\t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\\n\\t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\\n\\t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\\n\\t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\\n\\t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\\n\\t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\\n\\t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\\n\\t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\\n\\t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\\n\\t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\\n\\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\\n}\\n\\n// DelegationTarget is an interface which allows for composition of API servers with top level handling that works\\n// as expected.\\ntype DelegationTarget interface {\\n\\t// UnprotectedHandler returns a handler that is NOT protected by a normal chain\\n\\tUnprotectedHandler() http.Handler\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"c0b3c772-5470-4ce4-a9c3-40fbdee10f67\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:30.327Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"1e88bf3f-b61e-48cf-a044-e7609bb4dde0\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"c0b3c772-5470-4ce4-a9c3-40fbdee10f67\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01PUg3KQnMueV77CLcPRLKLo\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now
