-100\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":537,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":73943,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":537},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2xKM6NLGqex4mFoAVy\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"84f3705a-c701-4c20-8ba9-a6f49edd43ee\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:45.915Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"84f3705a-c701-4c20-8ba9-a6f49edd43ee\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01D7Tt4LPGhw8JaAYdQQYtYk\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage filters\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\\\"\\n\\tapirequest \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\\\"\\n\\tfcmetrics \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/metrics\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n)\\n\\nconst (\\n\\t// Constant for the retry-after interval on rate limiting.\\n\\tretryAfter = \\\"1\\\"\\n\\n\\t// How often inflight usage metric should be updated. Because\\n\\t// the metrics tracks maximal value over period making this\\n\\t// longer will increase the metric value.\\n\\tinflightUsageMetricUpdatePeriod = time.Second\\n)\\n\\nvar (\\n\\tnonMutatingRequestVerbs = sets.NewString(\\\"get\\\", \\\"list\\\", \\\"watch\\\")\\n\\twatchVerbs              = sets.NewString(\\\"watch\\\")\\n)\\n\\nfunc handleError(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, err error) {\\n\\terrorMsg := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Internal Server Error: %#v\\\", r.RequestURI)\\n\\thttp.Error(w, errorMsg, http.StatusInternalServerError)\\n\\tklog.Error(err.Error())\\n}\\n\\n// requestWatermark is used to track maximal numbers of requests in a particular phase of handling\\ntype requestWatermark struct {\\n\\tphase                                string\\n\\treadOnlyObserver, mutatingObserver   fcmetrics.RatioedGauge\\n\\tlock                                 sync.Mutex\\n\\treadOnlyWatermark, mutatingWatermark int\\n}\\n\\nfunc (w *requestWatermark) recordMutating(mutatingVal int) {\\n\\tw.mutatingObserver.Set(float64(mutatingVal))\\n\\n\\tw.lock.Lock()\\n\\tdefer w.lock.Unlock()\\n\\n\\tif w.mutatingWatermark < mutatingVal {\\n\\t\\tw.mutatingWatermark = mutatingVal\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\nfunc (w *requestWatermark) recordReadOnly(readOnlyVal int) {\\n\\tw.readOnlyObserver.Set(float64(readOnlyVal))\\n\\n\\tw.lock.Lock()\\n\\tdefer w.lock.Unlock()\\n\\n\\tif w.readOnlyWatermark < readOnlyVal {\\n\\t\\tw.readOnlyWatermark = readOnlyVal\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\n// watermark tracks requests being executed (not waiting in a queue)\\nvar watermark = &requestWatermark{\\n\\tphase: metrics.ExecutingPhase,\\n}\\n\\n// startWatermarkMaintenance starts the goroutines to observe and maintain the specified watermark.\\nfunc startWatermarkMaintenance(watermark *requestWatermark, stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\\n\\t// Periodically update the inflight usage metric.\\n\\tgo wait.Until(func() {\\n\\t\\twatermark.lock.Lock()\\n\\t\\treadOnlyWatermark := watermark.readOnlyWatermark\\n\\t\\tmutatingWatermark := watermark.mutatingWatermark\\n\\t\\twatermark.readOnlyWatermark = 0\\n\\t\\twatermark.mutatingWatermark = 0\\n\\t\\twatermark.lock.Unlock()\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"fab7ab0b-d04c-4d4d-b415-f518f17be426\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:45.931Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"84f3705a-c701-4c20-8ba9-a6f49edd43ee\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"fab7ab0b-d04c-4d4d-b415-f518f17be426\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01HANXT9zzYPgD99MHV9ZXny\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage handlers\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\tmetainternalversion \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion\\\"\\n\\tmetainternalversionscheme \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion/scheme\\\"\\n\\tmetainternalversionvalidation \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion/validation\\\"\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/validation\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation/field\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/finisher\\\"\\n\\trequestmetrics \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/metrics\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/apihelpers\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/dryrun\\\"\\n\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// DeleteResource returns a function that will handle a resource deletion\\n// TODO admission here becomes solely validating admission\\nfunc DeleteResource(r rest.GracefulDeleter, allowsOptions bool, scope *RequestScope, admit admission.Interface) http.HandlerFunc {\\n\\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n\\t\\tctx := req.Context()\\n\\t\\t// For performance tracking purposes.\\n\\t\\tctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, \\\"Delete\\\", traceFields(req)...)\\n\\t\\treq = req.WithContext(ctx)\\n\\t\\tdefer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)\\n\\n\\t\\tnamespace, name, err := scope.Namer.Name(req)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t// enforce a timeout of at most requestTimeoutUpperBound (34s) or less if the user-provided\\n\\t\\t// timeout inside the parent context is lower than requestTimeoutUpperBound.\\n\\t\\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, requestTimeoutUpperBound)\\n\\t\\tdefer cancel()\\n\\n\\t\\tctx = request.WithNamespace(ctx, namespace)\\n\\t\\tadmit = admission.WithAudit(admit)\\n\\n\\t\\toutputMediaType, _, err := negotiation.NegotiateOutputMediaType(req, scope.Serializer, scope)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\toptions := &metav1.DeleteOptions{}\\n\\t\\tif allowsOptions {\\n\\t\\t\\tbody, err := limitedReadBodyWithRecordMetric(ctx, req, scope.MaxRequestBodyBytes, scope.Resource.GroupResource(), requestmetrics.Delete)\\n\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"limitedReadBody failed\\\", attribute.Int(\\\"len\\\", len(body)), attribute.String(\\\"err\\\", err.Error()))\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"limitedReadBody succeeded\\\", attribute.Int(\\\"len\\\", len(body)))\\n\\t\\t\\tif len(body) > 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\ts, err := negotiation.NegotiateInputSerializer(req, false, apihelpers.GetMetaInternalVersionCodecs())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// For backwards compatibility, we need to allow existing clients to submit per group DeleteOptions\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// It is also allowed to pass a body with meta.k8s.io/v1.DeleteOptions\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tdefaultGVK := scope.MetaGroupVersion.WithKind(\\\"DeleteOptions\\\")\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tobj, gvk, err := apihelpers.GetMetaInternalVersionCodecs().DecoderToVersion(s.Serializer, defaultGVK.GroupVersion()).Decode(body, &defaultGVK, options)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tif obj != options {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(\\\"decoded object cannot be converted to DeleteOptions\\\")\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Decoded delete options\\\")\\n\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tobjGV := gvk.GroupVersion()\\n\\t\\t\\t\\taudit.LogRequestObject(req.Context(), obj, objGV, scope.Resource, scope.Subresource, apihelpers.GetMetaInternalVersionCodecs())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Recorded the audit event\\\")\\n\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tif err := metainternalversionscheme.ParameterCodec.DecodeParameters(req.URL.Query(), scope.MetaGroupVersion, options); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.AllowUnsafeMalformedObjectDeletion) && options != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\toptions.IgnoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential = nil\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif errs := validation.ValidateDeleteOptions(options); len(errs) > 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\terr := errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{Group: metav1.GroupName, Kind: \\\"DeleteOptions\\\"}, \\\"\\\", errs)\\n\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\toptions.TypeMeta.SetGroupVersionKind(metav1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind(\\\"DeleteOptions\\\"))\\n\\n\\t\\tuserInfo, _ := request.UserFrom(ctx)\\n\\t\\tstaticAdmissionAttrs := admission.NewAttributesRecord(nil, nil, scope.Kind, namespace, name, scope.Resource, scope.Subresource, admission.Delete, options, dryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun), userInfo)\\n\\n\\t\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.AllowUnsafeMalformedObjectDeletion) {\\n\\t\\t\\tif options != nil && ptr.Deref(options.IgnoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential, false) {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// let's make sure that the audit will reflect that this delete request\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// was tried with ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential enabled\\n\\t\\t\\t\\taudit.AddAuditAnnotation(ctx, \\\"apiserver.k8s.io/unsafe-delete-ignore-read-error\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tp, ok := r.(rest.CorruptObjectDeleterProvider)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tif !ok || p.GetCorruptObjDeleter() == nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// this is a developer error\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscope.err(errors.NewInternalError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"no unsafe deleter provided, can not honor ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential\\\")), w, req)\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"940cbe75-e259-4bf4-8afd-a516a98cbc41\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:47.446Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"b035dda5-2a23-42d3-a4ef-1e7178c4f0a9\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"940cbe75-e259-4bf4-8afd-a516a98cbc41\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01RKEeZ3QmyK7cJxNNbj9ieW\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now let me check the rest.go handler file:\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":2260,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":75587,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":2260},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2xb1RodnGAGkFGypyy\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"d6848315-e198-4ff0-b36e-4ebb60f0a300\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:48.907Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d6848315-e198-4ff0-b36e-4ebb60f0a300\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01RKEeZ3QmyK7cJxNNbj9ieW\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_012PTqGtkMBKGwpyvR5GuFRh\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/rest.go | head -200\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":2260,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":75587,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":2260},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2xb1RodnGAGkFGypyy\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"9a3e2d16-07fc-42fb-88e7-8de6340e34e1\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:49.241Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"9a3e2d16-07fc-42fb-88e7-8de6340e34e1\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_012PTqGtkMBKGwpyvR5GuFRh\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage handlers\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"encoding/hex\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"io\\\"\\n\\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n\\t\\\"net/url\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n\\n\\tgrpccodes \\\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\\\"\\n\\tgrpcstatus \\\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\\\"\\n\\n\\tapiequality \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\tmetav1beta1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1beta1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\\\"\\n\\trequestmetrics \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/metrics\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning\\\"\\n)\\n\\nconst (\\n\\t// 34 chose as a number close to 30 that is likely to be unique enough to jump out at me the next time I see a timeout.\\n\\t// Everyone chooses 30.\\n\\trequestTimeoutUpperBound = 34 * time.Second\\n\\t// DuplicateOwnerReferencesWarningFormat is the warning that a client receives when a create/update request contains\\n\\t// duplicate owner reference entries.\\n\\tDuplicateOwnerReferencesWarningFormat = \\\".metadata.ownerReferences contains duplicate entries; API server dedups owner references in 1.20+, and may reject such requests as early as 1.24; please fix your requests; duplicate UID(s) observed: %v\\\"\\n\\t// DuplicateOwnerReferencesAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat indicates the duplication was observed\\n\\t// after mutating admission.\\n\\t// NOTE: For CREATE and UPDATE requests the API server dedups both before and after mutating admission.\\n\\t// For PATCH request the API server only dedups after mutating admission.\\n\\tDuplicateOwnerReferencesAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat = \\\".metadata.ownerReferences contains duplicate entries after mutating admission happens; API server dedups owner references in 1.20+, and may reject such requests as early as 1.24; please fix your requests; duplicate UID(s) observed: %v\\\"\\n\\t// shortPrefix is one possible beginning of yaml unmarshal strict errors.\\n\\tshortPrefix = \\\"yaml: unmarshal errors:\\\\n\\\"\\n\\t// longPrefix is the other possible beginning of yaml unmarshal strict errors.\\n\\tlongPrefix = \\\"error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: unmarshal errors:\\\\n\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// RequestScope encapsulates common fields across all RESTful handler methods.\\ntype RequestScope struct {\\n\\tNamer ScopeNamer\\n\\n\\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\\n\\truntime.ParameterCodec\\n\\n\\t// StandardSerializers, if set, restricts which serializers can be used when\\n\\t// we aren't transforming the output (into Table or PartialObjectMetadata).\\n\\t// Used only by CRDs which do not yet support Protobuf.\\n\\tStandardSerializers []runtime.SerializerInfo\\n\\n\\tCreater         runtime.ObjectCreater\\n\\tConvertor       runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n\\tDefaulter       runtime.ObjectDefaulter\\n\\tTyper           runtime.ObjectTyper\\n\\tUnsafeConvertor runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n\\tAuthorizer      authorizer.Authorizer\\n\\n\\tEquivalentResourceMapper runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\\n\\n\\tTableConvertor rest.TableConvertor\\n\\tFieldManager   *managedfields.FieldManager\\n\\n\\tResource schema.GroupVersionResource\\n\\tKind     schema.GroupVersionKind\\n\\n\\t// AcceptsGroupVersionDelegate is an optional delegate that can be queried about whether a given GVK\\n\\t// can be accepted in create or update requests. If nil, only scope.Kind is accepted.\\n\\t// Note that this does not enable multi-version support for reads from a single endpoint.\\n\\tAcceptsGroupVersionDelegate rest.GroupVersionAcceptor\\n\\n\\tSubresource string\\n\\n\\tMetaGroupVersion schema.GroupVersion\\n\\n\\t// HubGroupVersion indicates what version objects read from etcd or incoming requests should be converted to for in-memory handling.\\n\\tHubGroupVersion schema.GroupVersion\\n\\n\\tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64\\n}\\n\\nfunc (scope *RequestScope) err(err error, w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n\\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(err, scope.Serializer, scope.Kind.GroupVersion(), w, req)\\n}\\n\\n// AcceptsGroupVersion returns true if the specified GroupVersion is allowed\\n// in create and update requests.\\nfunc (scope *RequestScope) AcceptsGroupVersion(gv schema.GroupVersion) bool {\\n\\t// If there's a custom acceptor, delegate to it. This is extremely rare.\\n\\tif scope.AcceptsGroupVersionDelegate != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn scope.AcceptsGroupVersionDelegate.AcceptsGroupVersion(gv)\\n\\t}\\n\\t// Fall back to only allowing the singular Kind. This is the typical behavior.\\n\\treturn gv == scope.Kind.GroupVersion()\\n}\\n\\nfunc (scope *RequestScope) AllowsMediaTypeTransform(mimeType, mimeSubType string, gvk *schema.GroupVersionKind) bool {\\n\\t// some handlers like CRDs can't serve all the mime types that PartialObjectMetadata or Table can - if\\n\\t// gvk is nil (no conversion) allow StandardSerializers to further restrict the set of mime types.\\n\\tif gvk == nil {\\n\\t\\tif len(scope.StandardSerializers) == 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tfor _, info := range scope.StandardSerializers {\\n\\t\\t\\tif info.MediaTypeType == mimeType && info.MediaTypeSubType == mimeSubType {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\treturn false\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// TODO: this is temporary, replace with an abstraction calculated at endpoint installation time\\n\\tif gvk.GroupVersion() == metav1beta1.SchemeGroupVersion || gvk.GroupVersion() == metav1.SchemeGroupVersion {\\n\\t\\tswitch gvk.Kind {\\n\\t\\tcase \\\"Table\\\":\\n\\t\\t\\treturn scope.TableConvertor != nil &&\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tmimeType == \\\"application\\\" &&\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t(mimeSubType == \\\"json\\\" || mimeSubType == \\\"yaml\\\")\\n\\t\\tcase \\\"PartialObjectMetadata\\\", \\\"PartialObjectMetadataList\\\":\\n\\t\\t\\t// TODO: should delineate between lists and non-list endpoints\\n\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n\\t\\tdefault:\\n\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn false\\n}\\n\\nfunc (scope *RequestScope) AllowsServerVersion(version string) bool {\\n\\treturn version == scope.MetaGroupVersion.Version\\n}\\n\\nfunc (scope *RequestScope) AllowsStreamSchema(s string) bool {\\n\\treturn s == \\\"watch\\\"\\n}\\n\\nvar _ admission.ObjectInterfaces = &RequestScope{}\\n\\nfunc (r *RequestScope) GetObjectCreater() runtime.ObjectCreater     { return r.Creater }\\nfunc (r *RequestScope) GetObjectTyper() runtime.ObjectTyper         { return r.Typer }\\nfunc (r *RequestScope) GetObjectDefaulter() runtime.ObjectDefaulter { return r.Defaulter }\\nfunc (r *RequestScope) GetObjectConvertor() runtime.ObjectConvertor { return r.Convertor }\\nfunc (r *RequestScope) GetEquivalentResourceMapper() runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper {\\n\\treturn r.EquivalentResourceMapper\\n}\\n\\n// ConnectResource returns a function that handles a connect request on a rest.Storage object.\\nfunc ConnectResource(connecter rest.Connecter, scope *RequestScope, admit admission.Interface, restPath string, isSubresource bool) http.HandlerFunc {\\n\\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n\\t\\tif isDryRun(req.URL) {\\n\\t\\t\\tscope.err(errors.NewBadRequest(\\\"dryRun is not supported\\\"), w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\tnamespace, name, err := scope.Namer.Name(req)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tctx := req.Context()\\n\\t\\tctx = request.WithNamespace(ctx, namespace)\\n\\t\\tadmit = admission.WithAudit(admit)\\n\\n\\t\\topts, subpath, subpathKey := connecter.NewConnectOptions()\\n\\t\\tif err := getRequestOptions(req, scope, opts, subpath, subpathKey, isSubresource); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\\n\\t\\t\\tscope.err(err, w, It's a map from version to resource to the storage.\\n\\tVersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\\n\\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the APIVersion used for common objects in the\\n\\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/waitgroup\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticatorfactory\\\"\\n\\tauthenticatorunion \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery/aggregated\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filterlatency\\\"\\n\\tgenericapifilters \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/impersonation\\\"\\n\\tapiopenapi \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/openapi\\\"\\n\\tapirequest \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\\\"\\n\\tgenericfeatures \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n\\tgenericregistry \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/dynamiccertificates\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/egressselector\\\"\\n\\tgenericfilters \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/flagz\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routine\\\"\\n\\tserverstore \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage\\\"\\n\\tstoragevalue \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/value\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\\\"\\n\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n\\tutilflowcontrol \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol\\\"\\n\\tflowcontrolrequest \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/request\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/informers\\\"\\n\\trestclient \\\"k8s.io/client-go/rest\\\"\\n\\tbasecompatibility \\\"k8s.io/component-base/compatibility\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/logs\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/features\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/prometheus/slis\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n\\topenapicommon \\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/spec3\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/clock\\\"\\n\\tutilsnet \\\"k8s.io/utils/net\\\"\\n\\n\\t// install apis\\n\\t_ \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/apiserver/install\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// hostnameFunc is a function to set the hostnameFunc of this apiserver.\\n// To be used for testing purpose only, to simulate scenarios where multiple apiservers\\n// exist. In such cases we want to ensure unique apiserver IDs which are a hash of hostnameFunc.\\nvar (\\n\\thostnameFunc = os.Hostname\\n)\\n\\nconst (\\n\\t// DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix is where the legacy APIs will be located.\\n\\tDefaultLegacyAPIPrefix = \\\"/api\\\"\\n\\n\\t// APIGroupPrefix is where non-legacy API group will be located.\\n\\tAPIGroupPrefix = \\\"/apis\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// Config is a structure used to configure a GenericAPIServer.\\n// Its members are sorted roughly in order of importance for composers.\\ntype Config struct {\\n\\t// SecureServing is required to serve https\\n\\tSecureServing *SecureServingInfo\\n\\n\\t// Authentication is the configuration for authentication\\n\\tAuthentication AuthenticationInfo\\n\\n\\t// Authorization is the configuration for authorization\\n\\tAuthorization AuthorizationInfo\\n\\n\\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\\n\\t// This is required for proper functioning of the PostStartHooks on a GenericAPIServer\\n\\t// TODO: move into SecureServing(WithLoopback) as soon as insecure serving is gone\\n\\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\\n\\n\\t// EgressSelector provides a lookup mechanism for dialing outbound connections.\\n\\t// It does so based on a EgressSelectorConfiguration which was read at startup.\\n\\tEgressSelector *egressselector.EgressSelector\\n\\n\\t// RuleResolver is required to get the list of rules that apply to a given user\\n\\t// in a given namespace\\n\\tRuleResolver authorizer.RuleResolver\\n\\t// AdmissionControl performs deep inspection of a given request (including content)\\n\\t// to set values and determine whether its allowed\\n\\tAdmissionControl      admission.Interface\\n\\tCorsAllowedOriginList []string\\n\\tHSTSDirectives        []string\\n\\t// FlowControl, if not nil, gives priority and fairness to request handling\\n\\tFlowControl utilflowcontrol.Interface\\n\\n\\tEnableIndex     bool\\n\\tEnableProfiling bool\\n\\tDebugSocketPath string\\n\\tEnableDiscovery bool\\n\\n\\t// Requires generic profiling enabled\\n\\tEnableContentionProfiling bool\\n\\tEnableMetrics             bool\\n\\n\\tDisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\\n\\t// done values in this values for this map are ignored.\\n\\tPostStartHooks map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry\\n\\n\\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\\n\\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\\n\\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\\n\\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\\n\\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\\n\\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\\n\\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\\n\\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\\n\\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n\\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\\n\\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\\n\\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\\n\\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n\\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\\n\\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\\n\\t// AuditBackend is where audit events are sent to.\\n\\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\\n\\t// AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator makes the decision of whether and how to audit log a request.\\n\\tAuditPolicyRuleEvaluator audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator\\n\\t// ExternalAddress is the host name to use for external (public internet) facing URLs (e.g. Swagger)\\n\\t// Will default to a value based on secure serving info and available ipv4 IPs.\\n\\tExternalAddress string\\n\\n\\t// TracerProvider can provide a tracer, which records spans for distributed tracing.\\n\\tTracerProvider tracing.TracerProvider\\n\\n\\t//===========================================================================\\n\\t// Fields you probably don't care about changing\\n\\t//===========================================================================\\n\\n\\t// BuildHandlerChainFunc allows you to build custom handler chains by decorating the apiHandler.\\n\\tBuildHandlerChainFunc func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) (secure http.Handler)\\n\\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\\n\\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\\n\\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\\n\\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\\n\\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\\n\\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\\n\\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\\n\\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\\n\\t// DiscoveryAddresses is used to build the IPs pass to discovery. If nil, the ExternalAddress is\\n\\t// always reported\\n\\tDiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\\n\\t// The default set of healthz checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\\n\\tHealthzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\\n\\t// The default set of livez checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"d7a3c28e-c3f2-46e9-8823-6f523f65d0b5\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:21.992Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"f1c8026c-84bc-4d6b-845b-63be4f77947a\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d7a3c28e-c3f2-46e9-8823-6f523f65d0b5\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01VaCiR9ygHmGdjREzQPEtzm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":2911,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":43441,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":2911},\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2vi3J82so8zxD9NppG\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"3e570bb1-667c-45b0-8ce8-5c9d156b1d6a\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:23.219Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"3e570bb1-667c-45b0-8ce8-5c9d156b1d6a\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01CBBjm62mMjfaT9QH2XBupB\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage server\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"bytes\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n\\trt \\\"runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n\\n\\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/mux\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// APIServerHandlers holds the different http.Handlers used by the API server.\\n// This includes the full handler chain, the director (which chooses between gorestful and nonGoRestful,\\n// the gorestful handler (used for the API) which falls through to the nonGoRestful handler on unregistered paths,\\n// and the nonGoRestful handler (which can contain a fallthrough of its own)\\n// FullHandlerChain -> Director -> {GoRestfulContainer,NonGoRestfulMux} based on inspection of registered web services\\ntype APIServerHandler struct {\\n\\t// FullHandlerChain is the one that is eventually served with.  It should include the full filter\\n\\t// chain and then call the Director.\\n\\tFullHandlerChain http.Handler\\n\\t// The registered APIs.  InstallAPIs uses this.  Other servers probably shouldn't access this directly.\\n\\tGoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\\n\\t// NonGoRestfulMux is the final HTTP handler in the chain.\\n\\t// It comes after all filters and the API handling\\n\\t// This is where other servers can attach handler to various parts of the chain.\\n\\tNonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux\\n\\n\\t// Director is here so that we can properly handle fall through and proxy cases.\\n\\t// This looks a bit bonkers, but here's what's happening.  We need to have /apis handling registered in gorestful in order to have\\n\\t// swagger generated for compatibility.  Doing that with `/apis` as a webservice, means that it forcibly 404s (no defaulting allowed)\\n\\t// all requests which are not /apis or /apis/.  We need those calls to fall through behind gorestful for proper delegation.  Trying to\\n\\t// register for a pattern which includes everything behind it doesn't work because gorestful negotiates for verbs and content encoding\\n\\t// and all those things go crazy when gorestful really just needs to pass through.  In addition, openapi enforces unique verb constraints\\n\\t// which we don't fit into and it still muddies up swagger.  Trying to switch the webservices into a route doesn't work because the\\n\\t//  containing webservice faces all the same problems listed above.\\n\\t// This leads to the crazy thing done here.  Our mux does what we need, so we'll place it in front of gorestful.  It will introspect to\\n\\t// decide if the route is likely to be handled by gorestful and route there if needed.  Otherwise, it goes to NonGoRestfulMux mux in\\n\\t// order to handle \\\"normal\\\" paths and delegation. Hopefully no API consumers will ever have to deal with this level of detail.  I think\\n\\t// we should consider completely removing gorestful.\\n\\t// Other servers should only use this opaquely to delegate to an API server.\\n\\tDirector http.Handler\\n}\\n\\n// HandlerChainBuilderFn is used to wrap the GoRestfulContainer handler using the provided handler chain.\\n// It is normally used to apply filtering like authentication and authorization\\ntype HandlerChainBuilderFn func(apiHandler http.Handler) http.Handler\\n\\nfunc NewAPIServerHandler(name string, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, handlerChainBuilder HandlerChainBuilderFn, notFoundHandler http.Handler) *APIServerHandler {\\n\\tnonGoRestfulMux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux(name)\\n\\tif notFoundHandler != nil {\\n\\t\\tnonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(notFoundHandler)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tgorestfulContainer := restful.NewContainer()\\n\\tgorestfulContainer.Router(restful.CurlyRouter{}) // e.g. for proxy/{kind}/{name}/{*}\\n\\tgorestfulContainer.RecoverHandler(func(panicReason interface{}, httpWriter http.ResponseWriter) {\\n\\t\\tlogStackOnRecover(s, panicReason, httpWriter)\\n\\t})\\n\\tgorestfulContainer.ServiceErrorHandler(func(serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, response *restful.Response) {\\n\\t\\tserviceErrorHandler(s, serviceErr, request, response)\\n\\t})\\n\\n\\tdirector := director{\\n\\t\\tname:               name,\\n\\t\\tgoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\\n\\t\\tnonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\treturn &APIServerHandler{\\n\\t\\tFullHandlerChain:   handlerChainBuilder(director),\\n\\t\\tGoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\\n\\t\\tNonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\\n\\t\\tDirector:           director,\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\n// ListedPaths returns the paths that should be shown under /\\nfunc (a *APIServerHandler) ListedPaths() []string {\\n\\tvar handledPaths []string\\n\\t// Extract the paths handled using restful.WebService\\n\\tfor _, ws := range a.GoRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\\n\\t\\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, ws.RootPath())\\n\\t}\\n\\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, a.NonGoRestfulMux.ListedPaths()...)\\n\\tsort.Strings(handledPaths)\\n\\n\\treturn handledPaths\\n}\\n\\ntype director struct {\\n\\tname               string\\n\\tgoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\\n\\tnonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux\\n}\\n\\nfunc (d director) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n\\tpath := req.URL.Path\\n\\n\\t// check to see if our webservices want to claim this path\\n\\tfor _, ws := range d.goRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\\n\\t\\tswitch {\\n\\t\\tcase ws.RootPath() == \\\"/apis\\\":\\n\\t\\t\\t// if we are exactly /apis or /apis/, then we need special handling in loop.\\n\\t\\t\\t// normally these are passed to the nonGoRestfulMux, but if discovery is enabled, it will go directly.\\n\\t\\t\\t// We can't rely on a prefix match since /apis matches everything (see the big comment on Director above)\\n\\t\\t\\tif path == \\\"/apis\\\" || path == \\\"/apis/\\\" {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tklog.V(5).Infof(\\\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\\\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\\n\\t\\t\\t\\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\tcase strings.HasPrefix(path, ws.RootPath()):\\n\\t\\t\\t// ensure an exact match or a path boundary match\\n\\t\\t\\tif len(path) == len(ws.RootPath()) || path[len(ws.RootPath())] == '/' {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tklog.V(5).Infof(\\\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\\\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\\n\\t\\t\\t\\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// if we didn't find a match, then we just skip gorestful altogether\\n\\tklog.V(5).Infof(\\\"%v: %v %q satisfied by nonGoRestful\\\", d.name, req.Method, path)\\n\\td.nonGoRestfulMux.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n}\\n\\n// TODO: Unify with RecoverPanics?\\nfunc logStackOnRecover(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, panicReason interface{}, w http.ResponseWriter) {\\n\\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\\n\\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"recover from panic situation: - %v\\\\r\\\\n\\\", panicReason))\\n\\tfor i := 2; ; i++ {\\n\\t\\t_, file, line, ok := rt.Caller(i)\\n\\t\\tif !ok {\\n\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"    %s:%d\\\\r\\\\n\\\", file, line))\\n\\t}\\n\\tklog.Errorln(buffer.String())\\n\\n\\theaders := http.Header{}\\n\\tif ct := w.Header().Get(\\\"Content-Type\\\"); len(ct) > 0 {\\n\\t\\theaders.Set(\\\"Accept\\\", ct)\\n\\t}\\n\\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusInternalServerError, \\\"\\\", schema.GroupResource{}, \\\"\\\", \\\"\\\", 0, false), s, schema.GroupVersion{}, w, &http.Request{Header: headers})\\n}\\n\\nfunc serviceErrorHandler(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, resp *restful.Response) {\\n\\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(\\n\\t\\tapierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(serviceErr.Code, \\\"\\\", schema.GroupResource{}, \\\"\\\", serviceErr.Message, 0, false),\\n\\t\\ts,\\n\\t\\tschema.GroupVersion{},\\n\\t\\tresp,\\n\\t\\trequest.Request,\\n\\t)\\n}\\n\\n// ServeHTTP makes it an http.Handler\\nfunc (a *APIServerHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n\\ta.FullHandlerChain.ServeHTTP(w, r)\\n}\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"e447226c-2d10-455c-a4b8-aa28877660d6\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:23.233Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"3e570bb1-667c-45b0-8ce8-5c9d156b1d6a\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"e447226c-2d10-455c-a4b8-aa28877660d6\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01UK5bXAxgohuX85yi3dQbj2\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now let me check the filters APIGroupInfo struct {\\n\\tPrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion\\n\\t// Info about the resources in this group. It's a map from version to resource to the storage.\\n\\tVersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\\n\\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the APIVersion used for common objects in the\\n\\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\\n\\t// define a version \\\"v1beta1\\\" but want to use the Kubernetes \\\"v1\\\" internal objects.\\n\\t// If nil, defaults to groupMeta.GroupVersion.\\n\\t// TODO: Remove this when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/19018 is fixed.\\n\\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n\\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \\\"meta.k8s.io/v1\\\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\\n\\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\\n\\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\\n\\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n\\n\\t// Scheme includes all of the types used by this group and how to convert between them (or\\n\\t// to convert objects from outside of this group that are accepted in this API).\\n\\t// TODO: replace with interfaces\\n\\tScheme *runtime.Scheme\\n\\t// NegotiatedSerializer controls how this group encodes and decodes data\\n\\tNegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\\n\\t// ParameterCodec performs conversions for query parameters passed to API calls\\n\\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\\n\\n\\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the definitions of all resources installed together.\\n\\t// It is set during InstallAPIGroups, InstallAPIGroup, and InstallLegacyAPIGroup.\\n\\tStaticOpenAPISpec map[string]*spec.Schema\\n}\\n\\nfunc (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {\\n\\tfor _, stores := range a.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\\n\\t\\tfor _, store := range stores {\\n\\t\\t\\tstore.Destroy()\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\n// GenericAPIServer contains state for a Kubernetes cluster api server.\\ntype GenericAPIServer struct {\\n\\t// discoveryAddresses is used to build cluster IPs for discovery.\\n\\tdiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\\n\\n\\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\\n\\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\\n\\n\\t// minRequestTimeout is how short the request timeout can be.  This is used to build the RESTHandler\\n\\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\\n\\n\\t// ShutdownTimeout is the timeout used for server shutdown. This specifies the timeout before server\\n\\t// gracefully shutdown returns.\\n\\tShutdownTimeout time.Duration\\n\\n\\t// legacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\\n\\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup\\n\\tlegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\\n\\n\\t// admissionControl is used to build the RESTStorage that backs an API Group.\\n\\tadmissionControl admission.Interface\\n\\n\\t// SecureServingInfo holds configuration of the TLS server.\\n\\tSecureServingInfo *SecureServingInfo\\n\\n\\t// ExternalAddress is the address (hostname or IP and port) that should be used in\\n\\t// external (public internet) URLs for this GenericAPIServer.\\n\\tExternalAddress string\\n\\n\\t// Serializer controls how common API objects not in a group/version prefix are serialized for this server.\\n\\t// Individual APIGroups may define their own serializers.\\n\\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\\n\\n\\t// \\\"Outputs\\\"\\n\\t// Handler holds the handlers being used by this API server\\n\\tHandler *APIServerHandler\\n\\n\\t// UnprotectedDebugSocket is used to serve pprof information in a unix-domain socket. This socket is\\n\\t// not protected by authentication/authorization.\\n\\tUnprotectedDebugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\\n\\n\\t// listedPathProvider is a lister which provides the set of paths to show at /\\n\\tlistedPathProvider routes.ListedPathProvider\\n\\n\\t// DiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an unaggregated form.\\n\\tDiscoveryGroupManager discovery.GroupManager\\n\\n\\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\\n\\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\\n\\n\\t// PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager serves /apis aggregated from all peer apiservers.\\n\\tPeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager discoveryendpoint.PeerAggregatedResourceManager\\n\\n\\t// AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager serves /api in an aggregated form.\\n\\tAggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\\n\\n\\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI if these configs are non-nil.\\n\\topenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\\n\\n\\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI V3 if these configs are non-nil.\\n\\topenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\\n\\n\\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation indicates not to install the OpenAPI handler\\n\\t// during PrepareRun.\\n\\t// Set this to true when the specific API Server has its own OpenAPI handler\\n\\t// (e.g. kube-aggregator)\\n\\tskipOpenAPIInstallation bool\\n\\n\\t// OpenAPIVersionedService controls the /openapi/v2 endpoint, and can be used to update the served spec.\\n\\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\\n\\tOpenAPIVersionedService *handler.OpenAPIService\\n\\n\\t// OpenAPIV3VersionedService controls the /openapi/v3 endpoint and can be used to update the served spec.\\n\\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\\n\\tOpenAPIV3VersionedService *handler3.OpenAPIService\\n\\n\\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the restful container endpoints.\\n\\t// It is set during PrepareRun.\\n\\tStaticOpenAPISpec *spec.Swagger\\n\\n\\t// PostStartHooks are each called after the server has started listening, in a separate go func for each\\n\\t// with no guarantee of ordering between them.  The map key is a name used for error reporting.\\n\\t// It may kill the process with a panic if it wishes to by returning an error.\\n\\tpostStartHookLock      sync.Mutex\\n\\tpostStartHooks         map[string]postStartHookEntry\\n\\tpostStartHooksCalled   bool\\n\\tdisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\\n\\n\\tpreShutdownHookLock    sync.Mutex\\n\\tpreShutdownHooks       map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\\n\\tpreShutdownHooksCalled bool\\n\\n\\t// healthz checks\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"4b6761ed-35b7-40db-a549-05e2a60f9529\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:28.686Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"2cc08146-ce34-4f19-9607-8abed0c4caac\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"4b6761ed-35b7-40db-a549-05e2a60f9529\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_015W6Y3bsgqrM627TxvLrn7z\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now healthz checks\\n\\thealthzRegistry healthCheckRegistry\\n\\treadyzRegistry  healthCheckRegistry\\n\\tlivezRegistry   healthCheckRegistry\\n\\n\\tlivezGracePeriod time.Duration\\n\\n\\t// auditing. The backend is started before the server starts listening.\\n\\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\\n\\n\\t// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary\\n\\t// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in\\n\\t// the create-on-update case\\n\\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\\n\\n\\t// EquivalentResourceRegistry provides information about resources equivalent to a given resource,\\n\\t// and the kind associated with a given resource. As resources are installed, they are registered here.\\n\\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\\n\\n\\t// delegationTarget is the next delegate in the chain. This is never nil.\\n\\tdelegationTarget DelegationTarget\\n\\n\\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\\n\\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\\n\\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\\n\\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\\n\\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\\n\\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\\n\\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\\n\\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\\n\\n\\t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\\n\\t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\\n\\t// but /readyz will return failure.\\n\\tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\\n\\n\\t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\\n\\t// 0 means no limit.\\n\\tmaxRequestBodyBytes int64\\n\\n\\t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\\n\\tAPIServerID string\\n\\n\\t// StorageReadinessHook implements post-start-hook functionality for checking readiness\\n\\t// of underlying storage for registered resources.\\n\\tStorageReadinessHook *StorageReadinessHook\\n\\n\\t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\\n\\tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\\n\\n\\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\\n\\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\\n\\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\\n\\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\\n\\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\\n\\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\\n\\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\\n\\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\\n\\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n\\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\\n\\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\\n\\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\\n\\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n\\n\\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\\n\\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\\n\\n\\t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals that happen during the life cycle of the apiserver.\\n\\tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\\n\\n\\t// destroyFns contains a list of functions that should be called on shutdown to clean up resources.\\n\\tdestroyFns []func()\\n\\n\\t// muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals holds signals that indicate all known HTTP paths have been registered.\\n\\t// it exists primarily to avoid returning a 404 response when a resource actually exists but we haven't installed the path to a handler.\\n\\t// it is exposed for easier composition of the individual servers.\\n\\t// the primary users of this field are the WithMuxCompleteProtection filter and the NotFoundHandler\\n\\tmuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals map[string]<-chan struct{}\\n\\n\\t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\\n\\t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\\n\\t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\\n\\t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\\n\\t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\\n\\t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\\n\\t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\\n\\tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\\n\\n\\t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\\n\\t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\\n\\t// watch request(s) to drain.\\n\\t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\\n\\t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\\n\\t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\\n\\t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\\n\\t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\\n\\t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\\n\\t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\\n\\t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\\n\\t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\\n\\t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\\n\\t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\\n\\t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\\n\\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\\n}\\n\\n// DelegationTarget is an interface which allows for composition of API servers with top level handling that works\\n// as expected.\\ntype DelegationTarget interface {\\n\\t// UnprotectedHandler returns a handler that is NOT protected by a normal chain\\n\\tUnprotectedHandler() http.Handler\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"c0b3c772-5470-4ce4-a9c3-40fbdee10f67\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:30.327Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"1e88bf3f-b61e-48cf-a044-e7609bb4dde0\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"c0b3c772-5470-4ce4-a9c3-40fbdee10f67\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01PUg3KQnMueV77CLcPRLKLo\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now (s *GenericAPIServer) PrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer {\\n\\ts.delegationTarget.PrepareRun()\\n\\n\\tif s.openAPIConfig != nil && !s.skipOpenAPIInstallation {\\n\\t\\ts.OpenAPIVersionedService, s.StaticOpenAPISpec = routes.OpenAPI{\\n\\t\\t\\tConfig: s.openAPIConfig,\\n\\t\\t}.InstallV2(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif s.openAPIV3Config != nil && !s.skipOpenAPIInstallation {\\n\\t\\ts.OpenAPIV3VersionedService = routes.OpenAPI{\\n\\t\\t\\tV3Config: s.openAPIV3Config,\\n\\t\\t}.InstallV3(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\ts.installHealthz()\\n\\ts.installLivez()\\n\\n\\t// as soon as shutdown is initiated, readiness should start failing\\n\\treadinessStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated.Signaled()\\n\\terr := s.addReadyzShutdownCheck(readinessStopCh)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\tklog.Errorf(\\\"Failed to install readyz shutdown check %s\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\ts.installReadyz()\\n\\n\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentStatusz) {\\n\\t\\tstatusz.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, \\\"apiserver\\\", statusz.NewRegistry(s.EffectiveVersion, statusz.WithListedPaths(s.ListedPaths())))\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\treturn preparedGenericAPIServer{s}\\n}\\n\\n// Run spawns the secure http server. It only returns if stopCh is closed\\n// or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\\n//\\n// Deprecated: use RunWithContext instead. Run will not get removed to avoid\\n// breaking consumers, but should not be used in new code.\\nfunc (s preparedGenericAPIServer) Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error {\\n\\tctx := wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh)\\n\\treturn s.RunWithContext(ctx)\\n}\\n\\n// RunWithContext spawns the secure http server. It only returns if ctx is canceled\\n// or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\\n// This is the diagram of what contexts/channels/signals are dependent on each other:\\n//\\n// |                                   ctx\\n// |                                    |\\n// |           ---------------------------------------------------------\\n// |           |                                                       |\\n// |    ShutdownInitiated (shutdownInitiatedCh)                        |\\n// |           |                                                       |\\n// | (ShutdownDelayDuration)                                    (PreShutdownHooks)\\n// |           |                                                       |\\n// |  AfterShutdownDelayDuration (delayedStopCh)   PreShutdownHooksStopped (preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh)\\n// |           |                                                       |\\n// |           |-------------------------------------------------------|\\n// |                                    |\\n// |                                    |\\n// |               NotAcceptingNewRequest (notAcceptingNewRequestCh)\\n// |                                    |\\n// |                                    |\\n// |           |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\\n// |           |                        |              |                                          |\\n// |        [without                 [with             |                                          |\\n// | ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  |                                          |\\n// |           |                        |              |                                          |\\n// |           |                        ---------------|                                          |\\n// |           |                                       |                                          |\\n// |           |                      |----------------|-----------------------|                  |\\n// |           |                      |                                        |                  |\\n// |           |         (NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   (WatchRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   |\\n// |           |                      |                                        |                  |\\n// |           |                      |------------------|---------------------|                  |\\n// |           |                                         |                                        |\\n// |           |                         InFlightRequestsDrained (drainedCh)                      |\\n// |           |                                         |                                        |\\n// |           |-------------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------|\\n// |                               |                     |\\n// |                       stopHttpServerCtx     (AuditBackend::Shutdown())\\n// |                               |\\n// |                       listenerStoppedCh\\n// |                               |\\n// |      HTTPServerStoppedListening (httpServerStoppedListeningCh)\\nfunc (s preparedGenericAPIServer) RunWithContext(ctx context.Context) error {\\n\\tstopCh := ctx.Done()\\n\\tdelayedStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.AfterShutdownDelayDuration\\n\\tshutdownInitiatedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated\\n\\n\\t// Clean up resources on shutdown.\\n\\tdefer s.Destroy()\\n\\n\\t// If UDS profiling is enabled, start a local http server listening on that socket\\n\\tif s.UnprotectedDebugSocket != nil {\\n\\t\\tgo func() {\\n\\t\\t\\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrashWithContext(ctx)\\n\\t\\t\\tklog.Error(s.UnprotectedDebugSocket.RunWithContext(ctx))\\n\\t\\t}()\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// spawn a new goroutine for closing the MuxAndDiscoveryComplete signal\\n\\t// registration happens during construction of the generic api server\\n\\t// the last server in the chain aggregates signals from the previous instances\\n\\tgo func() {\\n\\t\\tfor _, muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal := range s.GenericAPIServer.MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() {\\n\\t\\t\\tselect {\\n\\t\\t\\tcase <-muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal:\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n\\t\\t\\tcase <-stopCh:\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tklog.V(1).Infof(\\\"haven't completed %s, stop requested\\\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"828bb749-0471-4f56-b570-972a3729c3e6\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:31.727Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"18842c56-fb25-44da-99c4-eee10262b6ad\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"828bb749-0471-4f56-b570-972a3729c3e6\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01ERCX8FZUgCdMSRDucsE7ab\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let '550,700p'\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":1633,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":55757,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":1633},\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2wRh4kmWg4skAgLMVT\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"d56a594d-14f0-4bfd-a490-994569a8de02\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:33.495Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d56a594d-14f0-4bfd-a490-994569a8de02\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01NvmTo9xkUUBbGoPKuAiPKY\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"\\t\\t\\t\\tklog.V(1).Infof(\\\"haven't completed %s, stop requested\\\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\ts.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signal()\\n\\t\\tklog.V(1).Infof(\\\"%s has all endpoints registered and discovery information is complete\\\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\\n\\t}()\\n\\n\\tgo func() {\\n\\t\\tdefer delayedStopCh.Signal()\\n\\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", delayedStopCh.Name())\\n\\n\\t\\t<-stopCh\\n\\n\\t\\t// As soon as shutdown is initiated, /readyz should start returning failure.\\n\\t\\t// This gives the load balancer a window defined by ShutdownDelayDuration to detect that /readyz is red\\n\\t\\t// and stop sending traffic to this server.\\n\\t\\tshutdownInitiatedCh.Signal()\\n\\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", shutdownInitiatedCh.Name())\\n\\n\\t\\ttime.Sleep(s.ShutdownDelayDuration)\\n\\t}()\\n\\n\\t// close socket after delayed stopCh\\n\\tshutdownTimeout := s.ShutdownTimeout\\n\\tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\\n\\t\\t// when this mode is enabled, we do the following:\\n\\t\\t// - the server will continue to listen until all existing requests in flight\\n\\t\\t//   (not including active long running requests) have been drained.\\n\\t\\t// - once drained, http Server Shutdown is invoked with a timeout of 2s,\\n\\t\\t//   net/http waits for 1s for the peer to respond to a GO_AWAY frame, so\\n\\t\\t//   we should wait for a minimum of 2s\\n\\t\\tshutdownTimeout = 2 * time.Second\\n\\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] using HTTP Server shutdown timeout\\\", \\\"shutdownTimeout\\\", shutdownTimeout)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tnotAcceptingNewRequestCh := s.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest\\n\\tdrainedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.InFlightRequestsDrained\\n\\t// Canceling the parent context does not immediately cancel the HTTP server.\\n\\t// We only inherit context values here and deal with cancellation ourselves.\\n\\tstopHTTPServerCtx, stopHTTPServer := context.WithCancelCause(context.WithoutCancel(ctx))\\n\\tgo func() {\\n\\t\\tdefer stopHTTPServer(errors.New(\\\"time to stop HTTP server\\\"))\\n\\n\\t\\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh := notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\\n\\t\\tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\\n\\t\\t\\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh = drainedCh.Signaled()\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t<-timeToStopHttpServerCh\\n\\t}()\\n\\n\\t// Start the audit backend before any request comes in. This means we must call Backend.Run\\n\\t// before http server start serving. Otherwise the Backend.ProcessEvents call might block.\\n\\t// AuditBackend.Run will stop as soon as all in-flight requests are drained.\\n\\tif s.AuditBackend != nil {\\n\\t\\tif err := s.AuditBackend.Run(drainedCh.Signaled()); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to run the audit backend: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tstoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err := s.NonBlockingRunWithContext(stopHTTPServerCtx, shutdownTimeout)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\thttpServerStoppedListeningCh := s.lifecycleSignals.HTTPServerStoppedListening\\n\\tgo func() {\\n\\t\\t<-listenerStoppedCh\\n\\t\\thttpServerStoppedListeningCh.Signal()\\n\\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", httpServerStoppedListeningCh.Name())\\n\\t}()\\n\\n\\t// we don't accept new request as soon as both ShutdownDelayDuration has\\n\\t// elapsed and preshutdown hooks have completed.\\n\\tpreShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.PreShutdownHooksStopped\\n\\tgo func() {\\n\\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Name())\\n\\t\\tdefer notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signal()\\n\\n\\t\\t// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain\\n\\t\\t<-delayedStopCh.Signaled()\\n\\n\\t\\t// Additionally wait for preshutdown hooks to also be finished, as some of them need\\n\\t\\t// to send API calls to clean up after themselves (e.g. lease reconcilers removing\\n\\t\\t// itself from the active servers).\\n\\t\\t<-preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Signaled()\\n\\t}()\\n\\n\\t// wait for all in-flight non-long running requests to finish\\n\\tnonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})\\n\\tgo func() {\\n\\t\\tdefer close(nonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh)\\n\\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).Info(\\\"[graceful-termination] in-flight non long-running request(s) have drained\\\")\\n\\n\\t\\t// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain (it rejects everything after Wait has been called).\\n\\t\\t<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\\n\\n\\t\\t// Wait for all requests to finish, which are bounded by the RequestTimeout variable.\\n\\t\\t// once NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait is invoked, the apiserver is\\n\\t\\t// expected to reject any incoming request with a {503, Retry-After}\\n\\t\\t// response via the WithWaitGroup filter. On the contrary, we observe\\n\\t\\t// that incoming request(s) get a 'connection refused' error, this is\\n\\t\\t// because, at this point, we have called 'Server.Shutdown' and\\n\\t\\t// net/http server has stopped listening. This causes incoming\\n\\t\\t// request to get a 'connection refused' error.\\n\\t\\t// On the other hand, if 'ShutdownSendRetryAfter' is enabled incoming\\n\\t\\t// requests will be rejected with a {429, Retry-After} since\\n\\t\\t// 'Server.Shutdown' will be invoked only after in-flight requests\\n\\t\\t// have been drained.\\n\\t\\t// TODO: can we consolidate these two modes of graceful termination?\\n\\t\\ts.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait()\\n\\t}()\\n\\n\\t// wait for all in-flight watches to finish\\n\\tactiveWatchesDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})\\n\\tgo func() {\\n\\t\\tdefer close(activeWatchesDrainedCh)\\n\\n\\t\\t<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\\n\\t\\tif s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod <= time.Duration(0) {\\n\\t\\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] not going to wait for active watch request(s) to drain\\\")\\n\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t// Wait for all active watches to finish\\n\\t\\tgrace := s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod\\n\\t\\tactiveBefore, activeAfter, err := s.WatchRequestWaitGroup.Wait(func(count int) (utilwaitgroup.RateLimiter, context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {\\n\\t\\t\\tqps := float64(count) / grace.Seconds()\\n\\t\\t\\t// TODO: we don't want the QPS (max requests drained per second) to\\n\\t\\t\\t//  get below a certain floor value, since we want the server to\\n\\t\\t\\t//  drain the active watch requests as soon as possible.\\n\\t\\t\\t//  For now, it's hard coded to 200, and it is subject to change\\n\\t\\t\\t//  based on the result from the scale testing.\\n\\t\\t\\tif qps < 200 {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tqps = 200\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t\\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), grace)\\n\\t\\t\\t// We don't expect more than one token to be consumed\\n\\t\\t\\t// in a single Wait call, so setting burst to 1.\\n\\t\\t\\treturn rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(qps), 1), ctx, cancel\\n\\t\\t})\\n\\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] active watch request(s) have drained\\\",\\n\\t\\t\\t\\\"duration\\\", grace, \\\"activeWatchesBefore\\\", activeBefore, \\\"activeWatchesAfter\\\", activeAfter, \\\"error\\\", err)\\n\\t}()\\n\\n\\tgo func() {\\n\\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", drainedCh.Name())\\n\\t\\tdefer drainedCh.Signal()\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"8dd54bb2-81ab-4328-ba55-29b6176c74b5\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:33.512Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"d56a594d-14f0-4bfd-a490-994569a8de02\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"8dd54bb2-81ab-4328-ba55-29b6176c74b5\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_015bRWqE6y8ypxbnzbAMc8Px\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let me now read config.go to understand the Config structure:\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":2290,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":57390,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":2290},\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2wZNfubRiRxaNciyDL\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"1b83a0fa-b6f3-4941-9c16-fbff8c22b021\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:34.449Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"1b83a0fa-b6f3-4941-9c16-fbff8c22b021\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"ae8459c\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_015bRWqE6y8ypxbnzbAMc8Px\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_018tFV16ysqG8PWFeb1Y1oYV\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"git
