IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage storage\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n\\n\\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.\\ntype Feature = string\\n\\n// RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.\\nvar RequestWatchProgress Feature = \\\"RequestWatchProgress\\\"\\n\\n// Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response\\n// onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an\\n// object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be\\n// a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,\\n// intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of\\n// type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.\\ntype Versioner interface {\\n\\t// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object\\n\\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata\\n\\t// from database.\\n\\tUpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error\\n\\t// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object\\n\\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from\\n\\t// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client\\n\\t// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number\\n\\t// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during\\n\\t// serialization if it is set to nil.\\n\\tUpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error\\n\\t// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should\\n\\t// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.\\n\\tPrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error\\n\\t// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.\\n\\t// Should return an error if the specified object does not have a persistable version.\\n\\tObjectResourceVersion(obj runtime.Object) (uint64, error)\\n\\n\\t// ParseResourceVersion takes a resource version argument and\\n\\t// converts it to the storage backend. For watch we should pass to helper.Watch().\\n\\t// Because resourceVersion is an opaque value, the default watch\\n\\t// behavior for non-zero watch is to watch the next value (if you pass\\n\\t// \\\"1\\\", you will see updates from \\\"2\\\" onwards).\\n\\tParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) (uint64, error)\\n}\\n\\n// ResponseMeta contains information about the database metadata that is associated with\\n// an object. It abstracts the actual underlying objects to prevent coupling with concrete\\n// database and to improve testability.\\ntype ResponseMeta struct {\\n\\t// TTL is the time to live of the node that contained the returned object. It may be\\n\\t// zero or negative in some cases (objects may be expired after the requested\\n\\t// expiration time due to server lag).\\n\\tTTL int64\\n\\t// The resource version of the node that contained the returned object.\\n\\tResourceVersion uint64\\n}\\n\\n// IndexerFunc is a function that for a given object computes\\n// `<value of an index>` for a particular `<index>`.\\ntype IndexerFunc func(obj runtime.Object) string\\n\\n// IndexerFuncs is a mapping from `<index name>` to function that\\n// for a given object computes `<value for that index>`.\\ntype IndexerFuncs map[string]IndexerFunc\\n\\n// Everything accepts all objects.\\nvar Everything = SelectionPredicate{\\n\\tLabel: labels.Everything(),\\n\\tField: fields.Everything(),\\n}\\n\\n// MatchValue defines a pair (`<index name>`, `<value for that index>`).\\ntype MatchValue struct {\\n\\tIndexName string\\n\\tValue     string\\n}\\n\\n// Pass an UpdateFunc to Interface.GuaranteedUpdate to make an update\\n// that is guaranteed to succeed.\\n// See the comment for GuaranteedUpdate for more details.\\ntype UpdateFunc func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (output runtime.Object, ttl *uint64, err error)\\n\\n// ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\\n// if the hook cannot be completed. The function may NOT transform the provided\\n// object.\\n// NOTE: the object in obj may be nil if it cannot be read from the\\n// storage, due to transformation or decode error.\\ntype ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\\n\\n// ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \\\"admit everything\\\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\\nfunc ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.\\ntype Preconditions struct {\\n\\t// Specifies the target UID.\\n\\t// +optional\\n\\tUID *types.UID `json:\\\"uid,omitempty\\\"`\\n\\t// Specifies the target ResourceVersion\\n\\t// +optional\\n\\tResourceVersion *string `json:\\\"resourceVersion,omitempty\\\"`\\n}\\n\\n// NewUIDPreconditions returns a Preconditions with UID set.\\nfunc NewUIDPreconditions(uid string) *Preconditions {\\n\\tu := types.UID(uid)\\n\\treturn &Preconditions{UID: &u}\\n}\\n\\nfunc (p *Preconditions) Check(key string, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n\\tif p == nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t}\\n\\tobjMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn NewInternalError(\\n\\t\\t\\tfmt.Errorf(\\\"can't enforce preconditions %v on un-introspectable object %v, got error: %w\\\",\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t*p,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tobj,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\terr))\\n\\t}\\n\\tif p.UID != nil && *p.UID != objMeta.GetUID() {\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"80abdd2d-b9ad-4d3a-b60f-d4cae033b573\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:54.475Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"3e90b83e-d191-4e46-8113-346b387d15e4\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"80abdd2d-b9ad-4d3a-b60f-d4cae033b573\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01VdRA73GJ9sMKCQ4Juu92Gr\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Good! /tmp/interfaces.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":158,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":48886,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":158},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2ysqVCjx8vfycGb1Kj\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"78b52618-27eb-489e-98d6-4b3c81fbc507\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:06.374Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"78b52618-27eb-489e-98d6-4b3c81fbc507\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_011A6VugES6P4bRFcwYXJQZL\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage storage\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n\\n\\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.\\ntype Feature = string\\n\\n// RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.\\nvar RequestWatchProgress Feature = \\\"RequestWatchProgress\\\"\\n\\n// Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response\\n// onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an\\n// object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be\\n// a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,\\n// intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of\\n// type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.\\ntype Versioner interface {\\n\\t// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object\\n\\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata\\n\\t// from database.\\n\\tUpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error\\n\\t// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object\\n\\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from\\n\\t// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client\\n\\t// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number\\n\\t// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during\\n\\t// serialization if it is set to nil.\\n\\tUpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error\\n\\t// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should\\n\\t// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.\\n\\tPrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error\\n\\t// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.\\n\\t// Should return an error if the specified object does not have a persistable version.\\n\\tObjectResourceVersion(obj runtime.Object) (uint64, error)\\n\\n\\t// ParseResourceVersion takes a resource version argument and\\n\\t// converts it to the storage backend. For watch we should pass to helper.Watch().\\n\\t// Because resourceVersion is an opaque value, the default watch\\n\\t// behavior for non-zero watch is to watch the next value (if you pass\\n\\t// \\\"1\\\", you will see updates from \\\"2\\\" onwards).\\n\\tParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) (uint64, error)\\n}\\n\\n// ResponseMeta contains information about the database metadata that is associated with\\n// an object. It abstracts the actual underlying objects to prevent coupling with concrete\\n// database and to improve testability.\\ntype ResponseMeta struct {\\n\\t// TTL is the time to live of the node that contained the returned object. It may be\\n\\t// zero or negative in some cases (objects may be expired after the requested\\n\\t// expiration time due to server lag).\\n\\tTTL int64\\n\\t// The resource version of the node that contained the returned object.\\n\\tResourceVersion uint64\\n}\\n\\n// IndexerFunc is a function that for a given object computes\\n// `<value of an index>` for a particular `<index>`.\\ntype IndexerFunc func(obj runtime.Object) string\\n\\n// IndexerFuncs is a mapping from `<index name>` to function that\\n// for a given object computes `<value for that index>`.\\ntype IndexerFuncs map[string]IndexerFunc\\n\\n// Everything accepts all objects.\\nvar Everything = SelectionPredicate{\\n\\tLabel: labels.Everything(),\\n\\tField: fields.Everything(),\\n}\\n\\n// MatchValue defines a pair (`<index name>`, `<value for that index>`).\\ntype MatchValue struct {\\n\\tIndexName string\\n\\tValue     string\\n}\\n\\n// Pass an UpdateFunc to Interface.GuaranteedUpdate to make an update\\n// that is guaranteed to succeed.\\n// See the comment for GuaranteedUpdate for more details.\\ntype UpdateFunc func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (output runtime.Object, ttl *uint64, err error)\\n\\n// ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\\n// if the hook cannot be completed. The function may NOT transform the provided\\n// object.\\n// NOTE: the object in obj may be nil if it cannot be read from the\\n// storage, due to transformation or decode error.\\ntype ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\\n\\n// ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \\\"admit everything\\\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\\nfunc ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.\\ntype Preconditions struct {\\n\\t// Specifies the target UID.\\n\\t// +optional\\n\\tUID *types.UID `json:\\\"uid,omitempty\\\"`\\n\\t// Specifies the target ResourceVersion\\n\\t// +optional\\n\\tResourceVersion *string `json:\\\"resourceVersion,omitempty\\\"`\\n}\\n\\n// NewUIDPreconditions returns a Preconditions with UID set.\\nfunc NewUIDPreconditions(uid string) *Preconditions {\\n\\tu := types.UID(uid)\\n\\treturn &Preconditions{UID: &u}\\n}\\n\\nfunc (p *Preconditions) Check(key string, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n\\tif p == nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t}\\n\\tobjMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn NewInternalError(\\n\\t\\t\\tfmt.Errorf(\\\"can't enforce preconditions %v on un-introspectable object %v, got error: %w\\\",\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t*p,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tobj,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\terr))\\n\\t}\\n\\tif p.UID != nil && *p.UID != objMeta.GetUID() {\\n\\t\\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\\n\\t\\t\\t\\\"Precondition failed: UID in precondition: %v, UID in object meta: %v\\\",\\n\\t\\t\\t*p.UID,\\n\\t\\t\\tobjMeta.GetUID())\\n\\t\\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\\n\\t}\\n\\tif p.ResourceVersion != nil && *p.ResourceVersion != objMeta.GetResourceVersion() {\\n\\t\\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\\n\\t\\t\\t\\\"Precondition failed: ResourceVersion in precondition: %v, ResourceVersion in object meta: %v\\\",\\n\\t\\t\\t*p.ResourceVersion,\\n\\t\\t\\tobjMeta.GetResourceVersion())\\n\\t\\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// Interface offers a common interface for object marshaling/unmarshaling operations and\\n// hides all the storage-related operations behind it.\\ntype Interface interface {\\n\\t// Returns Versioner associated with this interface.\\n\\tVersioner() Versioner\\n\\n\\t// Create adds a new object at a key unless it already exists. 'ttl' is time-to-live\\n\\t// in seconds (0 means forever). If no error is returned and out is not nil, out will be\\n\\t// set to the read value from database.\\n\\tCreate(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error\\n\\n\\t// Delete removes the specified key and returns the value that existed at that spot.\\n\\t// If key didn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error.\\n\\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\\n\\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\\n\\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\\n\\tDelete(\\n\\t\\tctx context.Context, key string, out runtime.Object, preconditions *Preconditions,\\n\\t\\tvalidateDeletion ValidateObjectFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object, opts DeleteOptions) error\\n\\n\\t// Watch begins watching the specified key. Events are decoded into API objects,\\n\\t// and any items selected by 'p' are sent down to returned watch.Interface.\\n\\t// resourceVersion may be used to specify what version to begin watching,\\n\\t// which should be the current resourceVersion, and no longer rv+1\\n\\t// (e.g. reconnecting without missing any updates).\\n\\t// If resource version is \\\"0\\\", this interface will get current object at given key\\n\\t// and send it in an \\\"ADDED\\\" event, before watch starts.\\n\\tWatch(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\\n\\n\\t// Get unmarshals object found at key into objPtr. On a not found error, will either\\n\\t// return a zero object of the requested type, or an error, depending on 'opts.ignoreNotFound'.\\n\\t// Treats empty responses and nil response nodes exactly like a not found error.\\n\\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\\n\\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\\n\\tGet(ctx context.Context, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error\\n\\n\\t// GetList unmarshalls objects found at key into a *List api object (an object\\n\\t// that satisfies runtime.IsList definition).\\n\\t// If 'opts.Recursive' is false, 'key' is used as an exact match. If `opts.Recursive'\\n\\t// is true, 'key' is used as a prefix.\\n\\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\\n\\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\\n\\tGetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error\\n\\n\\t// GuaranteedUpdate keeps calling 'tryUpdate()' to update key 'key' (of type 'destination')\\n\\t// retrying the update until success if there is index conflict.\\n\\t// Note that object passed to tryUpdate may change across invocations of tryUpdate() if\\n\\t// other writers are simultaneously updating it, so tryUpdate() needs to take into account\\n\\t// the current contents of the object when deciding how the update object should look.\\n\\t// If the key doesn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error if ignoreNotFound=false\\n\\t// else `destination` will be set to the zero value of it's type.\\n\\t// If the eventual successful invocation of `tryUpdate` returns an output with the same serialized\\n\\t// contents as the input, it won't perform any update, but instead set `destination` to an object with those\\n\\t// contents.\\n\\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\\n\\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\\n\\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// Example:\\n\\t//\\n\\t// s := /* implementation of Interface */\\n\\t// err := s.GuaranteedUpdate(\\n\\t//     \\\"myKey\\\", &MyType{}, true, preconditions,\\n\\t//     func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (runtime.Object, *uint64, error) {\\n\\t//       // Before each invocation of the user defined function, \\\"input\\\" is reset to\\n\\t//       // current contents for \\\"myKey\\\" in database.\\n\\t//       curr := input.(*MyType)  // Guaranteed to succeed.\\n\\t//\\n\\t//       // Make the modification\\n\\t//       curr.Counter++\\n\\t//\\n\\t//       // Return the modified object - return an error to stop iterating. Return\\n\\t//       // a uint64 to alter the TTL on the object, or nil to keep it the same value.\\n\\t//       return cur, nil, nil\\n\\t//    }, cachedExistingObject\\n\\t// )\\n\\tGuaranteedUpdate(\\n\\t\\tctx context.Context, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,\\n\\t\\tpreconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error\\n\\n\\t// Stats returns storage stats.\\n\\tStats(ctx context.Context) (Stats, error)\\n\\n\\t// ReadinessCheck checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\\n\\tReadinessCheck() error\\n\\n\\t// RequestWatchProgress requests the a watch stream progress status be sent in the\\n\\t// watch response stream as soon as possible.\\n\\t// Used for monitor watch progress even if watching resources with no changes.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// If watch is lagging, progress status might:\\n\\t// * be pointing to stale resource version. Use etcd KV request to get linearizable resource version.\\n\\t// * not be delivered at all. It's recommended to poll request progress periodically.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// Note: Only watches with matching context grpc metadata will be notified.\\n\\t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/9325a57125e8502941d1b0c7379c4bb80a678d5c/vendor/go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/watch.go#L1037-L1042\\n\\t//\\n\\t// TODO: Remove when storage.Interface will be separate from etc3.store.\\n\\t// Deprecated: Added temporarily to simplify exposing RequestProgress for watch cache.\\n\\tRequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error\\n\\n\\t// GetCurrentResourceVersion gets the current resource version from etcd.\\n\\t// This method issues an empty list request and reads only the ResourceVersion from the object metadata\\n\\tGetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)\\n\\n\\t// EnableResourceSizeEstimation enables estimating resource size by providing function get keys from storage.\\n\\tEnableResourceSizeEstimation(KeysFunc) error\\n\\n\\t// CompactRevision returns latest observed revision that was compacted.\\n\\t// Without ListFromCacheSnapshot enabled only locally executed compaction will be observed.\\n\\t// Returns 0 if no compaction was yet observed.\\n\\tCompactRevision() int64\\n}\\n\\n// KeysFunc is a function prototype to fetch keys from storage.\\ntype KeysFunc func(context.Context) ([]string, error)\\n\\n// GetOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage get operations.\\ntype GetOptions struct {\\n\\t// IgnoreNotFound determines what is returned if the requested object is not found. If\\n\\t// true, a zero object is returned. If false, an error is returned.\\n\\tIgnoreNotFound bool\\n\\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the get operation\\n\\t// as a \\\"not older than\\\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\\n\\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\\n\\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\\n\\tResourceVersion string\\n}\\n\\n// ListOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage list operations.\\ntype ListOptions struct {\\n\\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the list operation\\n\\t// as a \\\"not older than\\\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\\n\\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\\n\\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\\n\\tResourceVersion string\\n\\t// ResourceVersionMatch provides the rule for how the resource version constraint applies. If set\\n\\t// to the default value \\\"\\\" the legacy resource version semantic apply.\\n\\tResourceVersionMatch metav1.ResourceVersionMatch\\n\\t// Predicate provides the selection rules for the list operation.\\n\\tPredicate SelectionPredicate\\n\\t// Recursive determines whether the list or watch is defined for a single object located at the\\n\\t// given key, or for the whole set of objects with the given key as a prefix.\\n\\tRecursive bool\\n\\t// ProgressNotify determines whether storage-originated bookmark (progress notify) events should\\n\\t// be delivered to the users. The option is ignored for non-watch requests.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// Firstly, note that this field is different from the Predicate.AllowWatchBookmarks field.\\n\\t// Secondly, this field is intended for internal clients only such as the watch cache.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// This means that external clients do not have the ability to set this field directly.\\n\\t// For example by setting the allowWatchBookmarks query parameter.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// The motivation for this approach is the fact that the frequency\\n\\t// of bookmark events from a storage like etcd might be very high.\\n\\t// As the number of watch requests increases, the server load would also increase.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// Furthermore, the server is not obligated to provide bookmark events at all,\\n\\t// as described in https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/956-watch-bookmark#proposal\\n\\tProgressNotify bool\\n\\t// SendInitialEvents, when set together with Watch option,\\n\\t// begin the watch stream with synthetic init events to build the\\n\\t// whole state of all resources followed by a synthetic \\\"Bookmark\\\"\\n\\t// event containing a ResourceVersion after which the server\\n\\t// continues streaming events.\\n\\tSendInitialEvents *bool\\n}\\n\\n// DeleteOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage delete operations.\\ntype DeleteOptions struct {\\n\\t// IgnoreStoreReadError, if enabled, will ignore store read error\\n\\t// such as transformation or decode failure and go ahead with the\\n\\t// deletion of the object.\\n\\t// NOTE: for normal deletion flow it should always be false, it may be\\n\\t// enabled by the caller only to facilitate unsafe deletion of corrupt\\n\\t// object which otherwise can not be deleted using the normal flow\\n\\tIgnoreStoreReadError bool\\n}\\n\\nfunc ValidateListOptions(keyPrefix string, versioner Versioner, opts ListOptions) (withRev int64, continueKey string, err error) {\\n\\tif opts.Recursive && len(opts.Predicate.Continue) > 0 {\\n\\t\\tcontinueKey, continueRV, err := DecodeContinue(opts.Predicate.Continue, keyPrefix)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn 0, \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"invalid continue token: %v\\\", err))\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif len(opts.ResourceVersion) > 0 && opts.ResourceVersion != \\\"0\\\" {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn 0, \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(\\\"specifying resource version is not allowed when using continue\\\")\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t// If continueRV > 0, the LIST request needs a specific resource version.\\n\\t\\t// continueRV==0 is invalid.\\n\\t\\t// If continueRV < 0, the request is for the latest resource version.\\n\\t\\tif continueRV > 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\twithRev = continueRV\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\treturn withRev, continueKey, nil\\n\\t}\\n\\tif len(opts.ResourceVersion) == 0 {\\n\\t\\treturn withRev, \\\"\\\", nil\\n\\t}\\n\\tparsedRV, err := versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn withRev, \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"invalid resource version: %v\\\", err))\\n\\t}\\n\\tswitch opts.ResourceVersionMatch {\\n\\tcase metav1.ResourceVersionMatchNotOlderThan:\\n\\t\\t// The not older than constraint is checked after we get a response from etcd,\\n\\t\\t// and returnedRV is then set to the revision we get from the etcd response.\\n\\tcase metav1.ResourceVersionMatchExact:\\n\\t\\twithRev = int64(parsedRV)\\n\\tcase \\\"\\\": // legacy case\\n\\t\\tif opts.Recursive && opts.Predicate.Limit > 0 && parsedRV > 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\twithRev = int64(parsedRV)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\tdefault:\\n\\t\\treturn withRev, \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"unknown ResourceVersionMatch value: %v\\\", opts.ResourceVersionMatch)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn withRev, \\\"\\\", nil\\n}\\n\\n// Stats provides statistics information about storage.\\ntype Stats struct {\\n\\t// ObjectCount informs about number of objects stored in the storage.\\n\\tObjectCount int64\\n\\t// EstimatedAverageObjectSizeBytes informs about size of objects stored in the storage, based on size of serialized values.\\n\\t// Value is an estimate, meaning it doesn't need to provide accurate nor consistent.\\n\\tEstimatedAverageObjectSizeBytes int64\\n}\\n\\nfunc PrepareKey(resourcePrefix, key string, recursive bool) (string, error) {\\n\\tif key == \\\"..\\\" ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.HasPrefix(key, \\\"../\\\") ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.HasSuffix(key, \\\"/..\\\") ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.Contains(key, \\\"/../\\\") {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid key: %q\\\", key)\\n\\t}\\n\\tif key == \\\".\\\" ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.HasPrefix(key, \\\"./\\\") ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.HasSuffix(key, \\\"/.\\\") ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.Contains(key, \\\"/./\\\") {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid key: %q\\\", key)\\n\\t}\\n\\tif key == \\\"\\\" || key == \\\"/\\\" {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"empty key: %q\\\", key)\\n\\t}\\n\\t// For recursive requests, we need to make sure the key ended with \\\"/\\\" so that we only\\n\\t// get children \\\"directories\\\". e.g. if we have key \\\"/a\\\", \\\"/a/b\\\", \\\"/ab\\\", getting keys\\n\\t// with prefix \\\"/a\\\" will return all three, while with prefix \\\"/a/\\\" will return only\\n\\t// \\\"/a/b\\\" which is the correct answer.\\n\\tif recursive && !strings.HasSuffix(key, \\\"/\\\") {\\n\\t\\tkey += \\\"/\\\"\\n\\t}\\n\\tif !strings.HasPrefix(key, resourcePrefix) {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid key: %q lacks resource prefix: %q\\\", key, resourcePrefix)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn key, nil\\n}\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"75811533-5e34-4c9f-9791-1fc39b6c04d7\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:06.386Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"78b52618-27eb-489e-98d6-4b3c81fbc507\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"75811533-5e34-4c9f-9791-1fc39b6c04d7\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01NHGaUnarc5m8TGqbDeCn3u\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Great! /tmp/store.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":5570,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":49044,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":5570},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2yyrdx7auLRZYM42G6\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"1e006ec1-2bd9-4c3a-962b-fadafe625756\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:07.491Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"1e006ec1-2bd9-4c3a-962b-fadafe625756\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_016SFGKALABrqhNKWvT6H6wv\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage etcd3\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"bytes\\\"\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"path\\\"\\n\\t\\\"reflect\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"go.etcd.io/etcd/api/v3/mvccpb\\\"\\n\\tclientv3 \\\"go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3\\\"\\n\\t\\\"go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/kubernetes\\\"\\n\\t\\\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\\\"\\n\\n\\tetcdrpc \\\"go.etcd.io/etcd/api/v3/v3rpc/rpctypes\\\"\\n\\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/metrics\\\"\\n\\tetcdfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/feature\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/value\\\"\\n\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n)\\n\\nconst (\\n\\t// maxLimit is a maximum page limit increase used when fetching objects from etcd.\\n\\t// This limit is used only for increasing page size by kube-apiserver. If request\\n\\t// specifies larger limit initially, it won't be changed.\\n\\tmaxLimit = 10000\\n)\\n\\n// authenticatedDataString satisfies the value.Context interface. It uses the key to\\n// authenticate the stored data. This does not defend against reuse of previously\\n// encrypted values under the same key, but will prevent an attacker from using an\\n// encrypted value from a different key. A stronger authenticated data segment would\\n// include the etcd3 Version field (which is incremented on each write to a key and\\n// reset when the key is deleted), but an attacker with write access to etcd can\\n// force deletion and recreation of keys to weaken that angle.\\ntype authenticatedDataString string\\n\\n// AuthenticatedData implements the value.Context interface.\\nfunc (d authenticatedDataString) AuthenticatedData() []byte {\\n\\treturn []byte(string(d))\\n}\\n\\nvar _ value.Context = authenticatedDataString(\\\"\\\")\\n\\ntype store struct {\\n\\tclient             *kubernetes.Client\\n\\tcodec              runtime.Codec\\n\\tversioner          storage.Versioner\\n\\ttransformer        value.Transformer\\n\\tpathPrefix         string\\n\\tgroupResource      schema.GroupResource\\n\\twatcher            *watcher\\n\\tleaseManager       *leaseManager\\n\\tdecoder            Decoder\\n\\tlistErrAggrFactory func() ListErrorAggregator\\n\\n\\tresourcePrefix string\\n\\tnewListFunc    func() runtime.Object\\n\\tcompactor      Compactor\\n\\n\\tcollectorMux          sync.RWMutex\\n\\tresourceSizeEstimator *resourceSizeEstimator\\n}\\n\\nvar _ storage.Interface = (*store)(nil)\\n\\nfunc (s *store) RequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error {\\n\\t// Use watchContext to match ctx metadata provided when creating the watch.\\n\\t// In best case scenario we would use the same context that watch was created, but there is no way access it from watchCache.\\n\\treturn s.client.RequestProgress(s.watchContext(ctx))\\n}\\n\\ntype objState struct {\\n\\tobj   runtime.Object\\n\\tmeta  *storage.ResponseMeta\\n\\trev   int64\\n\\tdata  []byte\\n\\tstale bool\\n}\\n\\n// ListErrorAggregator aggregates the error(s) that the LIST operation\\n// encounters while retrieving object(s) from the storage\\ntype ListErrorAggregator interface {\\n\\t// Aggregate aggregates the given error from list operation\\n\\t// key: it identifies the given object in the storage.\\n\\t// err: it represents the error the list operation encountered while\\n\\t// retrieving the given object from the storage.\\n\\t// done: true if the aggregation is done and the list operation should\\n\\t// abort, otherwise the list operation will continue\\n\\tAggregate(key string, err error) bool\\n\\n\\t// Err returns the aggregated error\\n\\tErr() error\\n}\\n\\n// defaultListErrorAggregatorFactory returns the default list error\\n// aggregator that maintains backward compatibility, which is abort\\n// the list operation as soon as it encounters the first error\\nfunc defaultListErrorAggregatorFactory() ListErrorAggregator { return &abortOnFirstError{} }\\n\\n// LIST aborts on the first error it encounters (backward compatible)\\ntype abortOnFirstError struct {\\n\\terr error\\n}\\n\\nfunc (a *abortOnFirstError) Aggregate(key string, err error) bool {\\n\\ta.err = err\\n\\treturn true\\n}\\nfunc (a *abortOnFirstError) Err() error { return a.err }\\n\\n// New returns an etcd3 implementation of storage.Interface.\\nfunc New(c *kubernetes.Client, compactor Compactor, codec runtime.Codec, newFunc, newListFunc func() runtime.Object, prefix, resourcePrefix string, groupResource schema.GroupResource, transformer value.Transformer, leaseManagerConfig LeaseManagerConfig, decoder Decoder, versioner storage.Versioner) (*store, error) {\\n\\t// for compatibility with etcd2 impl.\\n\\t// no-op for default prefix of '/registry'.\\n\\t// keeps compatibility with etcd2 impl for custom prefixes that don't start with '/'\\n\\tpathPrefix := path.Join(\\\"/\\\", prefix)\\n\\tif !strings.HasSuffix(pathPrefix, \\\"/\\\") {\\n\\t\\t// Ensure the pathPrefix ends in \\\"/\\\" here to simplify key concatenation later.\\n\\t\\tpathPrefix += \\\"/\\\"\\n\\t}\\n\\tif resourcePrefix == \\\"\\\" {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"resourcePrefix cannot be empty\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\tif resourcePrefix == \\\"/\\\" {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"resourcePrefix cannot be /\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\tif !strings.HasPrefix(resourcePrefix, \\\"/\\\") {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"resourcePrefix needs to start from /\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tlistErrAggrFactory := defaultListErrorAggregatorFactory\\n\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.AllowUnsafeMalformedObjectDeletion) {\\n\\t\\tlistErrAggrFactory = corruptObjErrAggregatorFactory(100)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tw := &watcher{\\n\\t\\tclient:        c.Client,\\n\\t\\tcodec:         codec,\\n\\t\\tnewFunc:       newFunc,\\n\\t\\tgroupResource: groupResource,\\n\\t\\tversioner:     versioner,\\n\\t\\ttransformer:   transformer,\\n\\t}\\n\\tif newFunc == nil {\\n\\t\\tw.objectType = \\\"<unknown>\\\"\\n\\t} else {\\n\\t\\tw.objectType = reflect.TypeOf(newFunc()).String()\\n\\t}\\n\\ts := &store{\\n\\t\\tclient:             c,\\n\\t\\tcodec:              codec,\\n\\t\\tversioner:          versioner,\\n\\t\\ttransformer:        transformer,\\n\\t\\tpathPrefix:         pathPrefix,\\n\\t\\tgroupResource:      groupResource,\\n\\t\\twatcher:            w,\\n\\t\\tleaseManager:       newDefaultLeaseManager(c.Client, leaseManagerConfig),\\n\\t\\tdecoder:            decoder,\\n\\t\\tlistErrAggrFactory: listErrAggrFactory,\\n\\n\\t\\tresourcePrefix: resourcePrefix,\\n\\t\\tnewListFunc:    newListFunc,\\n\\t\\tcompactor:      compactor,\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tw.getResourceSizeEstimator = s.getResourceSizeEstimator\\n\\tw.getCurrentStorageRV = func(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error) {\\n\\t\\treturn s.GetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx)\\n\\t}\\n\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ConsistentListFromCache) || utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.WatchList) {\\n\\t\\tetcdfeature.DefaultFeatureSupportChecker.CheckClient(c.Ctx(), c, storage.RequestWatchProgress)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn s, nil\\n}\\n\\nfunc (s *store) CompactRevision() int64 {\\n\\tif s.compactor == nil {\\n\\t\\treturn 0\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn s.compactor.CompactRevision()\\n}\\n\\n// Versioner implements storage.Interface.Versioner.\\nfunc (s *store) Versioner() storage.Versioner {\\n\\treturn s.versioner\\n}\\n\\nfunc (s *store) Close() {\\n\\tstats := s.getResourceSizeEstimator()\\n\\tif stats != nil {\\n\\t\\tstats.Close()\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\nfunc (s *store) getResourceSizeEstimator() *resourceSizeEstimator {\\n\\ts.collectorMux.RLock()\\n\\tdefer s.collectorMux.RUnlock()\\n\\treturn s.resourceSizeEstimator\\n}\\n\\n// Get implements storage.Interface.Get.\\nfunc (s *store) Get(ctx context.Context, key string, opts storage.GetOptions, out runtime.Object) error {\\n\\tpreparedKey, err := s.prepareKey(key, false)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tstartTime := time.Now()\\n\\tgetResp, err := s.client.Kubernetes.Get(ctx, preparedKey, kubernetes.GetOptions{})\\n\\tmetrics.RecordEtcdRequest(\\\"get\\\", s.groupResource, err, startTime)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tif err = s.validateMinimumResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion, uint64(getResp.Revision)); err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif getResp.KV == nil {\\n\\t\\tif opts.IgnoreNotFound {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn runtime.SetZeroValue(out)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\treturn storage.NewKeyNotFoundError(preparedKey, 0)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tdata, _, err := s.transformer.TransformFromStorage(ctx, getResp.KV.Value, authenticatedDataString(preparedKey))\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn storage.NewInternalError(err)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\terr = s.decoder.Decode(data, out, getResp.KV.ModRevision)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\trecordDecodeError(s.groupResource, preparedKey)\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// Create implements storage.Interface.Create.\\nfunc (s *store) Create(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error {\\n\\tpreparedKey, err := s.prepareKey(key, false)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, \\\"Create etcd3\\\",\\n\\t\\tattribute.String(\\\"audit-id\\\", audit.GetAuditIDTruncated(ctx)),\\n\\t\\tattribute.String(\\\"key\\\", key),\\n\\t\\tattribute.String(\\\"type\\\", getTypeName(obj)),\\n\\t\\tattribute.String(\\\"group\\\", s.groupResource.Group),\\n\\t\\tattribute.String(\\\"resource\\\", s.groupResource.Resource),\\n\\t)\\n\\tdefer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)\\n\\tif version, err := s.versioner.ObjectResourceVersion(obj); err == nil && version != 0 {\\n\\t\\treturn storage.ErrResourceVersionSetOnCreate\\n\\t}\\n\\tif err := s.versioner.PrepareObjectForStorage(obj); err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"PrepareObjectForStorage failed: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"About to Encode\\\")\\n\\tdata, err := runtime.Encode(s.codec, obj)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Encode failed\\\", attribute.Int(\\\"len\\\", len(data)), attribute.String(\\\"err\\\", err.Error()))\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Encode succeeded\\\", attribute.Int(\\\"len\\\", len(data)))\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"4d374ab6-b3bf-4bdb-95ac-6f3a85fe2a13\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:07.564Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"1e006ec1-2bd9-4c3a-962b-fadafe625756\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"4d374ab6-b3bf-4bdb-95ac-6f3a85fe2a13\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_019AoFbHdN8fbYhX7o5n23cc\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01VwAegn7djdJvNAHrG3aJoR\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"head -200 /tmp/rest.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":3310,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":54614,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":3310},\"output_tokens\":26,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2z58PYFpHHnKYjg9iD\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"de5397c6-0926-4efc-8dc5-4ae78c3ba32c\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:09.331Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"de5397c6-0926-4efc-8dc5-4ae78c3ba32c\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01VwAegn7djdJvNAHrG3aJoR\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage rest\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"io\\\"\\n\\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n\\t\\\"net/url\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n\\n\\tmetainternalversion \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion\\\"\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n)\\n\\n//TODO:\\n// Storage interfaces need to be separated into two groups; those that operate\\n// on collections and those that operate on individually named items.\\n// Collection interfaces:\\n// (Method: Current -> Proposed)\\n//    GET: Lister -> CollectionGetter\\n//    WATCH: Watcher -> CollectionWatcher\\n//    CREATE: Creater -> CollectionCreater\\n//    DELETE: (n/a) -> CollectionDeleter\\n//    UPDATE: (n/a) -> CollectionUpdater\\n//\\n// Single item interfaces:\\n// (Method: Current -> Proposed)\\n//    GET: Getter -> NamedGetter\\n//    WATCH: (n/a) -> NamedWatcher\\n//    CREATE: (n/a) -> NamedCreater\\n//    DELETE: Deleter -> NamedDeleter\\n//    UPDATE: Update -> NamedUpdater\\n\\n// Storage is a generic interface for RESTful storage services.\\n// Resources which are exported to the RESTful API of apiserver need to implement this interface. It is expected\\n// that objects may implement any of the below interfaces.\\n//\\n// Consider using StorageWithReadiness whenever possible.\\ntype Storage interface {\\n\\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create and Update after request data has been put into it.\\n\\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n\\tNew() runtime.Object\\n\\n\\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\\n\\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\\n\\t// for being called more than once.\\n\\tDestroy()\\n}\\n\\n// StorageWithReadiness extends Storage interface with the readiness check.\\ntype StorageWithReadiness interface {\\n\\tStorage\\n\\n\\t// ReadinessCheck allows for checking storage readiness.\\n\\tReadinessCheck() error\\n}\\n\\n// Scoper indicates what scope the resource is at. It must be specified.\\n// It is usually provided automatically based on your strategy.\\ntype Scoper interface {\\n\\t// NamespaceScoped returns true if the storage is namespaced\\n\\tNamespaceScoped() bool\\n}\\n\\n// KindProvider specifies a different kind for its API than for its internal storage.  This is necessary for external\\n// objects that are not compiled into the api server.  For such objects, there is no in-memory representation for\\n// the object, so they must be represented as generic objects (e.g. runtime.Unknown), but when we present the object as part of\\n// API discovery we want to present the specific kind, not the generic internal representation.\\ntype KindProvider interface {\\n\\tKind() string\\n}\\n\\n// ShortNamesProvider is an interface for RESTful storage services. Delivers a list of short names for a resource. The list is used by kubectl to have short names representation of resources.\\ntype ShortNamesProvider interface {\\n\\tShortNames() []string\\n}\\n\\n// CategoriesProvider allows a resource to specify which groups of resources (categories) it's part of. Categories can\\n// be used by API clients to refer to a batch of resources by using a single name (e.g. \\\"all\\\" could translate to \\\"pod,rc,svc,...\\\").\\ntype CategoriesProvider interface {\\n\\tCategories() []string\\n}\\n\\n// SingularNameProvider returns singular name of resources. This is used by kubectl discovery to have singular\\n// name representation of resources. In case of shortcut conflicts(with CRD shortcuts) singular name should always map to this resource.\\ntype SingularNameProvider interface {\\n\\tGetSingularName() string\\n}\\n\\n// GroupVersionKindProvider is used to specify a particular GroupVersionKind to discovery.  This is used for polymorphic endpoints\\n// which generally point to foreign versions.  Scale refers to Scale.v1beta1.extensions for instance.\\n// This trumps KindProvider since it is capable of providing the information required.\\n// TODO KindProvider (only used by federation) should be removed and replaced with this, but that presents greater risk late in 1.8.\\ntype GroupVersionKindProvider interface {\\n\\tGroupVersionKind(containingGV schema.GroupVersion) schema.GroupVersionKind\\n}\\n\\n// GroupVersionAcceptor is used to determine if a particular GroupVersion is acceptable to send to an endpoint.\\n// This is used for endpoints which accept multiple versions (which is extremely rare).\\n// The only known instance is pods/evictions which accepts policy/v1, but also policy/v1beta1 for backwards compatibility.\\ntype GroupVersionAcceptor interface {\\n\\tAcceptsGroupVersion(gv schema.GroupVersion) bool\\n}\\n\\n// Lister is an object that can retrieve resources that match the provided field and label criteria.\\ntype Lister interface {\\n\\t// NewList returns an empty object that can be used with the List call.\\n\\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n\\tNewList() runtime.Object\\n\\t// List selects resources in the storage which match to the selector. 'options' can be nil.\\n\\tList(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n\\t// TableConvertor ensures all list implementers also implement table conversion\\n\\tTableConvertor\\n}\\n\\n// Getter is an object that can retrieve a named RESTful resource.\\ntype Getter interface {\\n\\t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\\n\\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\\n\\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\\n\\tGet(ctx context.Context, name string, options *metav1.GetOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n}\\n\\n// GetterWithOptions is an object that retrieve a named RESTful resource and takes\\n// additional options on the get request. It allows a caller to also receive the\\n// subpath of the GET request.\\ntype GetterWithOptions interface {\\n\\t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\\n\\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\\n\\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\\n\\t// The options object passed to it is of the same type returned by the NewGetOptions\\n\\t// method.\\n\\t// TODO: Pass metav1.GetOptions.\\n\\tGet(ctx context.Context, name string, options runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error)\\n\\n\\t// NewGetOptions returns an empty options object that will be used to pass\\n\\t// options to the Get method. It may return a bool and a string, if true, the\\n\\t// value of the request path below the object will be included as the named\\n\\t// string in the serialization of the runtime object. E.g., returning \\\"path\\\"\\n\\t// will convert the trailing request scheme value to \\\"path\\\" in the map[string][]string\\n\\t// passed to the converter.\\n\\tNewGetOptions() (runtime.Object, bool, string)\\n}\\n\\ntype TableConvertor interface {\\n\\tConvertToTable(ctx context.Context, object runtime.Object, tableOptions runtime.Object) (*metav1.Table, error)\\n}\\n\\n// GracefulDeleter knows how to pass deletion options to allow delayed deletion of a\\n// RESTful object.\\ntype GracefulDeleter interface {\\n\\t// Delete finds a resource in the storage and deletes it.\\n\\t// The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.\\n\\t// If options are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an invalid\\n\\t// request error.\\n\\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\\n\\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\\n\\t// Delete *may* return the object that was deleted, or a status object indicating additional\\n\\t// information about deletion.\\n\\t// It also returns a boolean which is set to true if the resource was instantly\\n\\t// deleted or false if it will be deleted asynchronously.\\n\\tDelete(ctx context.Context, name string, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\\n}\\n\\n// MayReturnFullObjectDeleter may return deleted object (instead of a simple status) on deletion.\\ntype MayReturnFullObjectDeleter interface {\\n\\tDeleteReturnsDeletedObject() bool\\n}\\n\\n// CollectionDeleter is an object that can delete a collection\\n// of RESTful resources.\\ntype CollectionDeleter interface {\\n\\t// DeleteCollection selects all resources in the storage matching given 'listOptions'\\n\\t// and deletes them. The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.\\n\\t// If 'options' are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an\\n\\t// invalid request error.\\n\\t// DeleteCollection may not be atomic - i.e. it may delete some objects and still\\n\\t// return an error after it. On success, returns a list of deleted objects.\\n\\tDeleteCollection(ctx context.Context, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions, listOptions *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n}\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"70dab0ab-fad2-4023-93a3-e9f1cd6a6a53\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:09.364Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"de5397c6-0926-4efc-8dc5-4ae78c3ba32c\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"70dab0ab-fad2-4023-93a3-e9f1cd6a6a53\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01G3p5yUpM5YBXx7i4cEeJuE\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01TKd5LhEBpTAT2EBHH2n4KH\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"head -400 /tmp/store_generic.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":2626,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":57924,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":2626},\"output_tokens\":26,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2zCokHWSi398gQwH71\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"171f6655-9e46-4eae-91d0-c8591a73b892\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:12.176Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"171f6655-9e46-4eae-91d0-c8591a73b892\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01TKd5LhEBpTAT2EBHH2n4KH\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage registry\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n\\n\\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validate/content\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validation\\\"\\n\\tmetainternalversion \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion\\\"\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n\\tutilruntime \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation/field\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager\\\"\\n\\tgenericapirequest \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\\\"\\n\\tstoreerr \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/metrics\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/dryrun\\\"\\n\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n\\tflowcontrolrequest \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/request\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// FinishFunc is a function returned by Begin hooks to complete an operation.\\ntype FinishFunc func(ctx context.Context, success bool)\\n\\n// AfterDeleteFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterDelete hook.\\ntype AfterDeleteFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.DeleteOptions)\\n\\n// BeginCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.BeginCreate hook.\\ntype BeginCreateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (FinishFunc, error)\\n\\n// AfterCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterCreate hook.\\ntype AfterCreateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions)\\n\\n// BeginUpdateFunc is the type used for the Store.BeginUpdate hook.\\ntype BeginUpdateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (FinishFunc, error)\\n\\n// AfterUpdateFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterUpdate hook.\\ntype AfterUpdateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.UpdateOptions)\\n\\n// GenericStore interface can be used for type assertions when we need to access the underlying strategies.\\ntype GenericStore interface {\\n\\tGetCreateStrategy() rest.RESTCreateStrategy\\n\\tGetUpdateStrategy() rest.RESTUpdateStrategy\\n\\tGetDeleteStrategy() rest.RESTDeleteStrategy\\n}\\n\\n// Store implements k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest.StandardStorage. It's\\n// intended to be embeddable and allows the consumer to implement any\\n// non-generic functions that are required. This object is intended to be\\n// copyable so that it can be used in different ways but share the same\\n// underlying behavior.\\n//\\n// All fields are required unless specified.\\n//\\n// The intended use of this type is embedding within a Kind specific\\n// RESTStorage implementation. This type provides CRUD semantics on a Kubelike\\n// resource, handling details like conflict detection with ResourceVersion and\\n// semantics. The RESTCreateStrategy, RESTUpdateStrategy, and\\n// RESTDeleteStrategy are generic across all backends, and encapsulate logic\\n// specific to the API.\\n//\\n// TODO: make the default exposed methods exactly match a generic RESTStorage\\ntype Store struct {\\n\\t// NewFunc returns a new instance of the type this registry returns for a\\n\\t// GET of a single object, e.g.:\\n\\t//\\n\\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource/name-of-object\\n\\tNewFunc func() runtime.Object\\n\\n\\t// NewListFunc returns a new list of the type this registry; it is the\\n\\t// type returned when the resource is listed, e.g.:\\n\\t//\\n\\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource\\n\\tNewListFunc func() runtime.Object\\n\\n\\t// DefaultQualifiedResource is the pluralized name of the resource.\\n\\t// This field is used if there is no request info present in the context.\\n\\t// See qualifiedResourceFromContext for details.\\n\\tDefaultQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\\n\\n\\t// SingularQualifiedResource is the singular name of the resource.\\n\\tSingularQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\\n\\n\\t// KeyRootFunc returns the root etcd key for this resource; should not\\n\\t// include trailing \\\"/\\\".  This is used for operations that work on the\\n\\t// entire collection (listing and watching).\\n\\t//\\n\\t// KeyRootFunc and KeyFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\\n\\tKeyRootFunc func(ctx context.Context) string\\n\\n\\t// KeyFunc returns the key for a specific object in the collection.\\n\\t// KeyFunc is called for Create/Update/Get/Delete. Note that 'namespace'\\n\\t// can be gotten from ctx.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// KeyFunc and KeyRootFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\\n\\tKeyFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string) (string, error)\\n\\n\\t// ObjectNameFunc returns the name of an object or an error.\\n\\tObjectNameFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error)\\n\\n\\t// TTLFunc returns the TTL (time to live) that objects should be persisted\\n\\t// with. The existing parameter is the current TTL or the default for this\\n\\t// operation. The update parameter indicates whether this is an operation\\n\\t// against an existing object.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// Objects that are persisted with a TTL are evicted once the TTL expires.\\n\\tTTLFunc func(obj runtime.Object, existing uint64, update bool) (uint64, error)\\n\\n\\t// PredicateFunc returns a matcher corresponding to the provided labels\\n\\t// and fields. The SelectionPredicate returned should return true if the\\n\\t// object matches the given field and label selectors.\\n\\tPredicateFunc func(label labels.Selector, field fields.Selector) storage.SelectionPredicate\\n\\n\\t// EnableGarbageCollection affects the handling of Update and Delete\\n\\t// requests. Enabling garbage collection allows finalizers to do work to\\n\\t// finalize this object before the store deletes it.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// If any store has garbage collection enabled, it must also be enabled in\\n\\t// the kube-controller-manager.\\n\\tEnableGarbageCollection bool\\n\\n\\t// DeleteCollectionWorkers is the maximum number of workers in a single\\n\\t// DeleteCollection call. Delete requests for the items in a collection\\n\\t// are issued in parallel.\\n\\tDeleteCollectionWorkers int\\n\\n\\t// Decorator is an optional exit hook on an object returned from the\\n\\t// underlying storage. The returned object could be an individual object\\n\\t// (e.g. Pod) or a list type (e.g. PodList). Decorator is intended for\\n\\t// integrations that are above storage and should only be used for\\n\\t// specific cases where storage of the value is not appropriate, since\\n\\t// they cannot be watched.\\n\\tDecorator func(runtime.Object)\\n\\n\\t// CreateStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during creation.\\n\\tCreateStrategy rest.RESTCreateStrategy\\n\\t// BeginCreate is an optional hook that returns a \\\"transaction-like\\\"\\n\\t// commit/revert function which will be called at the end of the operation,\\n\\t// but before AfterCreate and Decorator, indicating via the argument\\n\\t// whether the operation succeeded.  If this returns an error, the function\\n\\t// is not called.  Almost nobody should use this hook.\\n\\tBeginCreate BeginCreateFunc\\n\\t// AfterCreate implements a further operation to run after a resource is\\n\\t// created and before it is decorated, optional.\\n\\tAfterCreate AfterCreateFunc\\n\\n\\t// UpdateStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during updates.\\n\\tUpdateStrategy rest.RESTUpdateStrategy\\n\\t// BeginUpdate is an optional hook that returns a \\\"transaction-like\\\"\\n\\t// commit/revert function which will be called at the end of the operation,\\n\\t// but before AfterUpdate and Decorator, indicating via the argument\\n\\t// whether the operation succeeded.  If this returns an error, the function\\n\\t// is not called.  Almost nobody should use this hook.\\n\\tBeginUpdate BeginUpdateFunc\\n\\t// AfterUpdate implements a further operation to run after a resource is\\n\\t// updated and before it is decorated, optional.\\n\\tAfterUpdate AfterUpdateFunc\\n\\n\\t// DeleteStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during deletion.\\n\\tDeleteStrategy rest.RESTDeleteStrategy\\n\\t// AfterDelete implements a further operation to run after a resource is\\n\\t// deleted and before it is decorated, optional.\\n\\tAfterDelete AfterDeleteFunc\\n\\t// ReturnDeletedObject determines whether the Store returns the object\\n\\t// that was deleted. Otherwise, return a generic success status response.\\n\\tReturnDeletedObject bool\\n\\t// ShouldDeleteDuringUpdate is an optional function to determine whether\\n\\t// an update from existing to obj should result in a delete.\\n\\t// If specified, this is checked in addition to standard finalizer,\\n\\t// deletionTimestamp, and deletionGracePeriodSeconds checks.\\n\\tShouldDeleteDuringUpdate func(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, existing runtime.Object) bool\\n\\n\\t// TableConvertor is an optional interface for transforming items or lists\\n\\t// of items into tabular output. If unset, the default will be used.\\n\\tTableConvertor rest.TableConvertor\\n\\n\\t// ResetFieldsStrategy provides the fields reset by the strategy that\\n\\t// should not be modified by the user.\\n\\tResetFieldsStrategy rest.ResetFieldsStrategy\\n\\n\\t// Storage is the interface for the underlying storage for the\\n\\t// resource. It is wrapped into a \\\"DryRunnableStorage\\\" that will\\n\\t// either pass-through or simply dry-run.\\n\\tStorage DryRunnableStorage\\n\\t// StorageVersioner outputs the <group/version/kind> an object will be\\n\\t// converted to before persisted in etcd, given a list of possible\\n\\t// kinds of the object.\\n\\t// If the StorageVersioner is nil, apiserver will leave the\\n\\t// storageVersionHash as empty in the discovery document.\\n\\tStorageVersioner runtime.GroupVersioner\\n\\n\\t// ReadinessCheckFunc checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\\n\\t// The field is optional, if set needs to be thread-safe.\\n\\tReadinessCheckFunc func() error\\n\\n\\t// DestroyFunc cleans up clients used by the underlying Storage; optional.\\n\\t// If set, DestroyFunc has to be implemented in thread-safe way and\\n\\t// be prepared for being called more than once.\\n\\tDestroyFunc func()\\n\\n\\t// corruptObjDeleter implements unsafe deletion flow to enable deletion\\n\\t// of corrupt object(s), it makes an attempt to perform a normal\\n\\t// deletion flow first, and if the normal deletion flow fails with a\\n\\t// corrupt object error then it proceeds with the unsafe deletion\\n\\t// of the object from the storage.\\n\\t// NOTE: it skips precondition checks, finalizer constraints, and any\\n\\t// after delete hook defined in 'AfterDelete' of the registry.\\n\\t// WARNING: This may break the cluster if the resource has\\n\\t// dependencies. Use when the cluster is broken, and there is no\\n\\t// other viable option to repair the cluster.\\n\\tcorruptObjDeleter rest.GracefulDeleter\\n}\\n\\n// Note: the rest.StandardStorage interface aggregates the common REST verbs\\nvar _ rest.StandardStorage = &Store{}\\nvar _ rest.StorageWithReadiness = &Store{}\\nvar _ rest.TableConvertor = &Store{}\\nvar _ GenericStore = &Store{}\\n\\nvar _ rest.SingularNameProvider = &Store{}\\n\\nvar _ rest.CorruptObjectDeleterProvider = &Store{}\\n\\nconst (\\n\\tOptimisticLockErrorMsg        = \\\"the object has been modified; please apply your changes to the latest version and try again\\\"\\n\\tresourceCountPollPeriodJitter = 1.2\\n)\\n\\n// NamespaceKeyRootFunc is the default function for constructing storage paths\\n// to resource directories enforcing namespace rules.\\nfunc NamespaceKeyRootFunc(ctx context.Context, prefix string) string {\\n\\tkey := prefix\\n\\tns, ok := genericapirequest.NamespaceFrom(ctx)\\n\\tif ok && len(ns) > 0 {\\n\\t\\tkey = key + \\\"/\\\" + ns\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn key\\n}\\n\\n// NamespaceKeyFunc is the default function for constructing storage paths to\\n// a resource relative to the given prefix enforcing namespace rules. If the\\n// context does not contain a namespace, it errors.\\nfunc NamespaceKeyFunc(ctx context.Context, prefix string, name string) (string, error) {\\n\\tkey := NamespaceKeyRootFunc(ctx, prefix)\\n\\tns, ok := genericapirequest.NamespaceFrom(ctx)\\n\\tif !ok || len(ns) == 0 {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(\\\"Namespace parameter required.\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\tif len(name) == 0 {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(\\\"Name parameter required.\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\tif msgs := content.IsPathSegmentName(name); len(msgs) != 0 {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Name parameter invalid: %q: %s\\\", name, strings.Join(msgs, \\\";\\\")))\\n\\t}\\n\\tkey = key + \\\"/\\\" + name\\n\\treturn key, nil\\n}\\n\\n// NoNamespaceKeyFunc is the default function for constructing storage paths\\n// to a resource relative to the given prefix without a namespace.\\nfunc NoNamespaceKeyFunc(ctx context.Context, prefix string, name string) (string, error) {\\n\\tif len(name) == 0 {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(\\\"Name parameter required.\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\tif msgs := content.IsPathSegmentName(name); len(msgs) != 0 {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Name parameter invalid: %q: %s\\\", name, strings.Join(msgs, \\\";\\\")))\\n\\t}\\n\\tkey := prefix + \\\"/\\\" + name\\n\\treturn key, nil\\n}\\n\\n// New implements RESTStorage.New.\\nfunc (e *Store) New() runtime.Object {\\n\\treturn e.NewFunc()\\n}\\n\\n// ReadinessCheck checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\\nfunc (e *Store) ReadinessCheck() error {\\n\\tif e.ReadinessCheckFunc != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn e.ReadinessCheckFunc()\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\\nfunc (e *Store) Destroy() {\\n\\tif e.DestroyFunc != nil {\\n\\t\\te.DestroyFunc()\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\n// NewList implements rest.Lister.\\nfunc (e *Store) NewList() runtime.Object {\\n\\treturn e.NewListFunc()\\n}\\n\\n// NamespaceScoped indicates whether the resource is namespaced\\nfunc (e *Store) NamespaceScoped() bool {\\n\\tif e.CreateStrategy != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn e.CreateStrategy.NamespaceScoped()\\n\\t}\\n\\tif e.UpdateStrategy != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn e.UpdateStrategy.NamespaceScoped()\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tpanic(\\\"programmer error: no CRUD for resource, override NamespaceScoped too\\\")\\n}\\n\\n// GetCreateStrategy implements GenericStore.\\nfunc (e *Store) GetCreateStrategy() rest.RESTCreateStrategy {\\n\\treturn e.CreateStrategy\\n}\\n\\n// GetUpdateStrategy implements GenericStore.\\nfunc (e *Store) GetUpdateStrategy() rest.RESTUpdateStrategy {\\n\\treturn e.UpdateStrategy\\n}\\n\\n// GetDeleteStrategy implements GenericStore.\\nfunc (e *Store) GetDeleteStrategy() rest.RESTDeleteStrategy {\\n\\treturn e.DeleteStrategy\\n}\\n\\n// GetCorruptObjDeleter returns the unsafe corrupt object deleter\\nfunc (e *Store) GetCorruptObjDeleter() rest.GracefulDeleter {\\n\\treturn e.corruptObjDeleter\\n}\\n\\n// List returns a list of items matching labels and field according to the\\n// store's PredicateFunc.\\nfunc (e *Store) List(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n\\tlabel := labels.Everything()\\n\\tif options != nil && options.LabelSelector != nil {\\n\\t\\tlabel = options.LabelSelector\\n\\t}\\n\\tfield := fields.Everything()\\n\\tif options != nil && options.FieldSelector != nil {\\n\\t\\tfield = options.FieldSelector\\n\\t}\\n\\tout, err := e.ListPredicate(ctx, e.PredicateFunc(label, field), options)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t}\\n\\tif e.Decorator != nil {\\n\\t\\te.Decorator(out)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn out, nil\\n}\\n\\n// ListPredicate returns a list of all the items matching the given\\n// SelectionPredicate.\\nfunc (e *Store) ListPredicate(ctx context.Context, p storage.SelectionPredicate, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n\\tif options == nil {\\n\\t\\t// By default we should serve the request from etcd.\\n\\t\\toptions = &metainternalversion.ListOptions{ResourceVersion: \\\"\\\"}\\n\\t}\\n\\tp.Limit = options.Limit\\n\\tp.Continue = options.Continue\\n\\tlist := e.NewListFunc()\\n\\tqualifiedResource := e.qualifiedResourceFromContext(ctx)\\n\\tstorageOpts := storage.ListOptions{\\n\\t\\tResourceVersion:      options.ResourceVersion,\\n\\t\\tResourceVersionMatch: options.ResourceVersionMatch,\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"09eff0f3-14c2-47ab-b914-9961971a6352\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:12.190Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"171f6655-9e46-4eae-91d0-c8591a73b892\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"09eff0f3-14c2-47ab-b914-9961971a6352\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01VJqrmZWoHGjuHALbDzxJif\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now
