/tmp/cacher.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4056,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":65342,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4056},\"output_tokens\":26,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2zWY6pk91Ex5jjp6ga\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"e0406c58-f936-4e3b-936c-bf40cc7e3fc2\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:15.775Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"e0406c58-f936-4e3b-936c-bf40cc7e3fc2\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01S57TQC32MceG1dYnXimRAc\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage cacher\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n\\t\\\"reflect\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\\\"\\n\\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/metadata\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/delegator\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/metrics\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/progress\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/store\\\"\\n\\tetcdfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/feature\\\"\\n\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/clock\\\"\\n)\\n\\nvar (\\n\\temptyFunc             = func(bool) {}\\n\\tcoreNamespaceResource = schema.GroupResource{Group: \\\"\\\", Resource: \\\"namespaces\\\"}\\n)\\n\\nconst (\\n\\t// storageWatchListPageSize is the cacher's request chunk size of\\n\\t// initial and resync watch lists to storage.\\n\\tstorageWatchListPageSize = int64(10000)\\n\\n\\t// DefaultEventFreshDuration is the default time duration of events\\n\\t// we want to keep.\\n\\t// We set it to defaultBookmarkFrequency plus epsilon to maximize\\n\\t// chances that last bookmark was sent within kept history, at the\\n\\t// same time, minimizing the needed memory usage.\\n\\tDefaultEventFreshDuration = defaultBookmarkFrequency + 15*time.Second\\n\\n\\t// defaultBookmarkFrequency defines how frequently watch bookmarks should be send\\n\\t// in addition to sending a bookmark right before watch deadline.\\n\\tdefaultBookmarkFrequency = time.Minute\\n)\\n\\n// Config contains the configuration for a given Cache.\\ntype Config struct {\\n\\t// An underlying storage.Interface.\\n\\tStorage storage.Interface\\n\\n\\t// An underlying storage.Versioner.\\n\\tVersioner storage.Versioner\\n\\n\\t// The GroupResource the cacher is caching. Used for disambiguating *unstructured.Unstructured (CRDs) in logging\\n\\t// and metrics.\\n\\tGroupResource schema.GroupResource\\n\\n\\t// EventsHistoryWindow specifies minimum history duration that storage is keeping.\\n\\t// If lower than DefaultEventFreshDuration, the cache creation will fail.\\n\\tEventsHistoryWindow time.Duration\\n\\n\\t// The Cache will be caching objects of a given Type and assumes that they\\n\\t// are all stored under ResourcePrefix directory in the underlying database.\\n\\tResourcePrefix string\\n\\n\\t// KeyFunc is used to get a key in the underlying storage for a given object.\\n\\tKeyFunc func(runtime.Object) (string, error)\\n\\n\\t// GetAttrsFunc is used to get object labels, fields\\n\\tGetAttrsFunc func(runtime.Object) (label labels.Set, field fields.Set, err error)\\n\\n\\t// IndexerFuncs is used for optimizing amount of watchers that\\n\\t// needs to process an incoming event.\\n\\tIndexerFuncs storage.IndexerFuncs\\n\\n\\t// Indexers is used to accelerate the list operation, falls back to regular list\\n\\t// operation if no indexer found.\\n\\tIndexers *cache.Indexers\\n\\n\\t// NewFunc is a function that creates new empty object storing a object of type Type.\\n\\tNewFunc func() runtime.Object\\n\\n\\t// NewList is a function that creates new empty object storing a list of\\n\\t// objects of type Type.\\n\\tNewListFunc func() runtime.Object\\n\\n\\tCodec runtime.Codec\\n\\n\\tClock clock.WithTicker\\n}\\n\\ntype watchersMap map[int]*cacheWatcher\\n\\nfunc (wm watchersMap) addWatcher(w *cacheWatcher, number int) {\\n\\twm[number] = w\\n}\\n\\nfunc (wm watchersMap) deleteWatcher(number int) {\\n\\tdelete(wm, number)\\n}\\n\\nfunc (wm watchersMap) terminateAll(done func(*cacheWatcher)) {\\n\\tfor key, watcher := range wm {\\n\\t\\tdelete(wm, key)\\n\\t\\tdone(watcher)\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\ntype indexedWatchers struct {\\n\\tallWatchers   map[namespacedName]watchersMap\\n\\tvalueWatchers map[string]watchersMap\\n}\\n\\nfunc (i *indexedWatchers) addWatcher(w *cacheWatcher, number int, scope namespacedName, value string, supported bool) {\\n\\tif supported {\\n\\t\\tif _, ok := i.valueWatchers[value]; !ok {\\n\\t\\t\\ti.valueWatchers[value] = watchersMap{}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\ti.valueWatchers[value].addWatcher(w, number)\\n\\t} else {\\n\\t\\tscopedWatchers, ok := i.allWatchers[scope]\\n\\t\\tif !ok {\\n\\t\\t\\tscopedWatchers = watchersMap{}\\n\\t\\t\\ti.allWatchers[scope] = scopedWatchers\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tscopedWatchers.addWatcher(w, number)\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\nfunc (i *indexedWatchers) deleteWatcher(number int, scope namespacedName, value string, supported bool) {\\n\\tif supported {\\n\\t\\ti.valueWatchers[value].deleteWatcher(number)\\n\\t\\tif len(i.valueWatchers[value]) == 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\tdelete(i.valueWatchers, value)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t} else {\\n\\t\\ti.allWatchers[scope].deleteWatcher(number)\\n\\t\\tif len(i.allWatchers[scope]) == 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\tdelete(i.allWatchers, scope)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\nfunc (i *indexedWatchers) terminateAll(groupResource schema.GroupResource, done func(*cacheWatcher)) {\\n\\t// note that we don't have to call setDrainInputBufferLocked method on the watchers\\n\\t// because we take advantage of the default value - stop immediately\\n\\t// also watchers that have had already its draining strategy set\\n\\t// are no longer available (they were removed from the allWatchers and the valueWatchers maps)\\n\\tif len(i.allWatchers) > 0 || len(i.valueWatchers) > 0 {\\n\\t\\tklog.Warningf(\\\"Terminating all watchers from cacher %v\\\", groupResource)\\n\\t}\\n\\tfor _, watchers := range i.allWatchers {\\n\\t\\twatchers.terminateAll(done)\\n\\t}\\n\\tfor _, watchers := range i.valueWatchers {\\n\\t\\twatchers.terminateAll(done)\\n\\t}\\n\\ti.allWatchers = map[namespacedName]watchersMap{}\\n\\ti.valueWatchers = map[string]watchersMap{}\\n}\\n\\n// As we don't need a high precision here, we keep all watchers timeout within a\\n// second in a bucket, and pop up them once at the timeout. To be more specific,\\n// if you set fire time at X, you can get the bookmark within (X-1,X+1) period.\\ntype watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets struct {\\n\\t// the key of watcherBuckets is the number of seconds since createTime\\n\\twatchersBuckets   map[int64][]*cacheWatcher\\n\\tcreateTime        time.Time\\n\\tstartBucketID     int64\\n\\tclock             clock.Clock\\n\\tbookmarkFrequency time.Duration\\n}\\n\\nfunc newTimeBucketWatchers(clock clock.Clock, bookmarkFrequency time.Duration) *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets {\\n\\treturn &watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets{\\n\\t\\twatchersBuckets:   make(map[int64][]*cacheWatcher),\\n\\t\\tcreateTime:        clock.Now(),\\n\\t\\tstartBucketID:     0,\\n\\t\\tclock:             clock,\\n\\t\\tbookmarkFrequency: bookmarkFrequency,\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\n// adds a watcher to the bucket, if the deadline is before the start, it will be\\n// added to the first one.\\nfunc (t *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets) addWatcherThreadUnsafe(w *cacheWatcher) bool {\\n\\t// note that the returned time can be before t.createTime,\\n\\t// especially in cases when the nextBookmarkTime method\\n\\t// give us the zero value of type Time\\n\\t// so buckedID can hold a negative value\\n\\tnextTime, ok := w.nextBookmarkTime(t.clock.Now(), t.bookmarkFrequency)\\n\\tif !ok {\\n\\t\\treturn false\\n\\t}\\n\\tbucketID := int64(nextTime.Sub(t.createTime) / time.Second)\\n\\tif bucketID < t.startBucketID {\\n\\t\\tbucketID = t.startBucketID\\n\\t}\\n\\twatchers := t.watchersBuckets[bucketID]\\n\\tt.watchersBuckets[bucketID] = append(watchers, w)\\n\\treturn true\\n}\\n\\nfunc (t *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets) popExpiredWatchersThreadUnsafe() [][]*cacheWatcher {\\n\\tcurrentBucketID := int64(t.clock.Since(t.createTime) / time.Second)\\n\\t// There should be one or two elements in almost all cases\\n\\texpiredWatchers := make([][]*cacheWatcher, 0, 2)\\n\\tfor ; t.startBucketID <= currentBucketID; t.startBucketID++ {\\n\\t\\tif watchers, ok := t.watchersBuckets[t.startBucketID]; ok {\\n\\t\\t\\tdelete(t.watchersBuckets, t.startBucketID)\\n\\t\\t\\texpiredWatchers = append(expiredWatchers, watchers)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn expiredWatchers\\n}\\n\\ntype filterWithAttrsFunc func(key string, l labels.Set, f fields.Set) bool\\n\\ntype indexedTriggerFunc struct {\\n\\tindexName   string\\n\\tindexerFunc storage.IndexerFunc\\n}\\n\\n// Cacher is responsible for serving WATCH and LIST requests for a given\\n// resource from its internal cache and updating its cache in the background\\n// based on the underlying storage contents.\\n// Cacher implements storage.Interface (although most of the calls are just\\n// delegated to the underlying storage).\\ntype Cacher struct {\\n\\t// HighWaterMarks for performance debugging.\\n\\t// Important: Since HighWaterMark is using sync/atomic, it has to be at the top of the struct due to a bug on 32-bit platforms\\n\\t// See: https://golang.org/pkg/sync/atomic/ for more information\\n\\tincomingHWM storage.HighWaterMark\\n\\t// Incoming events that should be dispatched to watchers.\\n\\tincoming chan watchCacheEvent\\n\\n\\tresourcePrefix string\\n\\n\\tsync.RWMutex\\n\\n\\t// Before accessing the cacher's cache, wait for the ready to be ok.\\n\\t// This is necessary to prevent users from accessing structures that are\\n\\t// uninitialized or are being repopulated right now.\\n\\t// ready needs to be set to false when the cacher is paused or stopped.\\n\\t// ready needs to be set to true when the cacher is ready to use after\\n\\t// initialization.\\n\\tready *ready\\n\\n\\t// Underlying storage.Interface.\\n\\tstorage storage.Interface\\n\\n\\t// Expected type of objects in the underlying cache.\\n\\tobjectType reflect.Type\\n\\t// Used for logging, to disambiguate *unstructured.Unstructured (CRDs)\\n\\tgroupResource schema.GroupResource\\n\\n\\t// \\\"sliding window\\\" of recent changes of objects and the current state.\\n\\twatchCache *watchCache\\n\\treflector  *cache.Reflector\\n\\n\\t// Versioner is used to handle resource versions.\\n\\tversioner storage.Versioner\\n\\n\\t// newFunc is a function that creates new empty object storing a object of type Type.\\n\\tnewFunc func() runtime.Object\\n\\n\\t// newListFunc is a function that creates new empty list for storing objects of type Type.\\n\\tnewListFunc func() runtime.Object\\n\\n\\t// indexedTrigger is used for optimizing amount of watchers that needs to process\\n\\t// an incoming event.\\n\\tindexedTrigger *indexedTriggerFunc\\n\\t// watchers is mapping from the value of trigger function that a\\n\\t// watcher is interested into the watchers\\n\\twatcherIdx int\\n\\twatchers   indexedWatchers\\n\\n\\t// Defines a time budget that can be spend on waiting for not-ready watchers\\n\\t// while dispatching event before shutting them down.\\n\\tdispatchTimeoutBudget timeBudget\\n\\n\\t// Handling graceful termination.\\n\\tstopLock sync.RWMutex\\n\\tstopped  bool\\n\\tstopCh   chan struct{}\\n\\tstopWg   sync.WaitGroup\\n\\n\\tclock clock.Clock\\n\\t// timer is used to avoid unnecessary allocations in underlying watchers.\\n\\ttimer *time.Timer\\n\\n\\t// dispatching determines whether there is currently dispatching of\\n\\t// any event in flight.\\n\\tdispatching bool\\n\\t// watchersBuffer is a list of watchers potentially interested in currently\\n\\t// dispatched event.\\n\\twatchersBuffer []*cacheWatcher\\n\\t// blockedWatchers is a list of watchers whose buffer is currently full.\\n\\tblockedWatchers []*cacheWatcher\\n\\t// watchersToStop is a list of watchers that were supposed to be stopped\\n\\t// during current dispatching, but stopping was deferred to the end of\\n\\t// dispatching that event to avoid race with closing channels in watchers.\\n\\twatchersToStop []*cacheWatcher\\n\\t// Maintain a timeout queue to send the bookmark event before the watcher times out.\\n\\t// Note that this field when accessed MUST be protected by the Cacher.lock.\\n\\tbookmarkWatchers *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets\\n\\t// expiredBookmarkWatchers is a list of watchers that were expired and need to be schedule for a next bookmark event\\n\\texpiredBookmarkWatchers []*cacheWatcher\\n\\tcompactor               *compactor\\n}\\n\\n// NewCacherFromConfig creates a new Cacher responsible for servicing WATCH and LIST requests from\\n// its internal cache and updating its cache in the background based on the\\n// given configuration.\\nfunc NewCacherFromConfig(config Config) (*Cacher, error) {\\n\\tstopCh := make(chan struct{})\\n\\tobj := config.NewFunc()\\n\\t// Give this error when it is constructed rather than when you get the\\n\\t// first watch item, because it's much easier to track down that way.\\n\\tif err := runtime.CheckCodec(config.Codec, obj); err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"storage codec doesn't seem to match given type: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tvar indexedTrigger *indexedTriggerFunc\\n\\tif config.IndexerFuncs != nil {\\n\\t\\t// For now, we don't support multiple trigger functions defined\\n\\t\\t// for a given resource.\\n\\t\\tif len(config.IndexerFuncs) > 1 {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cacher %s doesn't support more than one IndexerFunc: \\\", reflect.TypeOf(obj).String())\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tfor key, value := range config.IndexerFuncs {\\n\\t\\t\\tif value != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tindexedTrigger = &indexedTriggerFunc{\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tindexName:   key,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tindexerFunc: value,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif config.Clock == nil {\\n\\t\\tconfig.Clock = clock.RealClock{}\\n\\t}\\n\\tobjType := reflect.TypeOf(obj)\\n\\tresourcePrefix := config.ResourcePrefix\\n\\tif resourcePrefix == \\\"\\\" {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"resourcePrefix cannot be empty\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\tif resourcePrefix == \\\"/\\\" {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"resourcePrefix cannot be /\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\tif !strings.HasPrefix(resourcePrefix, \\\"/\\\") {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"resourcePrefix needs to start from /\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\tcacher := &Cacher{\\n\\t\\tresourcePrefix: resourcePrefix,\\n\\t\\tready:          newReady(config.Clock),\\n\\t\\tstorage:        config.Storage,\\n\\t\\tobjectType:     objType,\\n\\t\\tgroupResource:  config.GroupResource,\\n\\t\\tversioner:      config.Versioner,\\n\\t\\tnewFunc:        config.NewFunc,\\n\\t\\tnewListFunc:    config.NewListFunc,\\n\\t\\tindexedTrigger: indexedTrigger,\\n\\t\\twatcherIdx:     0,\\n\\t\\twatchers: indexedWatchers{\\n\\t\\t\\tallWatchers:   make(map[namespacedName]watchersMap),\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"a42822c6-5307-4f19-8ebf-7cc5cc0d55e6\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:15.866Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"e0406c58-f936-4e3b-936c-bf40cc7e3fc2\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"a42822c6-5307-4f19-8ebf-7cc5cc0d55e6\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01Q8PhDoKrCqmVdimmNButUY\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01JknJ8QRRTTjs4wZUAsbn7Z\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"sed -n '500,900p' /tmp/cacher.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4563,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":69398,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4563},\"output_tokens\":26,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2zgQvcFYXxGUXGdHPo\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"7d89e9bd-38f4-457d-89a8-45df9f63a493\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:17.015Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"7d89e9bd-38f4-457d-89a8-45df9f63a493\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01JknJ8QRRTTjs4wZUAsbn7Z\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"\\t}\\n}\\n\\ntype namespacedName struct {\\n\\tnamespace string\\n\\tname      string\\n}\\n\\nfunc (c *Cacher) Watch(ctx context.Context, key string, opts storage.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error) {\\n\\tkey, err := c.prepareKey(key, opts.Recursive)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t}\\n\\tpred := opts.Predicate\\n\\trequestedWatchRV, err := c.versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tvar readyGeneration int\\n\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ResilientWatchCacheInitialization) {\\n\\t\\tvar err error\\n\\t\\tvar downtime time.Duration\\n\\t\\treadyGeneration, downtime, err = c.ready.checkAndReadGeneration()\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewTooManyRequests(err.Error(), calculateRetryAfterForUnreadyCache(downtime))\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t} else {\\n\\t\\treadyGeneration, err = c.ready.waitAndReadGeneration(ctx)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, errors.NewServiceUnavailable(err.Error())\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// determine the namespace and name scope of the watch, first from the request, secondarily from the field selector\\n\\tscope := namespacedName{}\\n\\tif requestNamespace, ok := request.NamespaceFrom(ctx); ok && len(requestNamespace) > 0 {\\n\\t\\tscope.namespace = requestNamespace\\n\\t} else if selectorNamespace, ok := pred.Field.RequiresExactMatch(\\\"metadata.namespace\\\"); ok {\\n\\t\\tscope.namespace = selectorNamespace\\n\\t}\\n\\tif requestInfo, ok := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx); ok && requestInfo != nil && len(requestInfo.Name) > 0 {\\n\\t\\tscope.name = requestInfo.Name\\n\\t} else if selectorName, ok := pred.Field.RequiresExactMatch(\\\"metadata.name\\\"); ok {\\n\\t\\tscope.name = selectorName\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// for request like '/api/v1/watch/namespaces/*', set scope.namespace to empty.\\n\\t// namespaces don't populate metadata.namespace in ObjFields.\\n\\tif c.groupResource == coreNamespaceResource && len(scope.namespace) > 0 && scope.namespace == scope.name {\\n\\t\\tscope.namespace = \\\"\\\"\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\ttriggerValue, triggerSupported := \\\"\\\", false\\n\\tif c.indexedTrigger != nil {\\n\\t\\tfor _, field := range pred.IndexFields {\\n\\t\\t\\tif field == c.indexedTrigger.indexName {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tif value, ok := pred.Field.RequiresExactMatch(field); ok {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttriggerValue, triggerSupported = value, true\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// It boils down to a tradeoff between:\\n\\t// - having it as small as possible to reduce memory usage\\n\\t// - having it large enough to ensure that watchers that need to process\\n\\t//   a bunch of changes have enough buffer to avoid from blocking other\\n\\t//   watchers on our watcher having a processing hiccup\\n\\tchanSize := c.watchCache.suggestedWatchChannelSize(c.indexedTrigger != nil, triggerSupported)\\n\\n\\t// client-go is going to fall back to a standard LIST on any error\\n\\t// returned for watch-list requests\\n\\tif isListWatchRequest(opts) && !etcdfeature.DefaultFeatureSupportChecker.Supports(storage.RequestWatchProgress) {\\n\\t\\treturn newErrWatcher(fmt.Errorf(\\\"a watch stream was requested by the client but the required storage feature %s is disabled\\\", storage.RequestWatchProgress)), nil\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// Determine the ResourceVersion to which the watch cache must be synchronized\\n\\trequiredResourceVersion, err := c.getWatchCacheResourceVersion(ctx, requestedWatchRV, opts)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn newErrWatcher(err), nil\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// Determine a function that computes the bookmarkAfterResourceVersion\\n\\tbookmarkAfterResourceVersionFn, err := c.getBookmarkAfterResourceVersionLockedFunc(requestedWatchRV, requiredResourceVersion, opts)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn newErrWatcher(err), nil\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// Determine watch timeout('0' means deadline is not set, ignore checking)\\n\\tdeadline, _ := ctx.Deadline()\\n\\n\\tidentifier := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"key: %q, labels: %q, fields: %q\\\", key, pred.Label, pred.Field)\\n\\n\\t// Create a watcher here to reduce memory allocations under lock,\\n\\t// given that memory allocation may trigger GC and block the thread.\\n\\t// Also note that emptyFunc is a placeholder, until we will be able\\n\\t// to compute watcher.forget function (which has to happen under lock).\\n\\twatcher := newCacheWatcher(\\n\\t\\tchanSize,\\n\\t\\tfilterWithAttrsAndPrefixFunction(key, pred),\\n\\t\\temptyFunc,\\n\\t\\tc.versioner,\\n\\t\\tdeadline,\\n\\t\\tpred.AllowWatchBookmarks,\\n\\t\\tc.groupResource,\\n\\t\\tidentifier,\\n\\t)\\n\\n\\t// note that c.waitUntilWatchCacheFreshAndForceAllEvents must be called without\\n\\t// the c.watchCache.RLock held otherwise we are at risk of a deadlock\\n\\t// mainly because c.watchCache.processEvent method won't be able to make progress\\n\\t//\\n\\t// moreover even though the c.waitUntilWatchCacheFreshAndForceAllEvents acquires a lock\\n\\t// it is safe to release the lock after the method finishes because we don't require\\n\\t// any atomicity between the call to the method and further calls that actually get the events.\\n\\terr = c.waitUntilWatchCacheFreshAndForceAllEvents(ctx, requiredResourceVersion, opts)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn newErrWatcher(err), nil\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// We explicitly use thread unsafe version and do locking ourself to ensure that\\n\\t// no new events will be processed in the meantime. The watchCache will be unlocked\\n\\t// on return from this function.\\n\\t// Note that we cannot do it under Cacher lock, to avoid a deadlock, since the\\n\\t// underlying watchCache is calling processEvent under its lock.\\n\\tc.watchCache.RLock()\\n\\tdefer c.watchCache.RUnlock()\\n\\n\\tvar cacheInterval *watchCacheInterval\\n\\tcacheInterval, err = c.watchCache.getAllEventsSinceLocked(requiredResourceVersion, key, opts)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t// To match the uncached watch implementation, once we have passed authn/authz/admission,\\n\\t\\t// and successfully parsed a resource version, other errors must fail with a watch event of type ERROR,\\n\\t\\t// rather than a directly returned error.\\n\\t\\treturn newErrWatcher(err), nil\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tc.setInitialEventsEndBookmarkIfRequested(cacheInterval, opts, c.watchCache.resourceVersion)\\n\\n\\taddedWatcher := false\\n\\tfunc() {\\n\\t\\tc.Lock()\\n\\t\\tdefer c.Unlock()\\n\\n\\t\\tif generation, _, err := c.ready.checkAndReadGeneration(); generation != readyGeneration || err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t// We went unready or are already on a different generation.\\n\\t\\t\\t// Avoid registering and starting the watch as it will have to be\\n\\t\\t\\t// terminated immediately anyway.\\n\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t// Update watcher.forget function once we can compute it.\\n\\t\\twatcher.forget = forgetWatcher(c, watcher, c.watcherIdx, scope, triggerValue, triggerSupported)\\n\\t\\t// Update the bookMarkAfterResourceVersion\\n\\t\\twatcher.setBookmarkAfterResourceVersion(bookmarkAfterResourceVersionFn())\\n\\t\\tc.watchers.addWatcher(watcher, c.watcherIdx, scope, triggerValue, triggerSupported)\\n\\t\\taddedWatcher = true\\n\\n\\t\\t// Add it to the queue only when the client support watch bookmarks.\\n\\t\\tif watcher.allowWatchBookmarks {\\n\\t\\t\\tc.bookmarkWatchers.addWatcherThreadUnsafe(watcher)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tc.watcherIdx++\\n\\t}()\\n\\n\\tif !addedWatcher {\\n\\t\\t// Watcher isn't really started at this point, so it's safe to just drop it.\\n\\t\\t//\\n\\t\\t// We're simulating the immediate watch termination, which boils down to simply\\n\\t\\t// closing the watcher.\\n\\t\\treturn newImmediateCloseWatcher(), nil\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tgo watcher.processInterval(ctx, cacheInterval, requiredResourceVersion)\\n\\treturn watcher, nil\\n}\\n\\nfunc (c *Cacher) Get(ctx context.Context, key string, opts storage.GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error {\\n\\tkey, err := c.prepareKey(key, false)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tgetRV, err := c.versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tobjVal, err := conversion.EnforcePtr(objPtr)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tobj, exists, readResourceVersion, err := c.watchCache.WaitUntilFreshAndGet(ctx, getRV, key)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif exists {\\n\\t\\telem, ok := obj.(*store.Element)\\n\\t\\tif !ok {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"non *store.Element returned from storage: %v\\\", obj)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tobjVal.Set(reflect.ValueOf(elem.Object).Elem())\\n\\t} else {\\n\\t\\tobjVal.Set(reflect.Zero(objVal.Type()))\\n\\t\\tif !opts.IgnoreNotFound {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn storage.NewKeyNotFoundError(key, int64(readResourceVersion))\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// computeListLimit determines whether the cacher should\\n// apply a limit to an incoming LIST request and returns its value.\\n//\\n// note that this function doesn't check RVM nor the Continuation token.\\n// these parameters are validated by the shouldDelegateList function.\\n//\\n// as of today, the limit is ignored for requests that set RV == 0\\nfunc computeListLimit(opts storage.ListOptions) int64 {\\n\\tif opts.Predicate.Limit <= 0 || opts.ResourceVersion == \\\"0\\\" {\\n\\t\\treturn 0\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn opts.Predicate.Limit\\n}\\n\\ntype listResp struct {\\n\\tItems           []interface{}\\n\\tResourceVersion uint64\\n}\\n\\n// GetList implements storage.Interface\\nfunc (c *Cacher) GetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts storage.ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error {\\n\\tpreparedKey, err := c.prepareKey(key, opts.Recursive)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\t_, err = c.versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, \\\"cacher.GetList\\\",\\n\\t\\tattribute.String(\\\"audit-id\\\", audit.GetAuditIDTruncated(ctx)),\\n\\t\\tattribute.Stringer(\\\"type\\\", c.groupResource))\\n\\tdefer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)\\n\\n\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ResilientWatchCacheInitialization) {\\n\\t\\tif downtime, err := c.ready.check(); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t// If Cacher is not initialized, reject List requests\\n\\t\\t\\t// as described in https://kep.k8s.io/4568\\n\\t\\t\\treturn errors.NewTooManyRequests(err.Error(), calculateRetryAfterForUnreadyCache(downtime))\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t} else {\\n\\t\\tif err := c.ready.wait(ctx); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn errors.NewServiceUnavailable(err.Error())\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Ready\\\")\\n\\n\\t// List elements with at least 'listRV' from cache.\\n\\tlistPtr, err := meta.GetItemsPtr(listObj)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tlistVal, err := conversion.EnforcePtr(listPtr)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tif listVal.Kind() != reflect.Slice {\\n\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"need a pointer to slice, got %v\\\", listVal.Kind())\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tresp, indexUsed, err := c.watchCache.WaitUntilFreshAndGetList(ctx, preparedKey, opts)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Listed items from cache\\\", attribute.Int(\\\"count\\\", len(resp.Items)))\\n\\t// store pointer of eligible objects,\\n\\t// Why not directly put object in the items of listObj?\\n\\t//   the elements in ListObject are Struct type, making slice will bring excessive memory consumption.\\n\\t//   so we try to delay this action as much as possible\\n\\tvar selectedObjects []runtime.Object\\n\\tvar lastSelectedObjectKey string\\n\\tvar hasMoreListItems bool\\n\\tlimit := computeListLimit(opts)\\n\\tfor i, obj := range resp.Items {\\n\\t\\telem, ok := obj.(*store.Element)\\n\\t\\tif !ok {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"non *store.Element returned from storage: %v\\\", obj)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif opts.Predicate.MatchesObjectAttributes(elem.Labels, elem.Fields) {\\n\\t\\t\\tselectedObjects = append(selectedObjects, elem.Object)\\n\\t\\t\\tlastSelectedObjectKey = elem.Key\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif limit > 0 && int64(len(selectedObjects)) >= limit {\\n\\t\\t\\thasMoreListItems = i < len(resp.Items)-1\\n\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\tif len(selectedObjects) == 0 {\\n\\t\\t// Ensure that we never return a nil Items pointer in the result for consistency.\\n\\t\\tlistVal.Set(reflect.MakeSlice(listVal.Type(), 0, 0))\\n\\t} else {\\n\\t\\t// Resize the slice appropriately, since we already know that size of result set\\n\\t\\tlistVal.Set(reflect.MakeSlice(listVal.Type(), len(selectedObjects), len(selectedObjects)))\\n\\t\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Resized result\\\")\\n\\t\\tfor i, o := range selectedObjects {\\n\\t\\t\\tlistVal.Index(i).Set(reflect.ValueOf(o).Elem())\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\tspan.AddEvent(\\\"Filtered items\\\", attribute.Int(\\\"count\\\", listVal.Len()))\\n\\tif c.versioner != nil {\\n\\t\\tcontinueValue, remainingItemCount, err := storage.PrepareContinueToken(lastSelectedObjectKey, key, int64(resp.ResourceVersion), int64(len(resp.Items)), hasMoreListItems, opts)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\tif err = c.versioner.UpdateList(listObj, resp.ResourceVersion, continueValue, remainingItemCount); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\tmetrics.RecordListCacheMetrics(c.groupResource, indexUsed, len(resp.Items), listVal.Len())\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// baseObjectThreadUnsafe omits locking for cachingObject.\\nfunc baseObjectThreadUnsafe(object runtime.Object) runtime.Object {\\n\\tif co, ok := object.(*cachingObject); ok {\\n\\t\\treturn co.object\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn object\\n}\\n\\nfunc (c *Cacher) triggerValuesThreadUnsafe(event *watchCacheEvent) ([]string, bool) {\\n\\tif c.indexedTrigger == nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, false\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tresult := make([]string, 0, 2)\\n\\tresult = append(result, c.indexedTrigger.indexerFunc(baseObjectThreadUnsafe(event.Object)))\\n\\tif event.PrevObject == nil {\\n\\t\\treturn result, true\\n\\t}\\n\\tprevTriggerValue := c.indexedTrigger.indexerFunc(baseObjectThreadUnsafe(event.PrevObject))\\n\\tif result[0] != prevTriggerValue {\\n\\t\\tresult = append(result, prevTriggerValue)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn result, true\\n}\\n\\nfunc (c *Cacher) processEvent(event *watchCacheEvent) {\\n\\tif curLen := int64(len(c.incoming)); c.incomingHWM.Update(curLen) {\\n\\t\\t// Monitor if this gets backed up, and how much.\\n\\t\\tklog.V(1).Infof(\\\"cacher (%v): %v objects queued in incoming channel.\\\", c.groupResource.String(), curLen)\\n\\t}\\n\\tc.incoming <- *event\\n}\\n\\nfunc (c *Cacher) dispatchEvents() {\\n\\t// Jitter to help level out any aggregate load.\\n\\tbookmarkTimer := c.clock.NewTimer(wait.Jitter(time.Second, 0.25))\\n\\tdefer bookmarkTimer.Stop()\\n\\n\\t// The internal informer populates the RV as soon as it conducts\\n\\t// The first successful sync with the underlying store.\\n\\t// The cache must wait until this first sync is completed to be deemed ready.\\n\\t// Since we cannot send a bookmark when the lastProcessedResourceVersion is 0,\\n\\t// we poll aggressively for the first list RV before entering the dispatch loop.\\n\\tlastProcessedResourceVersion := uint64(0)\\n\\tif err := wait.PollUntilContextCancel(wait.ContextForChannel(c.stopCh), 10*time.Millisecond, true, func(_ context.Context) (bool, error) {\\n\\t\\tif rv := c.watchCache.getListResourceVersion(); rv != 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\tlastProcessedResourceVersion = rv\\n\\t\\t\\treturn true, nil\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\treturn false, nil\\n\\t}); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t// given the function above never returns error,\\n\\t\\t// the non-empty error means that the stopCh was closed\\n\\t\\treturn\\n\\t}\\n\\tfor {\\n\\t\\tselect {\\n\\t\\tcase event, ok := <-c.incoming:\\n\\t\\t\\tif !ok {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t// Don't dispatch bookmarks coming from the storage layer.\\n\\t\\t\\t// They can be very frequent (even to the level of subseconds)\\n\\t\\t\\t// to allow efficient watch resumption on kube-apiserver restarts,\\n\\t\\t\\t// and propagating them down may overload the whole system.\\n\\t\\t\\t//\\n\\t\\t\\t// TODO: If at some point we decide the performance and scalability\\n\\t\\t\\t// footprint is acceptable, this is the place to hook them in.\\n\\t\\t\\t// However, we then need to check if this was called as a result\\n\\t\\t\\t// of a bookmark event or regular Add/Update/Delete operation by\\n\\t\\t\\t// checking if resourceVersion here has changed.\\n\\t\\t\\tif event.Type != watch.Bookmark {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tc.dispatchEvent(&event)\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"27741f1e-dfd2-4d5d-aba4-c174677b9d15\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:17.029Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"7d89e9bd-38f4-457d-89a8-45df9f63a493\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"27741f1e-dfd2-4d5d-aba4-c174677b9d15\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01NJ7fDBaiMQZ4d4nZ3QMCFX\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now let me read the watch_cache and other critical sections:\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4728,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":73961,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4728},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2zmhv965feXwPbWkMD\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"69cede8a-ab1b-4580-808f-ad86ae183311\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:18.777Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"69cede8a-ab1b-4580-808f-ad86ae183311\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01NJ7fDBaiMQZ4d4nZ3QMCFX\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01TBLpvDtBYwcJ9qEEoDXrHb\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"head -350 /tmp/watch_cache.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4728,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":73961,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4728},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2zmhv965feXwPbWkMD\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"8dc512bc-73e5-49d8-b218-336cf097a5a4\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:18.997Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"8dc512bc-73e5-49d8-b218-336cf097a5a4\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01TBLpvDtBYwcJ9qEEoDXrHb\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage cacher\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"math\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sync/atomic\\\"\\n\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/delegator\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/metrics\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/progress\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/store\\\"\\n\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/clock\\\"\\n)\\n\\nconst (\\n\\t// blockTimeout determines how long we're willing to block the request\\n\\t// to wait for a given resource version to be propagated to cache,\\n\\t// before terminating request and returning Timeout error with retry\\n\\t// after suggestion.\\n\\tblockTimeout = 3 * time.Second\\n\\n\\t// resourceVersionTooHighRetrySeconds is the seconds before a operation should be retried by the client\\n\\t// after receiving a 'too high resource version' error.\\n\\tresourceVersionTooHighRetrySeconds = 1\\n\\n\\t// defaultLowerBoundCapacity is a default value for event cache capacity's lower bound.\\n\\t// TODO: Figure out, to what value we can decreased it.\\n\\tdefaultLowerBoundCapacity = 100\\n\\n\\t// defaultUpperBoundCapacity should be able to keep the required history.\\n\\tdefaultUpperBoundCapacity = 100 * 1024\\n)\\n\\n// watchCacheEvent is a single \\\"watch event\\\" that is send to users of\\n// watchCache. Additionally to a typical \\\"watch.Event\\\" it contains\\n// the previous value of the object to enable proper filtering in the\\n// upper layers.\\ntype watchCacheEvent struct {\\n\\tType            watch.EventType\\n\\tObject          runtime.Object\\n\\tObjLabels       labels.Set\\n\\tObjFields       fields.Set\\n\\tPrevObject      runtime.Object\\n\\tPrevObjLabels   labels.Set\\n\\tPrevObjFields   fields.Set\\n\\tKey             string\\n\\tResourceVersion uint64\\n\\tRecordTime      time.Time\\n}\\n\\n// watchCache implements a Store interface.\\n// However, it depends on the elements implementing runtime.Object interface.\\n//\\n// watchCache is a \\\"sliding window\\\" (with a limited capacity) of objects\\n// observed from a watch.\\ntype watchCache struct {\\n\\tsync.RWMutex\\n\\n\\t// Condition on which lists are waiting for the fresh enough\\n\\t// resource version.\\n\\tcond *sync.Cond\\n\\n\\t// Maximum size of history window.\\n\\tcapacity int\\n\\n\\t// upper bound of capacity since event cache has a dynamic size.\\n\\tupperBoundCapacity int\\n\\n\\t// lower bound of capacity since event cache has a dynamic size.\\n\\tlowerBoundCapacity int\\n\\n\\t// keyFunc is used to get a key in the underlying storage for a given object.\\n\\tkeyFunc func(runtime.Object) (string, error)\\n\\n\\t// getAttrsFunc is used to get labels and fields of an object.\\n\\tgetAttrsFunc func(runtime.Object) (labels.Set, fields.Set, error)\\n\\n\\t// cache is used a cyclic buffer - the \\\"current\\\" contents of it are\\n\\t// stored in [start_index%capacity, end_index%capacity) - so the\\n\\t// \\\"current\\\" contents have exactly end_index-start_index items.\\n\\tcache      []*watchCacheEvent\\n\\tstartIndex int\\n\\tendIndex   int\\n\\t// removedEventSinceRelist holds the information whether any of the events\\n\\t// were already removed from the `cache` cyclic buffer since the last relist\\n\\tremovedEventSinceRelist bool\\n\\n\\t// store will effectively support LIST operation from the \\\"end of cache\\n\\t// history\\\" i.e. from the moment just after the newest cached watched event.\\n\\t// It is necessary to effectively allow clients to start watching at now.\\n\\t// NOTE: We assume that <store> is thread-safe.\\n\\tstore store.Indexer\\n\\n\\t// ResourceVersion up to which the watchCache is propagated.\\n\\tresourceVersion uint64\\n\\n\\t// ResourceVersion of the last list result (populated via Replace() method).\\n\\tlistResourceVersion uint64\\n\\n\\t// This handler is run at the end of every successful Replace() method.\\n\\tonReplace func()\\n\\n\\t// This handler is run at the end of every Add/Update/Delete method\\n\\t// and additionally gets the previous value of the object.\\n\\teventHandler func(*watchCacheEvent)\\n\\n\\t// for testing timeouts.\\n\\tclock clock.Clock\\n\\n\\t// eventFreshDuration defines the minimum watch history watchcache will store.\\n\\teventFreshDuration time.Duration\\n\\n\\t// An underlying storage.Versioner.\\n\\tversioner storage.Versioner\\n\\n\\t// cacher's group resource\\n\\tgroupResource schema.GroupResource\\n\\n\\t// For testing cache interval invalidation.\\n\\tindexValidator indexValidator\\n\\n\\t// Requests progress notification if there are requests waiting for watch\\n\\t// to be fresh\\n\\twaitingUntilFresh *progress.ConditionalProgressRequester\\n\\n\\t// Stores previous snapshots of orderedLister to allow serving requests from previous revisions.\\n\\tsnapshots           store.Snapshotter\\n\\tsnapshottingEnabled atomic.Bool\\n\\n\\tgetCurrentRV func(context.Context) (uint64, error)\\n}\\n\\nfunc newWatchCache(\\n\\tkeyFunc func(runtime.Object) (string, error),\\n\\teventHandler func(*watchCacheEvent),\\n\\tgetAttrsFunc func(runtime.Object) (labels.Set, fields.Set, error),\\n\\tversioner storage.Versioner,\\n\\tindexers *cache.Indexers,\\n\\tclock clock.WithTicker,\\n\\teventFreshDuration time.Duration,\\n\\tgroupResource schema.GroupResource,\\n\\tprogressRequester *progress.ConditionalProgressRequester,\\n\\tgetCurrentRV func(context.Context) (uint64, error),\\n) *watchCache {\\n\\twc := &watchCache{\\n\\t\\tcapacity:            defaultLowerBoundCapacity,\\n\\t\\tkeyFunc:             keyFunc,\\n\\t\\tgetAttrsFunc:        getAttrsFunc,\\n\\t\\tcache:               make([]*watchCacheEvent, defaultLowerBoundCapacity),\\n\\t\\tlowerBoundCapacity:  defaultLowerBoundCapacity,\\n\\t\\tupperBoundCapacity:  capacityUpperBound(eventFreshDuration),\\n\\t\\tstartIndex:          0,\\n\\t\\tendIndex:            0,\\n\\t\\tstore:               store.NewIndexer(indexers),\\n\\t\\tresourceVersion:     0,\\n\\t\\tlistResourceVersion: 0,\\n\\t\\teventHandler:        eventHandler,\\n\\t\\tclock:               clock,\\n\\t\\teventFreshDuration:  eventFreshDuration,\\n\\t\\tversioner:           versioner,\\n\\t\\tgroupResource:       groupResource,\\n\\t\\twaitingUntilFresh:   progressRequester,\\n\\t\\tgetCurrentRV:        getCurrentRV,\\n\\t}\\n\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ListFromCacheSnapshot) {\\n\\t\\twc.snapshottingEnabled.Store(true)\\n\\t\\twc.snapshots = store.NewSnapshotter()\\n\\t}\\n\\tmetrics.WatchCacheCapacity.WithLabelValues(groupResource.Group, groupResource.Resource).Set(float64(wc.capacity))\\n\\twc.cond = sync.NewCond(wc.RLocker())\\n\\twc.indexValidator = wc.isIndexValidLocked\\n\\n\\treturn wc\\n}\\n\\n// capacityUpperBound denotes the maximum possible capacity of the watch cache\\n// to which it can resize.\\nfunc capacityUpperBound(eventFreshDuration time.Duration) int {\\n\\tif eventFreshDuration <= DefaultEventFreshDuration {\\n\\t\\treturn defaultUpperBoundCapacity\\n\\t}\\n\\t// eventFreshDuration determines how long the watch events are supposed\\n\\t// to be stored in the watch cache.\\n\\t// In very high churn situations, there is a need to store more events\\n\\t// in the watch cache, hence it would have to be upsized accordingly.\\n\\t// Because of that, for larger values of eventFreshDuration, we set the\\n\\t// upper bound of the watch cache's capacity proportionally to the ratio\\n\\t// between eventFreshDuration and DefaultEventFreshDuration.\\n\\t// Given that the watch cache size can only double, we round up that\\n\\t// proportion to the next power of two.\\n\\texponent := int(math.Ceil((math.Log2(eventFreshDuration.Seconds() / DefaultEventFreshDuration.Seconds()))))\\n\\tif maxExponent := int(math.Floor((math.Log2(math.MaxInt32 / defaultUpperBoundCapacity)))); exponent > maxExponent {\\n\\t\\t// Making sure that the capacity's upper bound fits in a 32-bit integer.\\n\\t\\texponent = maxExponent\\n\\t\\tklog.Warningf(\\\"Capping watch cache capacity upper bound to %v\\\", defaultUpperBoundCapacity<<exponent)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn defaultUpperBoundCapacity << exponent\\n}\\n\\n// Add takes runtime.Object as an argument.\\nfunc (w *watchCache) Add(obj interface{}) error {\\n\\tobject, resourceVersion, err := w.objectToVersionedRuntimeObject(obj)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tevent := watch.Event{Type: watch.Added, Object: object}\\n\\n\\tf := func(elem *store.Element) error { return w.store.Add(elem) }\\n\\treturn w.processEvent(event, resourceVersion, f)\\n}\\n\\n// Update takes runtime.Object as an argument.\\nfunc (w *watchCache) Update(obj interface{}) error {\\n\\tobject, resourceVersion, err := w.objectToVersionedRuntimeObject(obj)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tevent := watch.Event{Type: watch.Modified, Object: object}\\n\\n\\tf := func(elem *store.Element) error { return w.store.Update(elem) }\\n\\treturn w.processEvent(event, resourceVersion, f)\\n}\\n\\n// Delete takes runtime.Object as an argument.\\nfunc (w *watchCache) Delete(obj interface{}) error {\\n\\tobject, resourceVersion, err := w.objectToVersionedRuntimeObject(obj)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tevent := watch.Event{Type: watch.Deleted, Object: object}\\n\\n\\tf := func(elem *store.Element) error { return w.store.Delete(elem) }\\n\\treturn w.processEvent(event, resourceVersion, f)\\n}\\n\\nfunc (w *watchCache) objectToVersionedRuntimeObject(obj interface{}) (runtime.Object, uint64, error) {\\n\\tobject, ok := obj.(runtime.Object)\\n\\tif !ok {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, 0, fmt.Errorf(\\\"obj does not implement runtime.Object interface: %v\\\", obj)\\n\\t}\\n\\tresourceVersion, err := w.versioner.ObjectResourceVersion(object)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, 0, err\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn object, resourceVersion, nil\\n}\\n\\n// processEvent is safe as long as there is at most one call to it in flight\\n// at any point in time.\\nfunc (w *watchCache) processEvent(event watch.Event, resourceVersion uint64, updateFunc func(*store.Element) error) error {\\n\\tmetrics.EventsReceivedCounter.WithLabelValues(w.groupResource.Group, w.groupResource.Resource).Inc()\\n\\n\\tkey, err := w.keyFunc(event.Object)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"couldn't compute key: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\telem := &store.Element{Key: key, Object: event.Object}\\n\\telem.Labels, elem.Fields, err = w.getAttrsFunc(event.Object)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\twcEvent := &watchCacheEvent{\\n\\t\\tType:            event.Type,\\n\\t\\tObject:          elem.Object,\\n\\t\\tObjLabels:       elem.Labels,\\n\\t\\tObjFields:       elem.Fields,\\n\\t\\tKey:             key,\\n\\t\\tResourceVersion: resourceVersion,\\n\\t\\tRecordTime:      w.clock.Now(),\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// We can call w.store.Get() outside of a critical section,\\n\\t// because the w.store itself is thread-safe and the only\\n\\t// place where w.store is modified is below (via updateFunc)\\n\\t// and these calls are serialized because reflector is processing\\n\\t// events one-by-one.\\n\\tprevious, exists, err := w.store.Get(elem)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\tif exists {\\n\\t\\tpreviousElem := previous.(*store.Element)\\n\\t\\twcEvent.PrevObject = previousElem.Object\\n\\t\\twcEvent.PrevObjLabels = previousElem.Labels\\n\\t\\twcEvent.PrevObjFields = previousElem.Fields\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif err := func() error {\\n\\t\\tw.Lock()\\n\\t\\tdefer w.Unlock()\\n\\n\\t\\tw.updateCache(wcEvent)\\n\\t\\tw.resourceVersion = resourceVersion\\n\\t\\tdefer w.cond.Broadcast()\\n\\n\\t\\terr := updateFunc(elem)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif w.snapshots != nil && w.snapshottingEnabled.Load() {\\n\\t\\t\\tif orderedLister, ordered := w.store.(store.OrderedLister); ordered {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tif w.isCacheFullLocked() {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\toldestRV := w.cache[w.startIndex%w.capacity].ResourceVersion\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tw.snapshots.RemoveLess(oldestRV)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tw.snapshots.Add(w.resourceVersion, orderedLister)\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}(); err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// Avoid calling event handler under lock.\\n\\t// This is safe as long as there is at most one call to Add/Update/Delete and\\n\\t// UpdateResourceVersion in flight at any point in time, which is true now,\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"30b264d1-a6cb-40f1-9a67-795204b02715\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:19.009Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"8dc512bc-73e5-49d8-b218-336cf097a5a4\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"30b264d1-a6cb-40f1-9a67-795204b02715\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01Me36fus2F6AtsM2P9VKHq4\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01R8b69fCp8rP2DVvZ8J7uiD\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"sed -n '1,150p' /tmp/storage_decorator.go && echo \\\"---FULL FILE---\\\" && wc -l /tmp/storage_decorator.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":3830,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":78689,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":3830},\"output_tokens\":26,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2zur4Q2kVt8UkFtA3j\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"83e608e4-8c32-47ec-95c9-f7dd2538d1e8\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:20.329Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"83e608e4-8c32-47ec-95c9-f7dd2538d1e8\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01R8b69fCp8rP2DVvZ8J7uiD\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage generic\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/storagebackend\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/storagebackend/factory\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// StorageDecorator is a function signature for producing a storage.Interface\\n// and an associated DestroyFunc from given parameters.\\ntype StorageDecorator func(\\n\\tconfig *storagebackend.ConfigForResource,\\n\\tresourcePrefix string,\\n\\tkeyFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error),\\n\\tnewFunc func() runtime.Object,\\n\\tnewListFunc func() runtime.Object,\\n\\tgetAttrsFunc storage.AttrFunc,\\n\\ttrigger storage.IndexerFuncs,\\n\\tindexers *cache.Indexers) (storage.Interface, factory.DestroyFunc, error)\\n\\n// UndecoratedStorage returns the given a new storage from the given config\\n// without any decoration.\\nfunc UndecoratedStorage(\\n\\tconfig *storagebackend.ConfigForResource,\\n\\tresourcePrefix string,\\n\\tkeyFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error),\\n\\tnewFunc func() runtime.Object,\\n\\tnewListFunc func() runtime.Object,\\n\\tgetAttrsFunc storage.AttrFunc,\\n\\ttrigger storage.IndexerFuncs,\\n\\tindexers *cache.Indexers) (storage.Interface, factory.DestroyFunc, error) {\\n\\treturn NewRawStorage(config, newFunc, newListFunc, resourcePrefix)\\n}\\n\\n// NewRawStorage creates the low level kv storage. This is a work-around for current\\n// two layer of same storage interface.\\n// TODO: Once cacher is enabled on all registries (event registry is special), we will remove this method.\\nfunc NewRawStorage(config *storagebackend.ConfigForResource, newFunc, newListFunc func() runtime.Object, resourcePrefix string) (storage.Interface, factory.DestroyFunc, error) {\\n\\treturn factory.Create(*config, newFunc, newListFunc, resourcePrefix)\\n}\\n---FULL FILE---\\n58 /tmp/storage_decorator.go\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"be238246-7ea7-48c0-851d-6e7c37a5b6b8\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:20.364Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"83e608e4-8c32-47ec-95c9-f7dd2538d1e8\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"be238246-7ea7-48c0-851d-6e7c37a5b6b8\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_0158GWJeEgwhEBtXLLLp5hap\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01U5UM3HPyVTQcciN1yEubJy\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"sed -n '400,800p' /tmp/store_generic.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":818,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":82519,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":818},\"output_tokens\":25,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG311zQGMq1jXMCrN4SR\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"6a7a0b19-4552-4437-a73a-901ed4a3b815\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:21.568Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"6a7a0b19-4552-4437-a73a-901ed4a3b815\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01U5UM3HPyVTQcciN1yEubJy\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"\\t\\tResourceVersionMatch: options.ResourceVersionMatch,\\n\\t\\tPredicate:            p,\\n\\t\\tRecursive:            true,\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// if we're not already namespace-scoped, see if the field selector narrows the scope of the watch\\n\\tif requestNamespace, _ := genericapirequest.NamespaceFrom(ctx); len(requestNamespace) == 0 {\\n\\t\\tif selectorNamespace, ok := p.MatchesSingleNamespace(); ok {\\n\\t\\t\\tif len(validation.ValidateNamespaceName(selectorNamespace, false)) == 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tctx = genericapirequest.WithNamespace(ctx, selectorNamespace)\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif name, ok := p.MatchesSingle(); ok {\\n\\t\\tif key, err := e.KeyFunc(ctx, name); err == nil {\\n\\t\\t\\tstorageOpts.Recursive = false\\n\\t\\t\\terr := e.Storage.GetList(ctx, key, storageOpts, list)\\n\\t\\t\\treturn list, storeerr.InterpretListError(err, qualifiedResource)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t// if we cannot extract a key based on the current context, the optimization is skipped\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\terr := e.Storage.GetList(ctx, e.KeyRootFunc(ctx), storageOpts, list)\\n\\treturn list, storeerr.InterpretListError(err, qualifiedResource)\\n}\\n\\n// finishNothing is a do-nothing FinishFunc.\\nfunc finishNothing(context.Context, bool) {}\\n\\n// maxNameGenerationCreateAttempts is the maximum number of\\n// times create will be attempted when generateName is used\\n// and create attempts fails due to name conflict errors.\\n// Each attempt uses a newly randomly generated name.\\n// 8 was selected as the max because it is sufficient to generate\\n// 1 million names per generateName prefix, with only a 0.1%\\n// probability of any generated name conflicting with existing names.\\n// Without retry, a 0.1% probability occurs at ~500\\n// generated names and a 50% probability occurs at ~4500\\n// generated names.\\nconst maxNameGenerationCreateAttempts = 8\\n\\n// Create inserts a new item according to the unique key from the object.\\n// Note that registries may mutate the input object (e.g. in the strategy\\n// hooks).  Tests which call this might want to call DeepCopy if they expect to\\n// be able to examine the input and output objects for differences.\\nfunc (e *Store) Create(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, createValidation rest.ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.RetryGenerateName) && needsNameGeneration(obj) {\\n\\t\\treturn e.createWithGenerateNameRetry(ctx, obj, createValidation, options)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\treturn e.create(ctx, obj, createValidation, options)\\n}\\n\\n// needsNameGeneration returns true if the obj has a generateName but no name.\\nfunc needsNameGeneration(obj runtime.Object) bool {\\n\\tif objectMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj); err == nil {\\n\\t\\tif len(objectMeta.GetGenerateName()) > 0 && len(objectMeta.GetName()) == 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn false\\n}\\n\\n// createWithGenerateNameRetry attempts to create obj up to maxNameGenerationCreateAttempts\\n// when create fails due to a name conflict error. Each attempt randomly generates a new\\n// name based on generateName.\\nfunc (e *Store) createWithGenerateNameRetry(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, createValidation rest.ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (resultObj runtime.Object, err error) {\\n\\tfor i := 0; i < maxNameGenerationCreateAttempts; i++ {\\n\\t\\tresultObj, err = e.create(ctx, obj.DeepCopyObject(), createValidation, options)\\n\\t\\tif err == nil || !apierrors.IsAlreadyExists(err) {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn resultObj, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn resultObj, err\\n}\\n\\nfunc (e *Store) create(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, createValidation rest.ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error) {\\n\\tvar finishCreate FinishFunc = finishNothing\\n\\n\\t// Init metadata as early as possible.\\n\\tif objectMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj); err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t} else {\\n\\t\\trest.FillObjectMetaSystemFields(objectMeta)\\n\\t\\tif len(objectMeta.GetGenerateName()) > 0 && len(objectMeta.GetName()) == 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\tobjectMeta.SetName(e.CreateStrategy.GenerateName(objectMeta.GetGenerateName()))\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif e.BeginCreate != nil {\\n\\t\\tfn, err := e.BeginCreate(ctx, obj, options)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tfinishCreate = fn\\n\\t\\tdefer func() {\\n\\t\\t\\tfinishCreate(ctx, false)\\n\\t\\t}()\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif err := rest.BeforeCreate(e.CreateStrategy, ctx, obj); err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t}\\n\\t// at this point we have a fully formed object.  It is time to call the validators that the apiserver\\n\\t// handling chain wants to enforce.\\n\\tif createValidation != nil {\\n\\t\\tif err := createValidation(ctx, obj.DeepCopyObject()); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tname, err := e.ObjectNameFunc(obj)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t}\\n\\tkey, err := e.KeyFunc(ctx, name)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t}\\n\\tqualifiedResource := e.qualifiedResourceFromContext(ctx)\\n\\tttl, err := e.calculateTTL(obj, 0, false)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t}\\n\\tout := e.NewFunc()\\n\\tif err := e.Storage.Create(ctx, key, obj, out, ttl, dryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun)); err != nil {\\n\\t\\terr = storeerr.InterpretCreateError(err, qualifiedResource, name)\\n\\t\\terr = rest.CheckGeneratedNameError(ctx, e.CreateStrategy, err, obj)\\n\\t\\tif !apierrors.IsAlreadyExists(err) {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif errGet := e.Storage.Get(ctx, key, storage.GetOptions{}, out); errGet != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\taccessor, errGetAcc := meta.Accessor(out)\\n\\t\\tif errGetAcc != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif accessor.GetDeletionTimestamp() != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\tmsg := &err.(*apierrors.StatusError).ErrStatus.Message\\n\\t\\t\\t*msg = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"object is being deleted: %s\\\", *msg)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t}\\n\\t// The operation has succeeded.  Call the finish function if there is one,\\n\\t// and then make sure the defer doesn't call it again.\\n\\tfn := finishCreate\\n\\tfinishCreate = finishNothing\\n\\tfn(ctx, true)\\n\\n\\tif e.AfterCreate != nil {\\n\\t\\te.AfterCreate(out, options)\\n\\t}\\n\\tif e.Decorator != nil {\\n\\t\\te.Decorator(out)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn out, nil\\n}\\n\\n// ShouldDeleteDuringUpdate is the default function for\\n// checking if an object should be deleted during an update.\\n// It checks if the new object has no finalizers,\\n// the existing object's deletionTimestamp is set, and\\n// the existing object's deletionGracePeriodSeconds is 0 or nil\\nfunc ShouldDeleteDuringUpdate(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, existing runtime.Object) bool {\\n\\tnewMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(err)\\n\\t\\treturn false\\n\\t}\\n\\toldMeta, err := meta.Accessor(existing)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(err)\\n\\t\\treturn false\\n\\t}\\n\\tif len(newMeta.GetFinalizers()) > 0 {\\n\\t\\t// don't delete with finalizers remaining in the new object\\n\\t\\treturn false\\n\\t}\\n\\tif oldMeta.GetDeletionTimestamp() == nil {\\n\\t\\t// don't delete if the existing object hasn't had a delete request made\\n\\t\\treturn false\\n\\t}\\n\\t// delete if the existing object has no grace period or a grace period of 0\\n\\treturn oldMeta.GetDeletionGracePeriodSeconds() == nil || *oldMeta.GetDeletionGracePeriodSeconds() == 0\\n}\\n\\n// deleteWithoutFinalizers handles deleting an object ignoring its finalizer list.\\n// Used for objects that are either been finalized or have never initialized.\\nfunc (e *Store) deleteWithoutFinalizers(ctx context.Context, name, key string, obj runtime.Object, preconditions *storage.Preconditions, options *metav1.DeleteOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error) {\\n\\tout := e.NewFunc()\\n\\tklog.V(6).InfoS(\\\"Going to delete object from registry, triggered by update\\\", \\\"object\\\", klog.KRef(genericapirequest.NamespaceValue(ctx), name))\\n\\t// Using the rest.ValidateAllObjectFunc because the request is an UPDATE request and has already passed the admission for the UPDATE verb.\\n\\tif err := e.Storage.Delete(ctx, key, out, preconditions, rest.ValidateAllObjectFunc, dryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun), nil, storage.DeleteOptions{}); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t// Deletion is racy, i.e., there could be multiple update\\n\\t\\t// requests to remove all finalizers from the object, so we\\n\\t\\t// ignore the NotFound error.\\n\\t\\tif storage.IsNotFound(err) {\\n\\t\\t\\t_, err := e.finalizeDelete(ctx, obj, true, options)\\n\\t\\t\\t// clients are expecting an updated object if a PUT succeeded,\\n\\t\\t\\t// but finalizeDelete returns a metav1.Status, so return\\n\\t\\t\\t// the object in the request instead.\\n\\t\\t\\treturn obj, false, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\treturn nil, false, storeerr.InterpretDeleteError(err, e.qualifiedResourceFromContext(ctx), name)\\n\\t}\\n\\t_, err := e.finalizeDelete(ctx, out, true, options)\\n\\t// clients are expecting an updated object if a PUT succeeded, but\\n\\t// finalizeDelete returns a metav1.Status, so return the object in\\n\\t// the request instead.\\n\\treturn obj, false, err\\n}\\n\\n// Update performs an atomic update and set of the object. Returns the result of the update\\n// or an error. If the registry allows create-on-update, the create flow will be executed.\\n// A bool is returned along with the object and any errors, to indicate object creation.\\nfunc (e *Store) Update(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo rest.UpdatedObjectInfo, createValidation rest.ValidateObjectFunc, updateValidation rest.ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error) {\\n\\tkey, err := e.KeyFunc(ctx, name)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, false, err\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tvar (\\n\\t\\tcreatingObj runtime.Object\\n\\t\\tcreating    = false\\n\\t)\\n\\n\\tqualifiedResource := e.qualifiedResourceFromContext(ctx)\\n\\tstoragePreconditions := &storage.Preconditions{}\\n\\tif preconditions := objInfo.Preconditions(); preconditions != nil {\\n\\t\\tstoragePreconditions.UID = preconditions.UID\\n\\t\\tstoragePreconditions.ResourceVersion = preconditions.ResourceVersion\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tout := e.NewFunc()\\n\\n\\t// only ignore a not found error if this type allows creating on update, or we're forcing allowing create (like for server-side-apply)\\n\\tignoreNotFound := e.UpdateStrategy.AllowCreateOnUpdate() || forceAllowCreate\\n\\t// deleteObj is only used in case a deletion is carried out\\n\\tvar deleteObj runtime.Object\\n\\terr = e.Storage.GuaranteedUpdate(ctx, key, out, ignoreNotFound, storagePreconditions, func(existing runtime.Object, res storage.ResponseMeta) (runtime.Object, *uint64, error) {\\n\\t\\texistingResourceVersion, err := e.Storage.Versioner().ObjectResourceVersion(existing)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif existingResourceVersion == 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\tif !e.UpdateStrategy.AllowCreateOnUpdate() && !forceAllowCreate {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, apierrors.NewNotFound(qualifiedResource, name)\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t// Given the existing object, get the new object\\n\\t\\tobj, err := objInfo.UpdatedObject(ctx, existing)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t// If AllowUnconditionalUpdate() is true and the object specified by\\n\\t\\t// the user does not have a resource version, then we populate it with\\n\\t\\t// the latest version. Else, we check that the version specified by\\n\\t\\t// the user matches the version of latest storage object.\\n\\t\\tnewResourceVersion, err := e.Storage.Versioner().ObjectResourceVersion(obj)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tdoUnconditionalUpdate := newResourceVersion == 0 && e.UpdateStrategy.AllowUnconditionalUpdate()\\n\\n\\t\\tif existingResourceVersion == 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\t// Init metadata as early as possible.\\n\\t\\t\\tif objectMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\trest.FillObjectMetaSystemFields(objectMeta)\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t\\tvar finishCreate FinishFunc = finishNothing\\n\\n\\t\\t\\tif e.BeginCreate != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tfn, err := e.BeginCreate(ctx, obj, newCreateOptionsFromUpdateOptions(options))\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tfinishCreate = fn\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tdefer func() {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfinishCreate(ctx, false)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}()\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t\\tcreating = true\\n\\t\\t\\tcreatingObj = obj\\n\\t\\t\\tif err := rest.BeforeCreate(e.CreateStrategy, ctx, obj); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t// at this point we have a fully formed object.  It is time to call the validators that the apiserver\\n\\t\\t\\t// handling chain wants to enforce.\\n\\t\\t\\tif createValidation != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tif err := createValidation(ctx, obj.DeepCopyObject()); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\tttl, err := e.calculateTTL(obj, 0, false)\\n\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t\\t// The operation has succeeded.  Call the finish function if there is one,\\n\\t\\t\\t// and then make sure the defer doesn't call it again.\\n\\t\\t\\tfn := finishCreate\\n\\t\\t\\tfinishCreate = finishNothing\\n\\t\\t\\tfn(ctx, true)\\n\\n\\t\\t\\treturn obj, &ttl, nil\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\tcreating = false\\n\\t\\tcreatingObj = nil\\n\\t\\tif doUnconditionalUpdate {\\n\\t\\t\\t// Update the object's resource version to match the latest\\n\\t\\t\\t// storage object's resource version.\\n\\t\\t\\terr = e.Storage.Versioner().UpdateObject(obj, res.ResourceVersion)\\n\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t} else {\\n\\t\\t\\t// Check if the object's resource version matches the latest\\n\\t\\t\\t// resource version.\\n\\t\\t\\tif newResourceVersion == 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// TODO: The Invalid error should have a field for Resource.\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// After that field is added, we should fill the Resource and\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// leave the Kind field empty. See the discussion in #18526.\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tqualifiedKind := schema.GroupKind{Group: qualifiedResource.Group, Kind: qualifiedResource.Resource}\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tfieldErrList := field.ErrorList{field.Invalid(field.NewPath(\\\"metadata\\\").Child(\\\"resourceVersion\\\"), newResourceVersion, \\\"must be specified for an update\\\")}\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, apierrors.NewInvalid(qualifiedKind, name, fieldErrList)\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\tif newResourceVersion != existingResourceVersion {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, apierrors.NewConflict(qualifiedResource, name, fmt.Errorf(OptimisticLockErrorMsg))\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\tvar finishUpdate FinishFunc = finishNothing\\n\\n\\t\\tif e.BeginUpdate != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\tfn, err := e.BeginUpdate(ctx, obj, existing, options)\\n\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\tfinishUpdate = fn\\n\\t\\t\\tdefer func() {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tfinishUpdate(ctx, false)\\n\\t\\t\\t}()\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\tif err := rest.BeforeUpdate(e.UpdateStrategy, ctx, obj, existing); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t// Ignore changes that only affect managed fields timestamps.\\n\\t\\t// FieldManager can't know about changes like normalized fields, defaulted\\n\\t\\t// fields and other mutations.\\n\\t\\tobj, err = fieldmanager.IgnoreManagedFieldsTimestampsTransformer(ctx, obj, existing)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t// at this point we have a fully formed object.  It is time to call the validators that the apiserver\\n\\t\\t// handling chain wants to enforce.\\n\\t\\tif updateValidation != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\tif err := updateValidation(ctx, obj.DeepCopyObject(), existing.DeepCopyObject()); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t// Check the default delete-during-update conditions, and store-specific conditions if provided\\n\\t\\tif ShouldDeleteDuringUpdate(ctx, key, obj, existing) &&\\n\\t\\t\\t(e.ShouldDeleteDuringUpdate == nil || e.ShouldDeleteDuringUpdate(ctx, key, obj, existing)) {\\n\\t\\t\\tdeleteObj = obj\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, errEmptiedFinalizers\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tttl, err := e.calculateTTL(obj, res.TTL, true)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\t// The operation has succeeded.  Call the finish function if there is one,\\n\\t\\t// and then make sure the defer doesn't call it again.\\n\\t\\tfn := finishUpdate\\n\\t\\tfinishUpdate = finishNothing\\n\\t\\tfn(ctx, true)\\n\\n\\t\\tif int64(ttl) != res.TTL {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn obj, &ttl, nil\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\treturn obj, nil, nil\\n\\t}, dryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun), nil)\\n\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t// delete the object\\n\\t\\tif err == errEmptiedFinalizers {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn e.deleteWithoutFinalizers(ctx, name, key, deleteObj, storagePreconditions, newDeleteOptionsFromUpdateOptions(options))\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif creating {\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"b974e293-7290-4e82-a1c8-6b759c5569d9\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:21.582Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"6a7a0b19-4552-4437-a73a-901ed4a3b815\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"b974e293-7290-4e82-a1c8-6b759c5569d9\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01GFVGRfeTjLxQdYSRChzY4e\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now let me read more from watcher.go to understand the Watch implementation:\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4861,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":83337,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4861},\"output_tokens\":2,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG316sawwiyA6UmMHFKu\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"9ae921ca-b026-4f91-b516-0b1932eacacf\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:22.761Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"9ae921ca-b026-4f91-b516-0b1932eacacf\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01GFVGRfeTjLxQdYSRChzY4e\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_014emnWMyhZRjEPSeBm9yZBD\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"sed
