/tmp/watcher.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4861,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":83337,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4861},\"output_tokens\":2,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG316sawwiyA6UmMHFKu\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"1ddf0732-ee14-4992-9bec-2bb1d4271900\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:22.979Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"1ddf0732-ee14-4992-9bec-2bb1d4271900\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_014emnWMyhZRjEPSeBm9yZBD\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage etcd3\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n\\t\\\"sync/atomic\\\"\\n\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n\\n\\tclientv3 \\\"go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3\\\"\\n\\tgrpccodes \\\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\\\"\\n\\tgrpcstatus \\\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\\\"\\n\\n\\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/metrics\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/value\\\"\\n\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n\\tutilflowcontrol \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n)\\n\\nconst (\\n\\t// We have set a buffer in order to reduce times of context switches.\\n\\tincomingBufSize         = 100\\n\\toutgoingBufSize         = 100\\n\\tprocessEventConcurrency = 10\\n)\\n\\n// defaultWatcherMaxLimit is used to facilitate construction tests\\nvar defaultWatcherMaxLimit int64 = maxLimit\\n\\n// fatalOnDecodeError is used during testing to panic the server if watcher encounters a decoding error\\nvar fatalOnDecodeError atomic.Bool\\n\\nfunc init() {\\n\\t// check to see if we are running in a test environment\\n\\tb, _ := strconv.ParseBool(os.Getenv(\\\"KUBE_PANIC_WATCH_DECODE_ERROR\\\"))\\n\\tTestOnlySetFatalOnDecodeError(b)\\n}\\n\\n// TestOnlySetFatalOnDecodeError should only be used for cases where decode errors are expected and need to be tested. e.g. conversion webhooks.\\nfunc TestOnlySetFatalOnDecodeError(b bool) {\\n\\tfatalOnDecodeError.Store(b)\\n}\\n\\ntype watcher struct {\\n\\tclient                   *clientv3.Client\\n\\tcodec                    runtime.Codec\\n\\tnewFunc                  func() runtime.Object\\n\\tobjectType               string\\n\\tgroupResource            schema.GroupResource\\n\\tversioner                storage.Versioner\\n\\ttransformer              value.Transformer\\n\\tgetCurrentStorageRV      func(context.Context) (uint64, error)\\n\\tgetResourceSizeEstimator func() *resourceSizeEstimator\\n}\\n\\n// watchChan implements watch.Interface.\\ntype watchChan struct {\\n\\twatcher                  *watcher\\n\\tkey                      string\\n\\tinitialRev               int64\\n\\trecursive                bool\\n\\tprogressNotify           bool\\n\\tinternalPred             storage.SelectionPredicate\\n\\tctx                      context.Context\\n\\tcancel                   context.CancelFunc\\n\\tincomingEventChan        chan *event\\n\\tresultChan               chan watch.Event\\n\\tgetResourceSizeEstimator func() *resourceSizeEstimator\\n}\\n\\n// Watch watches on a key and returns a watch.Interface that transfers relevant notifications.\\n// If rev is zero, it will return the existing object(s) and then start watching from\\n// the maximum revision+1 from returned objects.\\n// If rev is non-zero, it will watch events happened after given revision.\\n// If opts.Recursive is false, it watches on given key.\\n// If opts.Recursive is true, it watches any children and directories under the key, excluding the root key itself.\\n// pred must be non-nil. Only if opts.Predicate matches the change, it will be returned.\\nfunc (w *watcher) Watch(ctx context.Context, key string, rev int64, opts storage.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error) {\\n\\tif opts.Recursive && !strings.HasSuffix(key, \\\"/\\\") {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(`recursive key needs to end with \\\"/\\\"`)\\n\\t}\\n\\tif opts.ProgressNotify && w.newFunc == nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, apierrors.NewInternalError(errors.New(\\\"progressNotify for watch is unsupported by the etcd storage because no newFunc was provided\\\"))\\n\\t}\\n\\tstartWatchRV, err := w.getStartWatchResourceVersion(ctx, rev, opts)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t}\\n\\twc := w.createWatchChan(ctx, key, startWatchRV, opts.Recursive, opts.ProgressNotify, opts.Predicate)\\n\\tgo wc.run(isInitialEventsEndBookmarkRequired(opts), areInitialEventsRequired(rev, opts))\\n\\n\\t// For etcd watch we don't have an easy way to answer whether the watch\\n\\t// has already caught up. So in the initial version (given that watchcache\\n\\t// is by default enabled for all resources but Events), we just deliver\\n\\t// the initialization signal immediately. Improving this will be explored\\n\\t// in the future.\\n\\tutilflowcontrol.WatchInitialized(ctx)\\n\\n\\treturn wc, nil\\n}\\n\\nfunc (w *watcher) createWatchChan(ctx context.Context, key string, rev int64, recursive, progressNotify bool, pred storage.SelectionPredicate) *watchChan {\\n\\twc := &watchChan{\\n\\t\\twatcher:                  w,\\n\\t\\tkey:                      key,\\n\\t\\tinitialRev:               rev,\\n\\t\\trecursive:                recursive,\\n\\t\\tprogressNotify:           progressNotify,\\n\\t\\tinternalPred:             pred,\\n\\t\\tincomingEventChan:        make(chan *event, incomingBufSize),\\n\\t\\tresultChan:               make(chan watch.Event, outgoingBufSize),\\n\\t\\tgetResourceSizeEstimator: w.getResourceSizeEstimator,\\n\\t}\\n\\tif pred.Empty() {\\n\\t\\t// The filter doesn't filter out any object.\\n\\t\\twc.internalPred = storage.Everything\\n\\t}\\n\\twc.ctx, wc.cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)\\n\\treturn wc\\n}\\n\\n// getStartWatchResourceVersion returns a ResourceVersion\\n// the watch will be started from.\\n// Depending on the input parameters the semantics of the returned ResourceVersion are:\\n//   - start at Exact (return resourceVersion)\\n//   - start at Most Recent (return an RV from etcd)\\nfunc (w *watcher) getStartWatchResourceVersion(ctx context.Context, resourceVersion int64, opts storage.ListOptions) (int64, error) {\\n\\tif resourceVersion > 0 {\\n\\t\\treturn resourceVersion, nil\\n\\t}\\n\\tif !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.WatchList) {\\n\\t\\treturn 0, nil\\n\\t}\\n\\tif opts.SendInitialEvents == nil || *opts.SendInitialEvents {\\n\\t\\t// note that when opts.SendInitialEvents=true\\n\\t\\t// we will be issuing a consistent LIST request\\n\\t\\t// against etcd followed by the special bookmark event\\n\\t\\treturn 0, nil\\n\\t}\\n\\t// at this point the clients is interested\\n\\t// only in getting a stream of events\\n\\t// starting at the MostRecent point in time (RV)\\n\\tcurrentStorageRV, err := w.getCurrentStorageRV(ctx)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn 0, err\\n\\t}\\n\\t// currentStorageRV is taken from resp.Header.Revision (int64)\\n\\t// and cast to uint64, so it is safe to do reverse\\n\\t// at some point we should unify the interface but that\\n\\t// would require changing  Versioner.UpdateList\\n\\treturn int64(currentStorageRV), nil\\n}\\n\\n// isInitialEventsEndBookmarkRequired since there is no way to directly set\\n// opts.ProgressNotify from the API and the etcd3 impl doesn't support\\n// notification for external clients we simply return initialEventsEndBookmarkRequired\\n// to only send the bookmark event after the initial list call.\\n//\\n// see: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/120348\\nfunc isInitialEventsEndBookmarkRequired(opts storage.ListOptions) bool {\\n\\tif !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.WatchList) {\\n\\t\\treturn false\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn opts.SendInitialEvents != nil && *opts.SendInitialEvents && opts.Predicate.AllowWatchBookmarks\\n}\\n\\n// areInitialEventsRequired returns true if all events from the etcd should be returned.\\nfunc areInitialEventsRequired(resourceVersion int64, opts storage.ListOptions) bool {\\n\\tif opts.SendInitialEvents == nil && resourceVersion == 0 {\\n\\t\\treturn true // legacy case\\n\\t}\\n\\tif !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.WatchList) {\\n\\t\\treturn false\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn opts.SendInitialEvents != nil && *opts.SendInitialEvents\\n}\\n\\ntype etcdError interface {\\n\\tCode() grpccodes.Code\\n\\tError() string\\n}\\n\\ntype grpcError interface {\\n\\tGRPCStatus() *grpcstatus.Status\\n}\\n\\nfunc isCancelError(err error) bool {\\n\\tif err == nil {\\n\\t\\treturn false\\n\\t}\\n\\tif err == context.Canceled {\\n\\t\\treturn true\\n\\t}\\n\\tif etcdErr, ok := err.(etcdError); ok && etcdErr.Code() == grpccodes.Canceled {\\n\\t\\treturn true\\n\\t}\\n\\tif grpcErr, ok := err.(grpcError); ok && grpcErr.GRPCStatus().Code() == grpccodes.Canceled {\\n\\t\\treturn true\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn false\\n}\\n\\nfunc (wc *watchChan) run(initialEventsEndBookmarkRequired, forceInitialEvents bool) {\\n\\twatchClosedCh := make(chan struct{})\\n\\tvar resultChanWG sync.WaitGroup\\n\\n\\tresultChanWG.Add(1)\\n\\tgo func() {\\n\\t\\tdefer resultChanWG.Done()\\n\\t\\twc.startWatching(watchClosedCh, initialEventsEndBookmarkRequired, forceInitialEvents)\\n\\t}()\\n\\twc.processEvents(&resultChanWG)\\n\\n\\tselect {\\n\\tcase <-watchClosedCh:\\n\\tcase <-wc.ctx.Done(): // user cancel\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// We use wc.ctx to reap all goroutines. Under whatever condition, we should stop them all.\\n\\t// It's fine to double cancel.\\n\\twc.cancel()\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"e6ab2bdd-2f04-45b8-8e29-7aed85d61129\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:22.992Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"1ddf0732-ee14-4992-9bec-2bb1d4271900\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"e6ab2bdd-2f04-45b8-8e29-7aed85d61129\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_018Wht9fZ6PM6KHJH3tybDfv\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document based on all these files. Let me read a few more key -n '200,400p' /tmp/rest.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":3009,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":88198,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":3009},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG31Cs1Dzhes3vunsaPJ\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"d0bd7e5c-be4e-43de-b435-e22876bd1a27\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:24.639Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d0bd7e5c-be4e-43de-b435-e22876bd1a27\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01U7zqKeqN5Zt382iFRsvTMG\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"// Creater is an object that can create an instance of a RESTful object.\\ntype Creater interface {\\n\\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create after request data has been put into it.\\n\\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n\\tNew() runtime.Object\\n\\n\\t// Create creates a new version of a resource.\\n\\tCreate(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n}\\n\\n// NamedCreater is an object that can create an instance of a RESTful object using a name parameter.\\ntype NamedCreater interface {\\n\\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create after request data has been put into it.\\n\\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n\\tNew() runtime.Object\\n\\n\\t// Create creates a new version of a resource. It expects a name parameter from the path.\\n\\t// This is needed for create operations on subresources which include the name of the parent\\n\\t// resource in the path.\\n\\tCreate(ctx context.Context, name string, obj runtime.Object, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n}\\n\\n// SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver adds configuration options to a Creater for subresources.\\ntype SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver interface {\\n\\t// PreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate indicates that a\\n\\t// handler should preserve fields of ObjectMeta that are managed by the system.\\n\\tPreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate() bool\\n}\\n\\n// UpdatedObjectInfo provides information about an updated object to an Updater.\\n// It requires access to the old object in order to return the newly updated object.\\ntype UpdatedObjectInfo interface {\\n\\t// Returns preconditions built from the updated object, if applicable.\\n\\t// May return nil, or a preconditions object containing nil fields,\\n\\t// if no preconditions can be determined from the updated object.\\n\\tPreconditions() *metav1.Preconditions\\n\\n\\t// UpdatedObject returns the updated object, given a context and old object.\\n\\t// The only time an empty oldObj should be passed in is if a \\\"create on update\\\" is occurring (there is no oldObj).\\n\\tUpdatedObject(ctx context.Context, oldObj runtime.Object) (newObj runtime.Object, err error)\\n}\\n\\n// ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\\n// if the hook cannot be completed. A ValidateObjectFunc may NOT transform the provided\\n// object.\\ntype ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\\n\\n// ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \\\"admit everything\\\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\\nfunc ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// ValidateObjectUpdateFunc is a function to act on a given object and its predecessor.\\n// An error may be returned if the hook cannot be completed. An UpdateObjectFunc\\n// may NOT transform the provided object.\\ntype ValidateObjectUpdateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error\\n\\n// ValidateAllObjectUpdateFunc is a \\\"admit everything\\\" instance of ValidateObjectUpdateFunc.\\nfunc ValidateAllObjectUpdateFunc(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error {\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// Updater is an object that can update an instance of a RESTful object.\\ntype Updater interface {\\n\\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Update after request data has been put into it.\\n\\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n\\tNew() runtime.Object\\n\\n\\t// Update finds a resource in the storage and updates it. Some implementations\\n\\t// may allow updates creates the object - they should set the created boolean\\n\\t// to true.\\n\\tUpdate(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo UpdatedObjectInfo, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, updateValidation ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\\n}\\n\\n// CreaterUpdater is a storage object that must support both create and update.\\n// Go prevents embedded interfaces that implement the same method.\\ntype CreaterUpdater interface {\\n\\tCreater\\n\\tUpdate(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo UpdatedObjectInfo, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, updateValidation ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\\n}\\n\\n// CreaterUpdater must satisfy the Updater interface.\\nvar _ Updater = CreaterUpdater(nil)\\n\\n// Patcher is a storage object that supports both get and update.\\ntype Patcher interface {\\n\\tGetter\\n\\tUpdater\\n}\\n\\n// Watcher should be implemented by all Storage objects that\\n// want to offer the ability to watch for changes through the watch api.\\ntype Watcher interface {\\n\\t// 'label' selects on labels; 'field' selects on the object's fields. Not all fields\\n\\t// are supported; an error should be returned if 'field' tries to select on a field that\\n\\t// isn't supported. 'resourceVersion' allows for continuing/starting a watch at a\\n\\t// particular version.\\n\\tWatch(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\\n}\\n\\n// StandardStorage is an interface covering the common verbs. Provided for testing whether a\\n// resource satisfies the normal storage methods. Use Storage when passing opaque storage objects.\\ntype StandardStorage interface {\\n\\tGetter\\n\\tLister\\n\\tCreaterUpdater\\n\\tGracefulDeleter\\n\\tCollectionDeleter\\n\\tWatcher\\n\\n\\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\\n\\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\\n\\t// for being called more than once.\\n\\tDestroy()\\n}\\n\\n// Redirector know how to return a remote resource's location.\\ntype Redirector interface {\\n\\t// ResourceLocation should return the remote location of the given resource, and an optional transport to use to request it, or an error.\\n\\tResourceLocation(ctx context.Context, id string) (remoteLocation *url.URL, transport http.RoundTripper, err error)\\n}\\n\\n// Responder abstracts the normal response behavior for a REST method and is passed to callers that\\n// may wish to handle the response directly in some cases, but delegate to the normal error or object\\n// behavior in other cases.\\ntype Responder interface {\\n\\t// Object writes the provided object to the response. Invoking this method multiple times is undefined.\\n\\tObject(statusCode int, obj runtime.Object)\\n\\t// Error writes the provided error to the response. This method may only be invoked once.\\n\\tError(err error)\\n}\\n\\n// Connecter is a storage object that responds to a connection request.\\ntype Connecter interface {\\n\\t// Connect returns an http.Handler that will handle the request/response for a given API invocation.\\n\\t// The provided responder may be used for common API responses. The responder will write both status\\n\\t// code and body, so the ServeHTTP method should exit after invoking the responder. The Handler will\\n\\t// be used for a single API request and then discarded. The Responder is guaranteed to write to the\\n\\t// same http.ResponseWriter passed to ServeHTTP.\\n\\tConnect(ctx context.Context, id string, options runtime.Object, r Responder) (http.Handler, error)\\n\\n\\t// NewConnectOptions returns an empty options object that will be used to pass\\n\\t// options to the Connect method. If nil, then a nil options object is passed to\\n\\t// Connect. It may return a bool and a string. If true, the value of the request\\n\\t// path below the object will be included as the named string in the serialization\\n\\t// of the runtime object.\\n\\tNewConnectOptions() (runtime.Object, bool, string)\\n\\n\\t// ConnectMethods returns the list of HTTP methods handled by Connect\\n\\tConnectMethods() []string\\n}\\n\\n// ResourceStreamer is an interface implemented by objects that prefer to be streamed from the server\\n// instead of decoded directly.\\ntype ResourceStreamer interface {\\n\\t// InputStream should return an io.ReadCloser if the provided object supports streaming. The desired\\n\\t// api version and an accept header (may be empty) are passed to the call. If no error occurs,\\n\\t// the caller may return a flag indicating whether the result should be flushed as writes occur\\n\\t// and a content type string that indicates the type of the stream.\\n\\t// If a null stream is returned, a StatusNoContent response wil be generated.\\n\\tInputStream(ctx context.Context, apiVersion, acceptHeader string) (stream io.ReadCloser, flush bool, mimeType string, err error)\\n}\\n\\n// StorageMetadata is an optional interface that callers can implement to provide additional\\n// information about their Storage objects.\\ntype StorageMetadata interface {\\n\\t// ProducesMIMETypes returns a list of the MIME types the specified HTTP verb (GET, POST, DELETE,\\n\\t// PATCH) can respond with.\\n\\tProducesMIMETypes(verb string) []string\\n\\n\\t// ProducesObject returns an object the specified HTTP verb respond with. It will overwrite storage object if\\n\\t// it is not nil. Only the type of the return object matters, the value will be ignored.\\n\\tProducesObject(verb string) interface{}\\n}\\n\\n// StorageVersionProvider is an optional interface that a storage object can\\n// implement if it wishes to disclose its storage version.\\ntype StorageVersionProvider interface {\\n\\t// StorageVersion returns a group versioner, which will outputs the gvk\\n\\t// an object will be converted to before persisted in etcd, given a\\n\\t// list of kinds the object might belong to.\\n\\tStorageVersion() runtime.GroupVersioner\\n}\\n\\n// ResetFieldsStrategy is an optional interface that a storage object can\\n// implement if it wishes to provide the fields reset by its strategies.\\ntype ResetFieldsStrategy interface {\\n\\tGetResetFields() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set\\n}\\n\\n// ResetFieldsFilterStrategy is an optional interface that a storage object can\\n// implement if it wishes to provide a fields filter reset by its strategies.\\ntype ResetFieldsFilterStrategy interface {\\n\\tGetResetFieldsFilter() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter\\n}\\n\\n// CreateUpdateResetFieldsStrategy is a union of RESTCreateUpdateStrategy\\n// and ResetFieldsStrategy.\\ntype CreateUpdateResetFieldsStrategy interface {\\n\\tRESTCreateUpdateStrategy\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"5c274569-e542-4595-9fa3-0e880f95dbf8\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:24.654Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"d0bd7e5c-be4e-43de-b435-e22876bd1a27\"}\n",

IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage storage\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n\\n\\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.\\ntype Feature = string\\n\\n// RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.\\nvar RequestWatchProgress Feature = \\\"RequestWatchProgress\\\"\\n\\n// Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response\\n// onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an\\n// object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be\\n// a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,\\n// intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of\\n// type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.\\ntype Versioner interface {\\n\\t// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object\\n\\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata\\n\\t// from database.\\n\\tUpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error\\n\\t// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object\\n\\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from\\n\\t// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client\\n\\t// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number\\n\\t// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during\\n\\t// serialization if it is set to nil.\\n\\tUpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error\\n\\t// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should\\n\\t// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.\\n\\tPrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error\\n\\t// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.\\n\\t// Should return an error if the specified object does not have a persistable version.\\n\\tObjectResourceVersion(obj runtime.Object) (uint64, error)\\n\\n\\t// ParseResourceVersion takes a resource version argument and\\n\\t// converts it to the storage backend. For watch we should pass to helper.Watch().\\n\\t// Because resourceVersion is an opaque value, the default watch\\n\\t// behavior for non-zero watch is to watch the next value (if you pass\\n\\t// \\\"1\\\", you will see updates from \\\"2\\\" onwards).\\n\\tParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) (uint64, error)\\n}\\n\\n// ResponseMeta contains information about the database metadata that is associated with\\n// an object. It abstracts the actual underlying objects to prevent coupling with concrete\\n// database and to improve testability.\\ntype ResponseMeta struct {\\n\\t// TTL is the time to live of the node that contained the returned object. It may be\\n\\t// zero or negative in some cases (objects may be expired after the requested\\n\\t// expiration time due to server lag).\\n\\tTTL int64\\n\\t// The resource version of the node that contained the returned object.\\n\\tResourceVersion uint64\\n}\\n\\n// IndexerFunc is a function that for a given object computes\\n// `<value of an index>` for a particular `<index>`.\\ntype IndexerFunc func(obj runtime.Object) string\\n\\n// IndexerFuncs is a mapping from `<index name>` to function that\\n// for a given object computes `<value for that index>`.\\ntype IndexerFuncs map[string]IndexerFunc\\n\\n// Everything accepts all objects.\\nvar Everything = SelectionPredicate{\\n\\tLabel: labels.Everything(),\\n\\tField: fields.Everything(),\\n}\\n\\n// MatchValue defines a pair (`<index name>`, `<value for that index>`).\\ntype MatchValue struct {\\n\\tIndexName string\\n\\tValue     string\\n}\\n\\n// Pass an UpdateFunc to Interface.GuaranteedUpdate to make an update\\n// that is guaranteed to succeed.\\n// See the comment for GuaranteedUpdate for more details.\\ntype UpdateFunc func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (output runtime.Object, ttl *uint64, err error)\\n\\n// ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\\n// if the hook cannot be completed. The function may NOT transform the provided\\n// object.\\n// NOTE: the object in obj may be nil if it cannot be read from the\\n// storage, due to transformation or decode error.\\ntype ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\\n\\n// ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \\\"admit everything\\\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\\nfunc ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.\\ntype Preconditions struct {\\n\\t// Specifies the target UID.\\n\\t// +optional\\n\\tUID *types.UID `json:\\\"uid,omitempty\\\"`\\n\\t// Specifies the target ResourceVersion\\n\\t// +optional\\n\\tResourceVersion *string `json:\\\"resourceVersion,omitempty\\\"`\\n}\\n\\n// NewUIDPreconditions returns a Preconditions with UID set.\\nfunc NewUIDPreconditions(uid string) *Preconditions {\\n\\tu := types.UID(uid)\\n\\treturn &Preconditions{UID: &u}\\n}\\n\\nfunc (p *Preconditions) Check(key string, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n\\tif p == nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t}\\n\\tobjMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn NewInternalError(\\n\\t\\t\\tfmt.Errorf(\\\"can't enforce preconditions %v on un-introspectable object %v, got error: %w\\\",\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t*p,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tobj,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\terr))\\n\\t}\\n\\tif p.UID != nil && *p.UID != objMeta.GetUID() {\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"80abdd2d-b9ad-4d3a-b60f-d4cae033b573\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:48:54.475Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"3e90b83e-d191-4e46-8113-346b387d15e4\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"80abdd2d-b9ad-4d3a-b60f-d4cae033b573\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01VdRA73GJ9sMKCQ4Juu92Gr\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Good! 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage storage\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n\\n\\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.\\ntype Feature = string\\n\\n// RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.\\nvar RequestWatchProgress Feature = \\\"RequestWatchProgress\\\"\\n\\n// Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response\\n// onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an\\n// object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be\\n// a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,\\n// intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of\\n// type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.\\ntype Versioner interface {\\n\\t// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object\\n\\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata\\n\\t// from database.\\n\\tUpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error\\n\\t// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object\\n\\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from\\n\\t// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client\\n\\t// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number\\n\\t// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during\\n\\t// serialization if it is set to nil.\\n\\tUpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error\\n\\t// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should\\n\\t// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.\\n\\tPrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error\\n\\t// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.\\n\\t// Should return an error if the specified object does not have a persistable version.\\n\\tObjectResourceVersion(obj runtime.Object) (uint64, error)\\n\\n\\t// ParseResourceVersion takes a resource version argument and\\n\\t// converts it to the storage backend. For watch we should pass to helper.Watch().\\n\\t// Because resourceVersion is an opaque value, the default watch\\n\\t// behavior for non-zero watch is to watch the next value (if you pass\\n\\t// \\\"1\\\", you will see updates from \\\"2\\\" onwards).\\n\\tParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) (uint64, error)\\n}\\n\\n// ResponseMeta contains information about the database metadata that is associated with\\n// an object. It abstracts the actual underlying objects to prevent coupling with concrete\\n// database and to improve testability.\\ntype ResponseMeta struct {\\n\\t// TTL is the time to live of the node that contained the returned object. It may be\\n\\t// zero or negative in some cases (objects may be expired after the requested\\n\\t// expiration time due to server lag).\\n\\tTTL int64\\n\\t// The resource version of the node that contained the returned object.\\n\\tResourceVersion uint64\\n}\\n\\n// IndexerFunc is a function that for a given object computes\\n// `<value of an index>` for a particular `<index>`.\\ntype IndexerFunc func(obj runtime.Object) string\\n\\n// IndexerFuncs is a mapping from `<index name>` to function that\\n// for a given object computes `<value for that index>`.\\ntype IndexerFuncs map[string]IndexerFunc\\n\\n// Everything accepts all objects.\\nvar Everything = SelectionPredicate{\\n\\tLabel: labels.Everything(),\\n\\tField: fields.Everything(),\\n}\\n\\n// MatchValue defines a pair (`<index name>`, `<value for that index>`).\\ntype MatchValue struct {\\n\\tIndexName string\\n\\tValue     string\\n}\\n\\n// Pass an UpdateFunc to Interface.GuaranteedUpdate to make an update\\n// that is guaranteed to succeed.\\n// See the comment for GuaranteedUpdate for more details.\\ntype UpdateFunc func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (output runtime.Object, ttl *uint64, err error)\\n\\n// ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\\n// if the hook cannot be completed. The function may NOT transform the provided\\n// object.\\n// NOTE: the object in obj may be nil if it cannot be read from the\\n// storage, due to transformation or decode error.\\ntype ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\\n\\n// ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \\\"admit everything\\\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\\nfunc ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.\\ntype Preconditions struct {\\n\\t// Specifies the target UID.\\n\\t// +optional\\n\\tUID *types.UID `json:\\\"uid,omitempty\\\"`\\n\\t// Specifies the target ResourceVersion\\n\\t// +optional\\n\\tResourceVersion *string `json:\\\"resourceVersion,omitempty\\\"`\\n}\\n\\n// NewUIDPreconditions returns a Preconditions with UID set.\\nfunc NewUIDPreconditions(uid string) *Preconditions {\\n\\tu := types.UID(uid)\\n\\treturn &Preconditions{UID: &u}\\n}\\n\\nfunc (p *Preconditions) Check(key string, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n\\tif p == nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t}\\n\\tobjMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn NewInternalError(\\n\\t\\t\\tfmt.Errorf(\\\"can't enforce preconditions %v on un-introspectable object %v, got error: %w\\\",\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t*p,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tobj,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\terr))\\n\\t}\\n\\tif p.UID != nil && *p.UID != objMeta.GetUID() {\\n\\t\\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\\n\\t\\t\\t\\\"Precondition failed: UID in precondition: %v, UID in object meta: %v\\\",\\n\\t\\t\\t*p.UID,\\n\\t\\t\\tobjMeta.GetUID())\\n\\t\\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\\n\\t}\\n\\tif p.ResourceVersion != nil && *p.ResourceVersion != objMeta.GetResourceVersion() {\\n\\t\\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\\n\\t\\t\\t\\\"Precondition failed: ResourceVersion in precondition: %v, ResourceVersion in object meta: %v\\\",\\n\\t\\t\\t*p.ResourceVersion,\\n\\t\\t\\tobjMeta.GetResourceVersion())\\n\\t\\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn nil\\n}\\n\\n// Interface offers a common interface for object marshaling/unmarshaling operations and\\n// hides all the storage-related operations behind it.\\ntype Interface interface {\\n\\t// Returns Versioner associated with this interface.\\n\\tVersioner() Versioner\\n\\n\\t// Create adds a new object at a key unless it already exists. 'ttl' is time-to-live\\n\\t// in seconds (0 means forever). If no error is returned and out is not nil, out will be\\n\\t// set to the read value from database.\\n\\tCreate(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error\\n\\n\\t// Delete removes the specified key and returns the value that existed at that spot.\\n\\t// If key didn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error.\\n\\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\\n\\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\\n\\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\\n\\tDelete(\\n\\t\\tctx context.Context, key string, out runtime.Object, preconditions *Preconditions,\\n\\t\\tvalidateDeletion ValidateObjectFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object, opts DeleteOptions) error\\n\\n\\t// Watch begins watching the specified key. Events are decoded into API objects,\\n\\t// and any items selected by 'p' are sent down to returned watch.Interface.\\n\\t// resourceVersion may be used to specify what version to begin watching,\\n\\t// which should be the current resourceVersion, and no longer rv+1\\n\\t// (e.g. reconnecting without missing any updates).\\n\\t// If resource version is \\\"0\\\", this interface will get current object at given key\\n\\t// and send it in an \\\"ADDED\\\" event, before watch starts.\\n\\tWatch(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\\n\\n\\t// Get unmarshals object found at key into objPtr. On a not found error, will either\\n\\t// return a zero object of the requested type, or an error, depending on 'opts.ignoreNotFound'.\\n\\t// Treats empty responses and nil response nodes exactly like a not found error.\\n\\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\\n\\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\\n\\tGet(ctx context.Context, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error\\n\\n\\t// GetList unmarshalls objects found at key into a *List api object (an object\\n\\t// that satisfies runtime.IsList definition).\\n\\t// If 'opts.Recursive' is false, 'key' is used as an exact match. If `opts.Recursive'\\n\\t// is true, 'key' is used as a prefix.\\n\\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\\n\\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\\n\\tGetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error\\n\\n\\t// GuaranteedUpdate keeps calling 'tryUpdate()' to update key 'key' (of type 'destination')\\n\\t// retrying the update until success if there is index conflict.\\n\\t// Note that object passed to tryUpdate may change across invocations of tryUpdate() if\\n\\t// other writers are simultaneously updating it, so tryUpdate() needs to take into account\\n\\t// the current contents of the object when deciding how the update object should look.\\n\\t// If the key doesn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error if ignoreNotFound=false\\n\\t// else `destination` will be set to the zero value of it's type.\\n\\t// If the eventual successful invocation of `tryUpdate` returns an output with the same serialized\\n\\t// contents as the input, it won't perform any update, but instead set `destination` to an object with those\\n\\t// contents.\\n\\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\\n\\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\\n\\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// Example:\\n\\t//\\n\\t// s := /* implementation of Interface */\\n\\t// err := s.GuaranteedUpdate(\\n\\t//     \\\"myKey\\\", &MyType{}, true, preconditions,\\n\\t//     func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (runtime.Object, *uint64, error) {\\n\\t//       // Before each invocation of the user defined function, \\\"input\\\" is reset to\\n\\t//       // current contents for \\\"myKey\\\" in database.\\n\\t//       curr := input.(*MyType)  // Guaranteed to succeed.\\n\\t//\\n\\t//       // Make the modification\\n\\t//       curr.Counter++\\n\\t//\\n\\t//       // Return the modified object - return an error to stop iterating. Return\\n\\t//       // a uint64 to alter the TTL on the object, or nil to keep it the same value.\\n\\t//       return cur, nil, nil\\n\\t//    }, cachedExistingObject\\n\\t// )\\n\\tGuaranteedUpdate(\\n\\t\\tctx context.Context, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,\\n\\t\\tpreconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error\\n\\n\\t// Stats returns storage stats.\\n\\tStats(ctx context.Context) (Stats, error)\\n\\n\\t// ReadinessCheck checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\\n\\tReadinessCheck() error\\n\\n\\t// RequestWatchProgress requests the a watch stream progress status be sent in the\\n\\t// watch response stream as soon as possible.\\n\\t// Used for monitor watch progress even if watching resources with no changes.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// If watch is lagging, progress status might:\\n\\t// * be pointing to stale resource version. Use etcd KV request to get linearizable resource version.\\n\\t// * not be delivered at all. It's recommended to poll request progress periodically.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// Note: Only watches with matching context grpc metadata will be notified.\\n\\t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/9325a57125e8502941d1b0c7379c4bb80a678d5c/vendor/go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/watch.go#L1037-L1042\\n\\t//\\n\\t// TODO: Remove when storage.Interface will be separate from etc3.store.\\n\\t// Deprecated: Added temporarily to simplify exposing RequestProgress for watch cache.\\n\\tRequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error\\n\\n\\t// GetCurrentResourceVersion gets the current resource version from etcd.\\n\\t// This method issues an empty list request and reads only the ResourceVersion from the object metadata\\n\\tGetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)\\n\\n\\t// EnableResourceSizeEstimation enables estimating resource size by providing function get keys from storage.\\n\\tEnableResourceSizeEstimation(KeysFunc) error\\n\\n\\t// CompactRevision returns latest observed revision that was compacted.\\n\\t// Without ListFromCacheSnapshot enabled only locally executed compaction will be observed.\\n\\t// Returns 0 if no compaction was yet observed.\\n\\tCompactRevision() int64\\n}\\n\\n// KeysFunc is a function prototype to fetch keys from storage.\\ntype KeysFunc func(context.Context) ([]string, error)\\n\\n// GetOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage get operations.\\ntype GetOptions struct {\\n\\t// IgnoreNotFound determines what is returned if the requested object is not found. If\\n\\t// true, a zero object is returned. If false, an error is returned.\\n\\tIgnoreNotFound bool\\n\\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the get operation\\n\\t// as a \\\"not older than\\\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\\n\\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\\n\\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\\n\\tResourceVersion string\\n}\\n\\n// ListOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage list operations.\\ntype ListOptions struct {\\n\\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the list operation\\n\\t// as a \\\"not older than\\\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\\n\\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\\n\\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\\n\\tResourceVersion string\\n\\t// ResourceVersionMatch provides the rule for how the resource version constraint applies. If set\\n\\t// to the default value \\\"\\\" the legacy resource version semantic apply.\\n\\tResourceVersionMatch metav1.ResourceVersionMatch\\n\\t// Predicate provides the selection rules for the list operation.\\n\\tPredicate SelectionPredicate\\n\\t// Recursive determines whether the list or watch is defined for a single object located at the\\n\\t// given key, or for the whole set of objects with the given key as a prefix.\\n\\tRecursive bool\\n\\t// ProgressNotify determines whether storage-originated bookmark (progress notify) events should\\n\\t// be delivered to the users. The option is ignored for non-watch requests.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// Firstly, note that this field is different from the Predicate.AllowWatchBookmarks field.\\n\\t// Secondly, this field is intended for internal clients only such as the watch cache.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// This means that external clients do not have the ability to set this field directly.\\n\\t// For example by setting the allowWatchBookmarks query parameter.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// The motivation for this approach is the fact that the frequency\\n\\t// of bookmark events from a storage like etcd might be very high.\\n\\t// As the number of watch requests increases, the server load would also increase.\\n\\t//\\n\\t// Furthermore, the server is not obligated to provide bookmark events at all,\\n\\t// as described in https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/956-watch-bookmark#proposal\\n\\tProgressNotify bool\\n\\t// SendInitialEvents, when set together with Watch option,\\n\\t// begin the watch stream with synthetic init events to build the\\n\\t// whole state of all resources followed by a synthetic \\\"Bookmark\\\"\\n\\t// event containing a ResourceVersion after which the server\\n\\t// continues streaming events.\\n\\tSendInitialEvents *bool\\n}\\n\\n// DeleteOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage delete operations.\\ntype DeleteOptions struct {\\n\\t// IgnoreStoreReadError, if enabled, will ignore store read error\\n\\t// such as transformation or decode failure and go ahead with the\\n\\t// deletion of the object.\\n\\t// NOTE: for normal deletion flow it should always be false, it may be\\n\\t// enabled by the caller only to facilitate unsafe deletion of corrupt\\n\\t// object which otherwise can not be deleted using the normal flow\\n\\tIgnoreStoreReadError bool\\n}\\n\\nfunc ValidateListOptions(keyPrefix string, versioner Versioner, opts ListOptions) (withRev int64, continueKey string, err error) {\\n\\tif opts.Recursive && len(opts.Predicate.Continue) > 0 {\\n\\t\\tcontinueKey, continueRV, err := DecodeContinue(opts.Predicate.Continue, keyPrefix)\\n\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn 0, \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"invalid continue token: %v\\\", err))\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif len(opts.ResourceVersion) > 0 && opts.ResourceVersion != \\\"0\\\" {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn 0, \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(\\\"specifying resource version is not allowed when using continue\\\")\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t// If continueRV > 0, the LIST request needs a specific resource version.\\n\\t\\t// continueRV==0 is invalid.\\n\\t\\t// If continueRV < 0, the request is for the latest resource version.\\n\\t\\tif continueRV > 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\twithRev = continueRV\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\treturn withRev, continueKey, nil\\n\\t}\\n\\tif len(opts.ResourceVersion) == 0 {\\n\\t\\treturn withRev, \\\"\\\", nil\\n\\t}\\n\\tparsedRV, err := versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn withRev, \\\"\\\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"invalid resource version: %v\\\", err))\\n\\t}\\n\\tswitch opts.ResourceVersionMatch {\\n\\tcase metav1.ResourceVersionMatchNotOlderThan:\\n\\t\\t// The not older than constraint is checked after we get a response from etcd,\\n\\t\\t// and returnedRV is then set to the revision we get from the etcd response.\\n\\tcase metav1.ResourceVersionMatchExact:\\n\\t\\twithRev = int64(parsedRV)\\n\\tcase \\\"\\\": // legacy case\\n\\t\\tif opts.Recursive && opts.Predicate.Limit > 0 && parsedRV > 0 {\\n\\t\\t\\twithRev = int64(parsedRV)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\tdefault:\\n\\t\\treturn withRev, \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"unknown ResourceVersionMatch value: %v\\\", opts.ResourceVersionMatch)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn withRev, \\\"\\\", nil\\n}\\n\\n// Stats provides statistics information about storage.\\ntype Stats struct {\\n\\t// ObjectCount informs about number of objects stored in the storage.\\n\\tObjectCount int64\\n\\t// EstimatedAverageObjectSizeBytes informs about size of objects stored in the storage, based on size of serialized values.\\n\\t// Value is an estimate, meaning it doesn't need to provide accurate nor consistent.\\n\\tEstimatedAverageObjectSizeBytes int64\\n}\\n\\nfunc PrepareKey(resourcePrefix, key string, recursive bool) (string, error) {\\n\\tif key == \\\"..\\\" ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.HasPrefix(key, \\\"../\\\") ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.HasSuffix(key, \\\"/..\\\") ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.Contains(key, \\\"/../\\\") {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid key: %q\\\", key)\\n\\t}\\n\\tif key == \\\".\\\" ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.HasPrefix(key, \\\"./\\\") ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.HasSuffix(key, \\\"/.\\\") ||\\n\\t\\tstrings.Contains(key, \\\"/./\\\") {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid key: %q\\\", key)\\n\\t}\\n\\tif key == \\\"\\\" || key == \\\"/\\\" {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"empty key: %q\\\", key)\\n\\t}\\n\\t// For recursive requests, we need to make sure the key ended with \\\"/\\\" so that we only\\n\\t// get children \\\"directories\\\". e.g. if we have key \\\"/a\\\", \\\"/a/b\\\", \\\"/ab\\\", getting keys\\n\\t// with prefix \\\"/a\\\" will return all three, while with prefix \\\"/a/\\\" will return only\\n\\t// \\\"/a/b\\\" which is the correct answer.\\n\\tif recursive && !strings.HasSuffix(key, \\\"/\\\") {\\n\\t\\tkey += \\\"/\\\"\\n\\t}\\n\\tif !strings.HasPrefix(key, resourcePrefix) {\\n\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid key: %q lacks resource prefix: %q\\\", key, resourcePrefix)\\n\\t}\\n\\treturn key, nil\\n}\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"75811533-5e34-4c9f-9791-1fc39b6c04d7\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:06.386Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"78b52618-27eb-489e-98d6-4b3c81fbc507\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"75811533-5e34-4c9f-9791-1fc39b6c04d7\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a09b299\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01NHGaUnarc5m8TGqbDeCn3u\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Great!
