-300\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":5,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":951,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":67936,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":951},\"output_tokens\":25,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG2ymn7HCSvhBVG7PuNR\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"86044cf8-e130-4219-9a1c-9e0547996b5c\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:04.617Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"86044cf8-e130-4219-9a1c-9e0547996b5c\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a664ea0\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_0131w3pzK1RrGzMH24bFYnN9\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"/*\\nCopyright 2023 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n\\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\\n\\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n\\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage apiserver\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n\\n\\tcoordinationapiv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/coordination/v1\\\"\\n\\tapiv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n\\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/uuid\\\"\\n\\tapiserverfeatures \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n\\tpeerreconcilers \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/reconcilers\\\"\\n\\tgenericregistry \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic\\\"\\n\\tgenericapiserver \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/dynamiccertificates\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/flagz\\\"\\n\\tserverstorage \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage\\\"\\n\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n\\tclientgoinformers \\\"k8s.io/client-go/informers\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\\\"\\n\\tzpagesfeatures \\\"k8s.io/component-base/zpages/features\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-helpers/apimachinery/lease\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/clock\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controlplane/controller/apiserverleasegc\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controlplane/controller/clusterauthenticationtrust\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controlplane/controller/leaderelection\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controlplane/controller/legacytokentracking\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controlplane/controller/systemnamespaces\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/routes\\\"\\n\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/serviceaccount\\\"\\n)\\n\\nvar (\\n\\t// IdentityLeaseGCPeriod is the interval which the lease GC controller checks for expired leases\\n\\t// IdentityLeaseGCPeriod is exposed so integration tests can tune this value.\\n\\tIdentityLeaseGCPeriod = 3600 * time.Second\\n\\t// IdentityLeaseDurationSeconds is the duration of kube-apiserver lease in seconds\\n\\t// IdentityLeaseDurationSeconds is exposed so integration tests can tune this value.\\n\\tIdentityLeaseDurationSeconds = 3600\\n\\t// IdentityLeaseRenewIntervalPeriod is the interval of kube-apiserver renewing its lease in seconds\\n\\t// IdentityLeaseRenewIntervalPeriod is exposed so integration tests can tune this value.\\n\\tIdentityLeaseRenewIntervalPeriod = 10 * time.Second\\n\\n\\t// LeaseCandidateGCPeriod is the interval which the leasecandidate GC controller checks for expired leases\\n\\t// This is exposed so integration tests can tune this value.\\n\\tLeaseCandidateGCPeriod = 30 * time.Minute\\n)\\n\\nconst (\\n\\t// IdentityLeaseComponentLabelKey is used to apply a component label to identity lease objects, indicating:\\n\\t//   1. the lease is an identity lease (different from leader election leases)\\n\\t//   2. which component owns this lease\\n\\tIdentityLeaseComponentLabelKey = \\\"apiserver.kubernetes.io/identity\\\"\\n\\t// KubeAPIServer defines variable used internally when referring to kube-apiserver component\\n\\tKubeAPIServer = \\\"kube-apiserver\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// Server is a struct that contains a generic control plane apiserver instance\\n// that can be run to start serving the APIs.\\ntype Server struct {\\n\\tGenericAPIServer *genericapiserver.GenericAPIServer\\n\\n\\tAPIResourceConfigSource   serverstorage.APIResourceConfigSource\\n\\tRESTOptionsGetter         genericregistry.RESTOptionsGetter\\n\\tClusterAuthenticationInfo clusterauthenticationtrust.ClusterAuthenticationInfo\\n\\tVersionedInformers        clientgoinformers.SharedInformerFactory\\n}\\n\\n// New returns a new instance of Master from the given config.\\n// Certain config fields will be set to a default value if unset.\\n// Certain config fields must be specified, including:\\n// KubeletClientConfig\\nfunc (c completedConfig) New(name string, delegationTarget genericapiserver.DelegationTarget) (*Server, error) {\\n\\tgeneric, err := c.Generic.New(name, delegationTarget)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif c.EnableLogsSupport {\\n\\t\\troutes.Logs{}.Install(generic.Handler.GoRestfulContainer)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tmd, err := serviceaccount.NewOpenIDMetadataProvider(\\n\\t\\tc.ServiceAccountIssuerURL,\\n\\t\\tc.ServiceAccountJWKSURI,\\n\\t\\tc.Generic.ExternalAddress,\\n\\t\\tc.ServiceAccountPublicKeysGetter,\\n\\t)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\t// If there was an error, skip installing the endpoints and log the\\n\\t\\t// error, but continue on. We don't return the error because the\\n\\t\\t// metadata responses require additional, backwards incompatible\\n\\t\\t// validation of command-line options.\\n\\t\\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Could not construct pre-rendered responses for\\\"+\\n\\t\\t\\t\\\" ServiceAccountIssuerDiscovery endpoints. Endpoints will not be\\\"+\\n\\t\\t\\t\\\" enabled. Error: %v\\\", err)\\n\\t\\tif c.ServiceAccountIssuerURL != \\\"\\\" {\\n\\t\\t\\t// The user likely expects this feature to be enabled if issuer URL is\\n\\t\\t\\t// set and the feature gate is enabled. In the future, if there is no\\n\\t\\t\\t// longer a feature gate and issuer URL is not set, the user may not\\n\\t\\t\\t// expect this feature to be enabled. We log the former case as an Error\\n\\t\\t\\t// and the latter case as an Info.\\n\\t\\t\\tklog.Error(msg)\\n\\t\\t} else {\\n\\t\\t\\tklog.Info(msg)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t} else {\\n\\t\\troutes.NewOpenIDMetadataServer(md).Install(generic.Handler.GoRestfulContainer)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\ts := &Server{\\n\\t\\tGenericAPIServer: generic,\\n\\n\\t\\tAPIResourceConfigSource:   c.APIResourceConfigSource,\\n\\t\\tRESTOptionsGetter:         c.Generic.RESTOptionsGetter,\\n\\t\\tClusterAuthenticationInfo: c.ClusterAuthenticationInfo,\\n\\t\\tVersionedInformers:        c.VersionedInformers,\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tclient, err := kubernetes.NewForConfig(s.GenericAPIServer.LoopbackClientConfig)\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\t}\\n\\tif len(c.SystemNamespaces) > 0 {\\n\\t\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPostStartHookOrDie(\\\"start-system-namespaces-controller\\\", func(hookContext genericapiserver.PostStartHookContext) error {\\n\\t\\t\\tgo systemnamespaces.NewController(c.SystemNamespaces, client, s.VersionedInformers.Core().V1().Namespaces()).Run(hookContext.Done())\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t\\t})\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t_, publicServicePort, err := c.Generic.SecureServing.HostPort()\\n\\tif err != nil {\\n\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to get listener address: %w\\\", err)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentFlagz) {\\n\\t\\tif c.Generic.Flagz != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\tflagz.Install(s.GenericAPIServer.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, name, c.Generic.Flagz)\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(apiserverfeatures.CoordinatedLeaderElection) {\\n\\t\\tleaseInformer := s.VersionedInformers.Coordination().V1().Leases()\\n\\t\\tlcInformer := s.VersionedInformers.Coordination().V1beta1().LeaseCandidates()\\n\\t\\t// Ensure that informers are registered before starting. Coordinated Leader Election leader-elected\\n\\t\\t// and may register informer handlers after they are started.\\n\\t\\t_ = leaseInformer.Informer()\\n\\t\\t_ = lcInformer.Informer()\\n\\t\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPostStartHookOrDie(\\\"start-kube-apiserver-coordinated-leader-election-controller\\\", func(hookContext genericapiserver.PostStartHookContext) error {\\n\\t\\t\\tgo leaderelection.RunWithLeaderElection(hookContext, s.GenericAPIServer.LoopbackClientConfig, func() (func(ctx context.Context, workers int), error) {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tcontroller, err := leaderelection.NewController(\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tleaseInformer,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlcInformer,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tclient.CoordinationV1(),\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tclient.CoordinationV1beta1(),\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tgccontroller := leaderelection.NewLeaseCandidateGC(\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tclient,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tLeaseCandidateGCPeriod,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlcInformer,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn func(ctx context.Context, workers int) {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgo controller.Run(ctx, workers)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tgo gccontroller.Run(ctx)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}, err\\n\\t\\t\\t}, leaderelection.LeaderElectionTimers{\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tLeaseDuration: c.CoordinatedLeadershipLeaseDuration,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tRenewDeadline: c.CoordinatedLeadershipRenewDeadline,\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tRetryPeriod:   c.CoordinatedLeadershipRetryPeriod,\\n\\t\\t\\t})\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t\\t})\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(apiserverfeatures.UnknownVersionInteroperabilityProxy) {\\n\\t\\tpeeraddress := getPeerAddress(c.Extra.PeerAdvertiseAddress, c.Generic.PublicAddress, publicServicePort)\\n\\t\\tpeerEndpointCtrl := peerreconcilers.New(\\n\\t\\t\\tc.Generic.APIServerID,\\n\\t\\t\\tpeeraddress,\\n\\t\\t\\tc.Extra.PeerEndpointLeaseReconciler,\\n\\t\\t\\tc.Extra.PeerEndpointReconcileInterval,\\n\\t\\t\\tclient)\\n\\t\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPostStartHookOrDie(\\\"peer-endpoint-reconciler-controller\\\",\\n\\t\\t\\tfunc(hookContext genericapiserver.PostStartHookContext) error {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tpeerEndpointCtrl.Start(hookContext.Done())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t\\t\\t})\\n\\t\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPreShutdownHookOrDie(\\\"peer-endpoint-reconciler-controller\\\",\\n\\t\\t\\tfunc() error {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tpeerEndpointCtrl.Stop()\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t\\t\\t})\\n\\t\\tif c.Extra.PeerProxy != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t// Wait for handler to be ready\\n\\t\\t\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPostStartHookOrDie(\\\"mixed-version-proxy-handler\\\", func(context genericapiserver.PostStartHookContext) error {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\terr := c.Extra.PeerProxy.WaitForCacheSync(context.Done())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t\\t\\t})\\n\\n\\t\\t\\t// Run local-discovery sync loop\\n\\t\\t\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPostStartHookOrDie(\\\"local-discovery-cache-sync\\\", func(context genericapiserver.PostStartHookContext) error {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\terr := c.Extra.PeerProxy.RunLocalDiscoveryCacheSync(context.Done())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n\\t\\t\\t})\\n\\n\\t\\t\\t// Run peer-discovery sync loop\\n\\t\\t\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPostStartHookOrDie(\\\"peer-discovery-cache-sync\\\", func(context genericapiserver.PostStartHookContext) error {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tgo c.Extra.PeerProxy.RunPeerDiscoveryCacheSync(context, 1)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t\\t\\t})\\n\\n\\t\\t\\t// RunGVDeletionWorkers processes GVs from deleted CRDs/APIServices. If a GV is no longer in use,\\n\\t\\t\\t// it is marked for removal from peer-discovery (with a deletion timestamp), triggering a grace period before cleanup.\\n\\t\\t\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPostStartHookOrDie(\\\"gv-deletion-workers\\\", func(context genericapiserver.PostStartHookContext) error {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tgo c.Extra.PeerProxy.RunGVDeletionWorkers(context, 1)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t\\t\\t})\\n\\n\\t\\t\\t// RunExcludedGVsReaper removes GVs from the peer-discovery exclusion list after their grace period expires.\\n\\t\\t\\t// This ensures we don't include stale CRDs/aggregated APIs from peer discovery in the aggregated discovery.\\n\\t\\t\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPostStartHookOrDie(\\\"excluded-groups-reaper\\\", func(context genericapiserver.PostStartHookContext) error {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tgo c.Extra.PeerProxy.RunExcludedGVsReaper(context.Done())\\n\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t\\t\\t})\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPostStartHookOrDie(\\\"start-cluster-authentication-info-controller\\\", func(hookContext genericapiserver.PostStartHookContext) error {\\n\\t\\tcontroller := clusterauthenticationtrust.NewClusterAuthenticationTrustController(s.ClusterAuthenticationInfo, client)\\n\\t\\t// prime values and start listeners\\n\\t\\tif s.ClusterAuthenticationInfo.ClientCA != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\ts.ClusterAuthenticationInfo.ClientCA.AddListener(controller)\\n\\t\\t\\tif controller, ok := s.ClusterAuthenticationInfo.ClientCA.(dynamiccertificates.ControllerRunner); ok {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// runonce to be sure that we have a value.\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tif err := controller.RunOnce(hookContext); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\truntime.HandleError(err)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tgo controller.Run(hookContext, 1)\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\tif s.ClusterAuthenticationInfo.RequestHeaderCA != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\ts.ClusterAuthenticationInfo.RequestHeaderCA.AddListener(controller)\\n\\t\\t\\tif controller, ok := s.ClusterAuthenticationInfo.RequestHeaderCA.(dynamiccertificates.ControllerRunner); ok {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t// runonce to be sure that we have a value.\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tif err := controller.RunOnce(hookContext); err != nil {\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t\\truntime.HandleError(err)\\n\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t\\t\\tgo controller.Run(hookContext, 1)\\n\\t\\t\\t}\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\n\\t\\tgo controller.Run(hookContext, 1)\\n\\t\\treturn nil\\n\\t})\\n\\n\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(apiserverfeatures.APIServerIdentity) {\\n\\t\\tleaseName := s.GenericAPIServer.APIServerID\\n\\t\\tholderIdentity := s.GenericAPIServer.APIServerID + \\\"_\\\" + string(uuid.NewUUID())\\n\\t\\tpeeraddress := getPeerAddress(c.Extra.PeerAdvertiseAddress, c.Generic.PublicAddress, publicServicePort)\\n\\n\\t\\t// must replace ':,[]' in [ip:port] to be able to store this as a valid label value\\n\\t\\tcontroller := lease.NewController(\\n\\t\\t\\tclock.RealClock{},\\n\\t\\t\\tclient,\\n\\t\\t\\tholderIdentity,\\n\\t\\t\\tint32(IdentityLeaseDurationSeconds),\\n\\t\\t\\tnil,\\n\\t\\t\\tIdentityLeaseRenewIntervalPeriod,\\n\\t\\t\\tleaseName,\\n\\t\\t\\tmetav1.NamespaceSystem,\\n\\t\\t\\t// TODO: receive identity label value as a parameter when post start hook is moved to generic apiserver.\\n\\t\\t\\tlabelAPIServerHeartbeatFunc(name, peeraddress),\\n\\t\\t)\\n\\t\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPostStartHookOrDie(\\\"start-kube-apiserver-identity-lease-controller\\\", func(hookContext genericapiserver.PostStartHookContext) error {\\n\\t\\t\\treturn controller.Start(hookContext.Done())\\n\\t\\t})\\n\\t\\ts.GenericAPIServer.AddPreShutdownHookOrDie(\\\"stop-kube-apiserver-identity-lease-controller\\\", func() error {\\n\\t\\t\\tcontroller.Stop()\\n\\t\\t\\treturn nil\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"e40cc5b2-f57a-4a73-b1ec-e35b578d6f75\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:04.667Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"86044cf8-e130-4219-9a1c-9e0547996b5c\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"e40cc5b2-f57a-4a73-b1ec-e35b578d6f75\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"37785fb0-7836-44bb-b599-5d8e96c419d9\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"a664ea0\",\"slug\":\"iterative-jingling-otter\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01YSDDWn64mJx3e6NNRUq5it\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01X18en3256RmgHHqEVqGX4S\",\"name\":\"Bash\",\"input\":{\"command\":\"git IS\\\" BASIS,\\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\nlimitations under the License.\\n*/\\n\\npackage controlplane\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n\\t\\\"net\\\"\\n\\t\\\"reflect\\\"\\n\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n\\n\\tadmissionregistrationv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/admissionregistration/v1\\\"\\n\\tadmissionregistrationv1alpha1 \\\"k8s.io/api/admissionregistration/v1alpha1\\\"\\n\\tadmissionregistrationv1beta1 \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/batch/rest\\\"\\n\\tcertificatesrest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/certificates/rest\\\"\\n\\tcoordinationrest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/coordination/rest\\\"\\n\\tcorerest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/core/rest\\\"\\n\\tdiscoveryrest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/flowcontrol/rest\\\"\\n\\tnetworkingrest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/networking/rest\\\"\\n\\tnoderest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/node/rest\\\"\\n\\tpolicyrest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/policy/rest\\\"\\n\\trbacrest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/rbac/rest\\\"\\n\\tresourcerest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/resource/rest\\\"\\n\\tschedulingrest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/scheduling/rest\\\"\\n\\tstoragerest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/storage/rest\\\"\\n\\tsvmrest \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/registry/storagemigration/rest\\\"\\n)\\n\\nconst (\\n\\t// DefaultEndpointReconcilerInterval is the default amount of time for how often the endpoints for\\n\\t// the kubernetes Service are reconciled.\\n\\tDefaultEndpointReconcilerInterval = 10 * time.Second\\n\\t// DefaultEndpointReconcilerTTL is the default TTL timeout for the storage layer\\n\\tDefaultEndpointReconcilerTTL = 15 * time.Second\\n\\t// IdentityLeaseComponentLabelKey is used to apply a component label to identity lease objects, indicating:\\n\\t//   1. the lease is an identity lease (different from leader election leases)\\n\\t//   2. which component owns this lease\\n\\t// TODO(sttts): remove this indirection\\n\\tIdentityLeaseComponentLabelKey = controlplaneapiserver.IdentityLeaseComponentLabelKey\\n\\t// repairLoopInterval defines the interval used to run the Services ClusterIP and NodePort repair loops\\n\\trepairLoopInterval = 3 * time.Minute\\n)\\n\\nvar (\\n\\t// AdditionalStorageProvidersForTests allows tests to inject additional test-only API groups.\\n\\t// Only meant for use in integration tests.\\n\\tAdditionalStorageProvidersForTests func(client *kubernetes.Clientset) []controlplaneapiserver.RESTStorageProvider\\n)\\n\\n// Extra defines extra configuration for kube-apiserver\\ntype Extra struct {\\n\\tEndpointReconcilerConfig EndpointReconcilerConfig\\n\\tKubeletClientConfig      kubeletclient.KubeletClientConfig\\n\\n\\t// Values to build the IP addresses used by discovery\\n\\t// The range of IPs to be assigned to services with type=ClusterIP or greater\\n\\tServiceIPRange net.IPNet\\n\\t// The IP address for the GenericAPIServer service (must be inside ServiceIPRange)\\n\\tAPIServerServiceIP net.IP\\n\\n\\t// dual stack services, the range represents an alternative IP range for service IP\\n\\t// must be of different family than primary (ServiceIPRange)\\n\\tSecondaryServiceIPRange net.IPNet\\n\\t// the secondary IP address the GenericAPIServer service (must be inside SecondaryServiceIPRange)\\n\\tSecondaryAPIServerServiceIP net.IP\\n\\n\\t// Port for the apiserver service.\\n\\tAPIServerServicePort int\\n\\n\\t// TODO, we can probably group service related items into a substruct to make it easier to configure\\n\\t// the API server items and `Extra*` fields likely fit nicely together.\\n\\n\\t// The range of ports to be assigned to services with type=NodePort or greater\\n\\tServiceNodePortRange utilnet.PortRange\\n\\t// If non-zero, the \\\"kubernetes\\\" services uses this port as NodePort.\\n\\tKubernetesServiceNodePort int\\n\\n\\t// Number of masters running; all masters must be started with the\\n\\t// same value for this field. (Numbers > 1 currently untested.)\\n\\tMasterCount int\\n\\n\\t// MasterEndpointReconcileTTL sets the time to live in seconds of an\\n\\t// endpoint record recorded by each master. The endpoints are checked at an\\n\\t// interval that is 2/3 of this value and this value defaults to 15s if\\n\\t// unset. In very large clusters, this value may be increased to reduce the\\n\\t// possibility that the master endpoint record expires (due to other load\\n\\t// on the etcd server) and causes masters to drop in and out of the\\n\\t// kubernetes service record. It is not recommended to set this value below\\n\\t// 15s.\\n\\tMasterEndpointReconcileTTL time.Duration\\n\\n\\t// Selects which reconciler to use\\n\\tEndpointReconcilerType reconcilers.Type\\n\\n\\t// RepairServicesInterval interval used by the repair loops for\\n\\t// the Services NodePort and ClusterIP resources\\n\\tRepairServicesInterval time.Duration\\n}\\n\\n// Config defines configuration for the master\\ntype Config struct {\\n\\tControlPlane controlplaneapiserver.Config\\n\\tExtra\\n}\\n\\ntype completedConfig struct {\\n\\tControlPlane controlplaneapiserver.CompletedConfig\\n\\t*Extra\\n}\\n\\n// CompletedConfig embeds a private pointer that cannot be instantiated outside of this package\\ntype CompletedConfig struct {\\n\\t*completedConfig\\n}\\n\\n// EndpointReconcilerConfig holds the endpoint reconciler and endpoint reconciliation interval to be\\n// used by the master.\\ntype EndpointReconcilerConfig struct {\\n\\tReconciler reconcilers.EndpointReconciler\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"67c33241-ccab-41d4-8afa-bc348c205edd\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T18:49:04.816Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"81ed0d32-d135-492e-bf0b-0470b15c289b\"}\n",
    "status": "running",
    "task_id": "a664ea0",
    "task_type": "local_agent"
  }
}

Other implementors may\\n78: \\t// define a version \\\"v1beta1\\\" but want to use the Kubernetes \\\"v1\\\" internal objects.\\n79: \\t// If nil, defaults to groupMeta.GroupVersion.\\n80: \\t// TODO: Remove this when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/19018 is fixed.\\n81: \\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n82: \\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \\\"meta.k8s.io/v1\\\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\\n83: \\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\\n84: \\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\\n85: \\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n86: \\n87: \\t// Scheme includes all of the types used by this group and how to convert between them (or\\n88: \\t// to convert objects from outside of this group that are accepted in this API).\\n89: \\t// TODO: replace with interfaces\\n90: \\tScheme *runtime.Scheme\\n91: \\t// NegotiatedSerializer controls how this group encodes and decodes data\\n92: \\tNegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\\n93: \\t// ParameterCodec performs conversions for query parameters passed to API calls\\n94: \\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\\n95: \\n96: \\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the definitions of all resources installed together.\\n97: \\t// It is set during InstallAPIGroups, InstallAPIGroup, and InstallLegacyAPIGroup.\\n98: \\tStaticOpenAPISpec map[string]*spec.Schema\\n99: }\\n100: \\n101: func (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {\\n102: \\tfor _, stores := range a.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\\n103: \\t\\tfor _, store := range stores {\\n104: \\t\\t\\tstore.Destroy()\\n105: \\t\\t}\\n106: \\t}\\n107: }\\n108: \\n109: // GenericAPIServer contains state for a Kubernetes cluster api server.\\n110: type GenericAPIServer struct {\\n111: \\t// discoveryAddresses is used to build cluster IPs for discovery.\\n112: \\tdiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\\n113: \\n114: \\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\\n115: \\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\\n116: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL72-115\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "72: type APIGroupInfo struct {\n73: \tPrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion\n74: \t// Info about the resources in this group. It's a map from version to resource to the storage.\n75: \tVersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\n76: \t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the APIVersion used for common objects in the\n77: \t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\n78: \t// define a version \"v1beta1\" but want to use the Kubernetes \"v1\" internal objects.\n79: \t// If nil, defaults to groupMeta.GroupVersion.\n80: \t// TODO: Remove this when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/19018 is fixed.\n81: \tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n82: \t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \"meta.k8s.io/v1\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\n83: \t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\n84: \t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\n85: \tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n86: \n87: \t// Scheme includes all of the types used by this group and how to convert between them (or\n88: \t// to convert objects from outside of this group that are accepted in this API).\n89: \t// TODO: replace with interfaces\n90: \tScheme *runtime.Scheme\n91: \t// NegotiatedSerializer controls how this group encodes and decodes data\n92: \tNegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n93: \t// ParameterCodec performs conversions for query parameters passed to API calls\n94: \tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\n95: \n96: \t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the definitions of all resources installed together.\n97: \t// It is set during InstallAPIGroups, InstallAPIGroup, and InstallLegacyAPIGroup.\n98: \tStaticOpenAPISpec map[string]*spec.Schema\n99: }\n100: \n101: func (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {\n102: \tfor _, stores := range a.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\n103: \t\tfor _, store := range stores {\n104: \t\t\tstore.Destroy()\n105: \t\t}\n106: \t}\n107: }\n108: \n109: // GenericAPIServer contains state for a Kubernetes cluster api server.\n110: type GenericAPIServer struct {\n111: \t// discoveryAddresses is used to build cluster IPs for discovery.\n112: \tdiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n113: \n114: \t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n115: \tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n116: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL72-115"

\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n57: )\\n58: \\n59: // FinishFunc is a function returned by Begin hooks to complete an operation.\\n60: type FinishFunc func(ctx context.Context, success bool)\\n61: \\n62: // AfterDeleteFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterDelete hook.\\n63: type AfterDeleteFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.DeleteOptions)\\n64: \\n65: // BeginCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.BeginCreate hook.\\n66: type BeginCreateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (FinishFunc, error)\\n67: \\n68: // AfterCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterCreate hook.\\n69: type AfterCreateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions)\\n70: \\n71: // BeginUpdateFunc is the type used for the Store.BeginUpdate hook.\\n72: type BeginUpdateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (FinishFunc, error)\\n73: \\n74: // AfterUpdateFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterUpdate hook.\\n75: type AfterUpdateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.UpdateOptions)\\n76: \\n77: // GenericStore interface can be used for type assertions when we need to access the underlying strategies.\\n78: type GenericStore interface {\\n79: \\tGetCreateStrategy() rest.RESTCreateStrategy\\n80: \\tGetUpdateStrategy() rest.RESTUpdateStrategy\\n81: \\tGetDeleteStrategy() rest.RESTDeleteStrategy\\n82: }\\n83: \\n84: // Store implements k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest.StandardStorage. It's\\n85: // intended to be embeddable and allows the consumer to implement any\\n86: // non-generic functions that are required. This object is intended to be\\n87: // copyable so that it can be used in different ways but share the same\\n88: // underlying behavior.\\n89: //\\n90: // All fields are required unless specified.\\n91: //\\n92: // The intended use of this type is embedding within a Kind specific\\n93: // RESTStorage implementation. This type provides CRUD semantics on a Kubelike\\n94: // resource, handling details like conflict detection with ResourceVersion and\\n95: // semantics. The RESTCreateStrategy, RESTUpdateStrategy, and\\n96: // RESTDeleteStrategy are generic across all backends, and encapsulate logic\\n97: // specific to the API.\\n98: //\\n99: // TODO: make the default exposed methods exactly match a generic RESTStorage\\n100: type Store struct {\\n101: \\t// NewFunc returns a new instance of the type this registry returns for a\\n102: \\t// GET of a single object, e.g.:\\n103: \\t//\\n104: \\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource/name-of-object\\n105: \\tNewFunc func() runtime.Object\\n106: \\n107: \\t// NewListFunc returns a new list of the type this registry; it is the\\n108: \\t// type returned when the resource is listed, e.g.:\\n109: \\t//\\n110: \\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource\\n111: \\tNewListFunc func() runtime.Object\\n112: \\n113: \\t// DefaultQualifiedResource is the pluralized name of the resource.\\n114: \\t// This field is used if there is no request info present in the context.\\n115: \\t// See qualifiedResourceFromContext for details.\\n116: \\tDefaultQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\\n117: \\n118: \\t// SingularQualifiedResource is the singular name of the resource.\\n119: \\tSingularQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\\n120: \\n121: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go%3FL1-120\"}",

(\n58: \temptyFunc             = func(bool) {}\n59: \tcoreNamespaceResource = schema.GroupResource{Group: \"\", Resource: \"namespaces\"}\n60: )\n61: \n62: const (\n63: \t// storageWatchListPageSize duration of events\n68: \t// we want to keep.\n69: \t// We set it to defaultBookmarkFrequency plus epsilon to maximize\n70: \t// chances that last bookmark was sent within kept history, at the\n71: \t// same time, minimizing the needed memory usage.\n72: \tDefaultEventFreshDuration = defaultBookmarkFrequency + 15*time.Second\n73: \n74: \t// defaultBookmarkFrequency defines how frequently watch for a given Cache.\n80: type Config struct {\n81: \t// An underlying storage.Interface.\n82: \tStorage storage.Interface\n83: \n84: \t// An underlying storage.Versioner.\n85: \tVersioner {

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"490: \\tctx := wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh)\\n491: \\treturn s.RunWithContext(ctx)\\n492: }\\n493: \\n494: // RunWithContext spawns the secure http server. It only returns if ctx is canceled\\n495: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\\n496: // This is the diagram of what contexts/channels/signals are dependent on each other:\\n497: //\\n498: // |                                   ctx\\n499: // |                                    |\\n500: // |           ---------------------------------------------------------\\n501: // |           |                                                       |\\n502: // |    ShutdownInitiated (shutdownInitiatedCh)                        |\\n503: // |           |                                                       |\\n504: // | (ShutdownDelayDuration)                                    (PreShutdownHooks)\\n505: // |           |                                                       |\\n506: // |  AfterShutdownDelayDuration (delayedStopCh)   PreShutdownHooksStopped (preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh)\\n507: // |           |                                                       |\\n508: // |           |-------------------------------------------------------|\\n509: // |                                    |\\n510: // |                                    |\\n511: // |               NotAcceptingNewRequest (notAcceptingNewRequestCh)\\n512: // |                                    |\\n513: // |                                    |\\n514: // |           |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\\n515: // |           |                        |              |                                          |\\n516: // |        [without                 [with             |                                          |\\n517: // | ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  |                                          |\\n518: // |           |                        |              |                                          |\\n519: // |           |                        ---------------|                                          |\\n520: // |           |                                       |                                          |\\n521: // |           |                      |----------------|-----------------------|                  |\\n522: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |\\n523: // |           |         (NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   (WatchRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   |\\n524: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |\\n525: // |           |                      |------------------|---------------------|                  |\\n526: // |           |                                         |                                        |\\n527: // |           |                         InFlightRequestsDrained (drainedCh)                      |\\n528: // |           |                                         |                                        |\\n529: // |           |-------------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------|\\n530: // |                               |                     |\\n531: // |                       stopHttpServerCtx     (AuditBackend::Shutdown())\\n532: // |                               |\\n533: // |                       listenerStoppedCh\\n534: // |                               |\\n535: // |      HTTPServerStoppedListening (httpServerStoppedListeningCh)\\n536: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) RunWithContext(ctx context.Context) error {\\n537: \\tstopCh := ctx.Done()\\n538: \\tdelayedStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.AfterShutdownDelayDuration\\n539: \\tshutdownInitiatedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated\\n540: \\n541: \\t// Clean up resources on   "structuredContent": {
    "content": "490: \tctx := wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh)\n491: \treturn s.RunWithContext(ctx)\n492: }\n493: \n494: // RunWithContext spawns the secure http server. It only returns if ctx is canceled\n495: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\n496: // This is the diagram of what contexts/channels/signals are dependent on each other:\n497: //\n498: // |                                   ctx\n499: // |                                    |\n500: // |           ---------------------------------------------------------\n501: // |           |                                                       |\n502: // |    ShutdownInitiated (shutdownInitiatedCh)                        |\n503: // |           |   "content": "{\"content\":\"120: \\n121: \\t// KeyRootFunc returns the root etcd key for this resource; should not\\n122: \\t// include trailing \\\"/\\\".  This is used for operations that work on the\\n123: \\t// entire collection (listing and watching).\\n124: \\t//\\n125: \\t// KeyRootFunc and KeyFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\\n126: \\tKeyRootFunc func(ctx context.Context) string\\n127: \\n128: \\t// KeyFunc returns the key for a specific object in the collection.\\n129: \\t// KeyFunc is called for Create/Update/Get/Delete. Note that 'namespace'\\n130: \\t// can be gotten from ctx.\\n131: \\t//\\n132: \\t// KeyFunc and KeyRootFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\\n133: \\tKeyFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string) (string, error)\\n134: \\n135: \\t// ObjectNameFunc returns the name of an object or an error.\\n136: \\tObjectNameFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error)\\n137: \\n138: \\t// TTLFunc returns the TTL (time to live) that objects should be persisted\\n139: \\t// with. The existing parameter is the current TTL or the default for this\\n140: \\t// operation. The update parameter indicates whether this is an operation\\n141: \\t// against an existing object.\\n142: \\t//\\n143: \\t// Objects that are persisted with a TTL are evicted once the TTL expires.\\n144: \\tTTLFunc func(obj runtime.Object, existing uint64, update bool) (uint64, error)\\n145: \\n146: \\t// PredicateFunc returns a matcher corresponding to the provided labels\\n147: \\t// and fields. The SelectionPredicate returned should return true if the\\n148: \\t// object matches the given field and label selectors.\\n149: \\tPredicateFunc func(label labels.Selector, field fields.Selector) storage.SelectionPredicate\\n150: \\n151: \\t// EnableGarbageCollection affects the handling of Update and Delete\\n152: \\t// requests. Enabling garbage collection allows finalizers to do work to\\n153: \\t// finalize this object before the store deletes it.\\n154: \\t//\\n155: \\t// If any store has garbage collection enabled, it must also be enabled in\\n156: \\t// the kube-controller-manager.\\n157: \\tEnableGarbageCollection bool\\n158: \\n159: \\t// DeleteCollectionWorkers is the maximum number of workers in a single\\n160: \\t// DeleteCollection call. Delete requests for the items in a collection\\n161: \\t// are issued in parallel.\\n162: \\tDeleteCollectionWorkers int\\n163: \\n164: \\t// Decorator is an optional exit hook on an object returned from the\\n165: \\t// underlying storage. The returned object could be an individual object\\n166: \\t// (e.g. Pod) or a list type (e.g. PodList). Decorator is intended for\\n167: \\t// integrations that are above storage and should only be used for\\n168: \\t// specific cases where storage of the value is not appropriate, since\\n169: \\t// they cannot be watched.\\n170: \\tDecorator func(runtime.Object)\\n171: \\n172: \\t// CreateStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during creation.\\n173: \\tCreateStrategy rest.RESTCreateStrategy\\n174: \\t// BeginCreate is an optional hook that returns a \\\"transaction-like\\\"\\n175: \\t// commit/revert function which will be called at the end of the operation,\\n176: \\t// but before AfterCreate and Decorator, indicating via the argument\\n177: \\t// whether the operation succeeded.  If this returns an error, the function\\n178: \\t// is not called.  Almost nobody should use this hook.\\n179: \\tBeginCreate BeginCreateFunc\\n180: \\t// AfterCreate implements a further operation to run after a resource is\\n181: \\t// created and before it is decorated, optional.\\n182: \\tAfterCreate AfterCreateFunc\\n183: \\n184: \\t// UpdateStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during updates.\\n185: \\tUpdateStrategy rest.RESTUpdateStrategy\\n186: \\t// BeginUpdate is an optional hook that returns a \\\"transaction-like\\\"\\n187: \\t// commit/revert function which will be called at the end of the operation,\\n188: \\t// but before AfterUpdate and Decorator, indicating via the argument\\n189: \\t// whether the operation succeeded.  If this returns an error, the function\\n190: \\t// is not called.  Almost nobody should use this hook.\\n191: \\tBeginUpdate BeginUpdateFunc\\n192: \\t// AfterUpdate implements a further operation to run after a resource is\\n193: \\t// updated and before it is decorated, optional.\\n194: \\tAfterUpdate AfterUpdateFunc\\n195: \\n196: \\t// DeleteStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during deletion.\\n197: \\tDeleteStrategy rest.RESTDeleteStrategy\\n198: \\t// AfterDelete implements a further operation to run after a resource is\\n199: \\t// deleted and before it is decorated, optional.\\n200: \\tAfterDelete AfterDeleteFunc\\n201: \\t// ReturnDeletedObject determines whether the Store returns the object\\n202: \\t// that was deleted. Otherwise, return a generic success status response.\\n203: \\tReturnDeletedObject bool\\n204: \\t// ShouldDeleteDuringUpdate is an optional function to determine whether\\n205: \\t// an update from existing to obj should result in a delete.\\n206: \\t// If specified, this is checked in addition to standard finalizer,\\n207: \\t// deletionTimestamp, and deletionGracePeriodSeconds checks.\\n208: \\tShouldDeleteDuringUpdate func(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, existing runtime.Object) bool\\n209: \\n210: \\t// TableConvertor is an optional interface for transforming items or lists\\n211: \\t// of items into tabular output. If unset, the default will be used.\\n212: \\tTableConvertor rest.TableConvertor\\n213: \\n214: \\t// ResetFieldsStrategy provides the fields reset by the strategy that\\n215: \\t// should not be modified by the user.\\n216: \\tResetFieldsStrategy rest.ResetFieldsStrategy\\n217: \\n218: \\t// Storage is the interface for the underlying storage for the\\n219: \\t// resource. It is wrapped into a \\\"DryRunnableStorage\\\" that will\\n220: \\t// either pass-through or simply dry-run.\\n221: \\tStorage DryRunnableStorage\\n222: \\t// StorageVersioner outputs the <group/version/kind> an object will be\\n223: \\t// converted to before persisted in etcd, given a list of possible\\n224: \\t// kinds of the object.\\n225: \\t// If the StorageVersioner is nil, apiserver will leave the\\n226: \\t// storageVersionHash as empty in the discovery document.\\n227: \\tStorageVersioner runtime.GroupVersioner\\n228: \\n229: \\t// ReadinessCheckFunc checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\\n230: \\t// The field is optional, if set needs to be thread-safe.\\n231: \\tReadinessCheckFunc func() error\\n232: \\n233: \\t// DestroyFunc cleans up clients used by the underlying Storage; optional.\\n234: \\t// If set, DestroyFunc has to be implemented in thread-safe way and\\n235: \\t// be prepared for being called more than once.\\n236: \\tDestroyFunc func()\\n237: \\n238: \\t// corruptObjDeleter implements unsafe deletion flow to enable deletion\\n239: \\t// of corrupt object(s), it makes an attempt to perform a normal\\n240: \\t// deletion flow first, and if the normal deletion flow fails with a\\n241: \\t// corrupt object error then it proceeds with the unsafe deletion\\n242: \\t// of the object from the storage.\\n243: \\t// NOTE: it skips precondition checks, finalizer constraints, and any\\n244: \\t// after delete hook defined in 'AfterDelete' of the registry.\\n245: \\t// WARNING: This may break the cluster if the resource has\\n246: \\t// dependencies. Use when the cluster is broken, and there is no\\n247: \\t// other viable option to repair the cluster.\\n248: \\tcorruptObjDeleter rest.GracefulDeleter\\n249: }\\n250: \\n251: // Note: the rest.StandardStorage interface aggregates the common REST verbs\\n252: var _ rest.StandardStorage = &Store{}\\n253: var _ rest.StorageWithReadiness = &Store{}\\n254: var _ rest.TableConvertor = &Store{}\\n255: var _ GenericStore = &Store{}\\n256: \\n257: var _ rest.SingularNameProvider = &Store{}\\n258: \\n259: var _ rest.CorruptObjectDeleterProvider = &Store{}\\n260: \\n261: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go%3FL120-260\"}",

    "content": "120: \n121: \t// KeyRootFunc returns the root etcd key for this resource; should not\n122: \t// include trailing \"/\". Delete requests for the items in a collection\n161: \t// are issued in parallel.\n162: \tDeleteCollectionWorkers int\n163: \n164: \t// Decorator is an optional exit hook on an object returned from the\n165: \t// underlying storage. Decorator is intended for\n167: \t// integrations that are above storage and should only be used for\n168: \t// specific cases where storage of the value is not appropriate, since\n169: \t// they cannot be watched.\n170: \tDecorator func(runtime.Object)\n171: \n172: \t// CreateStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during creation.\n173: \tCreateStrategy rest.RESTCreateStrategy\n174: \t// BeginCreate is an optional hook that returns a \"transaction-like\"\n175: \t// commit/revert function which will be called at the end of the operation,\n176: \t// but before AfterCreate and Decorator, indicating via the argument\n177: \t// whether the operation succeeded. a.group.Storage[path], ws)\\n211: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n212: \\t\\t\\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error in registering resource: %s, %v\\\", path, err))\\n213: \\t\\t}\\n214: \\t\\tif apiResource != nil {\\n215: \\t\\t\\tapiResources = append(apiResources, *apiResource)\\n216: \\t\\t}\\n217: \\t\\tif resourceInfo != nil {\\n218: \\t\\t\\tresourceInfos = append(resourceInfos, resourceInfo)\\n219: \\t\\t}\\n220: \\t}\\n221: \\treturn apiResources, resourceInfos, ws, errors\\n222: }\\n223: \\n224: // newWebService creates a new restful webservice with the api installer's prefix and version.\\n225: func (a *APIInstaller) newWebService() *restful.WebService {\\n226: \\tws := new(restful.WebService)\\n227: \\tws.Path(a.prefix)\\n228: \\t// a.prefix contains \\\"prefix/group/version\\\"\\n229: \\tws.Doc(\\\"API at \\\" + a.prefix)\\n230: \\t// Backwards compatibility, we accepted objects with empty content-type at V1.\\n231: \\t// If we stop using go-restful, we can default empty content-type to application/json on an\\n232: \\t// endpoint by endpoint basis\\n233: \\tws.Consumes(\\\"*/*\\\")\\n234: \\tmediaTypes, streamMediaTypes := negotiation.MediaTypesForSerializer(a.group.Serializer)\\n235: \\tws.Produces(append(mediaTypes, streamMediaTypes...)...)\\n236: \\tws.ApiVersion(a.group.GroupVersion.String())\\n237: \\n238: \\treturn ws\\n239: }\\n240: \\n241: // calculate the storage gvk, the gvk objects are converted to before persisted to the etcd.\\n242: func getStorageVersionKind(storageVersioner runtime.GroupVersioner, storage rest.Storage, typer runtime.ObjectTyper) (schema.GroupVersionKind, error) {\\n243: \\tobject := storage.New()\\n244: \\tfqKinds, _, err := typer.ObjectKinds(object)\\n245: \\tif err != nil {\\n246: \\t\\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, err\\n247: \\t}\\n248: \\tgvk, ok := storageVersioner.KindForGroupVersionKinds(fqKinds)\\n249: \\tif !ok {\\n250: \\t\\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cannot find the storage version kind for %v\\\", reflect.TypeOf(object))\\n251: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go%3FL120-250\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "120: \t\t}\n121: \n122: \t\tvar scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope\n123: \t\tif r.Namespaced {\n124: \t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace\n125: \t\t} else {\n126: \t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster\n127: \t\t}\n128: \n129: \t\tparentidx, exists := parentResources[split[0]]\n130: \t\tif !exists {\n131: \t\t\t// If a subresource exists without a parent, create a parent\n132: \t\t\tapiResourceList = append(apiResourceList, apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{\n133: \t\t\t\tResource: split[0],\n134: \t\t\t\tScope:    scope,\n135: \t\t\t\t// avoid nil panics in v0.26.0-v0.26.3 client-go clients\n136: \t\t\t\t// see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/118361\n137: \t\t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{},\n138: \t\t\t})\n139: \t\t\tparentidx = len(apiResourceList) - 1\n140: \t\t\tparentResources[split[0]] = parentidx\n141: \t\t}\n142: \n143: \t\tif apiResourceList[parentidx].Scope != scope {\n144: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Error: Parent %s (scope: %s) and subresource %s (scope: %s) scope do not match\", split[0], apiResourceList[parentidx].Scope, split[1], scope)\n145: \t\t\t//\n146: \t\t}\n147: \n148: \t\tsubresource := apidiscoveryv2.APISubresourceDiscovery{\n149: \t\t\tSubresource: split[1],\n150: \t\t\tVerbs:       r.Verbs,\n151: \t\t\t// avoid nil panics in v0.26.0-v0.26.3 client-go clients\n152: \t\t\t// see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/118361\n153: \t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{},\n154: \t\t}\n155: \t\tif r.Kind != \"\" {\n156: \t\t\tsubresource.ResponseKind = &metav1.GroupVersionKind{\n157: \t\t\t\tGroup:   r.Group,\n158: \t\t\t\tVersion: r.Version,\n159: \t\t\t\tKind:    r.Kind,\n160: \t\t\t}\n161: \t\t}\n162: \t\tapiResourceList[parentidx].Subresources = append(apiResourceList[parentidx].Subresources, subresource)\n163: \t}\n164: \n165: \treturn apiResourceList, nil\n166: }\n167: \n168: // An interface to see if one storage supports override its default verb for monitoring\n169: type StorageMetricsOverride interface {\n170: \t// OverrideMetricsVerb gives a storage object an opportunity to override the verb reported to the metrics endpoint\n171: \tOverrideMetricsVerb(oldVerb string) (newVerb string)\n172: }\n173: \n174: // An interface to see if an object supports swagger documentation as a method\n175: type documentable interface {\n176: \tSwaggerDoc() map[string]string\n177: }\n178: \n179: // toDiscoveryKubeVerb maps an action.Verb to the logical kube verb, used for discovery\n180: var toDiscoveryKubeVerb = map[string]string{\n181: \trequest.MethodConnect:          \"\", // do not list in discovery.\n182: \trequest.MethodDelete:           \"delete\",\n183: \trequest.MethodDeleteCollection: \"deletecollection\",\n184: \trequest.MethodGet:              \"get\",\n185: \trequest.MethodList:             \"list\",\n186: \trequest.MethodPatch:            \"patch\",\n187: \trequest.MethodPost:             \"create\",\n188: \trequest.MethodProxy:            \"proxy\",\n189: \trequest.MethodPut:              \"update\",\n190: \trequest.MethodWatch:            \"watch\",\n191: \trequest.MethodWatchList:        \"watch\",\n192: }\n193: \n194: // Install handlers for API resources.\n195: func (a *APIInstaller) Install() ([]metav1.APIResource, []*storageversion.ResourceInfo, *restful.WebService, []error) {\n196: \tvar apiResources []metav1.APIResource\n197: \tvar resourceInfos []*storageversion.ResourceInfo\n198: \tvar errors []error\n199: \tws := a.newWebService()\n200: \n201: \t// Register the paths in a deterministic (sorted) order to get a deterministic swagger spec.\n202: \tpaths := make([]string, len(a.group.Storage))\n203: \tvar i int = 0\n204: \tfor path := range a.group.Storage {\n205: \t\tpaths[i] = path\n206: \t\ti++\n207: \t}\n208: \tsort.Strings(paths)\n209: \tfor _, path := range paths {\n210: \t\tapiResource, resourceInfo, err := a.registerResourceHandlers(path, a.group.Storage[path], ws)\n211: \t\tif err != nil {\n212: \t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Errorf(\"error in registering resource: %s, %v\", path, err))\n213: \t\t}\n214: \t\tif apiResource != nil {\n215: \t\t\tapiResources = append(apiResources, *apiResource)\n216: \t\t}\n217: \t\tif resourceInfo != nil {\n218: \t\t\tresourceInfos = append(resourceInfos, resourceInfo)\n219: \t\t}\n220: \t}\n221: \treturn apiResources, resourceInfos, ws, errors\n222: }\n223: \n224: // newWebService creates a new restful webservice with the api installer's prefix and version.\n225: func (a *APIInstaller) newWebService() *restful.WebService {\n226: \tws := new(restful.WebService)\n227: \tws.Path(a.prefix)\n228: \t// a.prefix contains \"prefix/group/version\"\n229: \tws.Doc(\"API at \" + a.prefix)\n230: \t// Backwards compatibility, we accepted objects with empty content-type at V1.\n231: \t// If we stop using go-restful, we can default empty content-type to application/json on an\n232: \t// endpoint by endpoint basis\n233: \tws.Consumes(\"*/*\")\n234: \tmediaTypes, streamMediaTypes := negotiation.MediaTypesForSerializer(a.group.Serializer)\n235: \tws.Produces(append(mediaTypes, streamMediaTypes...)...)\n236: \tws.ApiVersion(a.group.GroupVersion.String())\n237: \n238: \treturn ws\n239: }\n240: \n241: // calculate the storage gvk, the gvk objects are converted to before persisted to the etcd.\n242: func getStorageVersionKind(storageVersioner runtime.GroupVersioner, storage rest.Storage, typer runtime.ObjectTyper) (schema.GroupVersionKind, error) {\n243: \tobject := storage.New()\n244: \tfqKinds, _, err := typer.ObjectKinds(object)\n245: \tif err != nil {\n246: \t\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, err\n247: \t}\n248: \tgvk, ok := storageVersioner.KindForGroupVersionKinds(fqKinds)\n249: \tif !ok {\n250: \t\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot find the storage version kind for %v\", reflect.TypeOf(object))\n251: ",

sets.String\\n152: \\t// done values in this values for this map are ignored.\\n153: \\tPostStartHooks map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry\\n154: \\n155: \\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\\n156: \\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\\n157: \\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\\n158: \\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\\n159: \\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\\n160: \\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\\n161: \\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\\n162: \\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\\n163: \\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n164: \\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\\n165: \\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\\n166: \\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\\n167: \\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n168: \\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\\n169: \\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\\n170: \\t// AuditBackend is where audit events are sent to.\\n171: \\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\\n172: \\t// AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator makes the decision of whether and how to audit log a request.\\n173: \\tAuditPolicyRuleEvaluator audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator\\n174: \\t// ExternalAddress is the host name to use for external (public internet) facing URLs (e.g. Swagger)\\n175: \\t// Will default to a value based on secure serving info and available ipv4 IPs.\\n176: \\tExternalAddress string\\n177: \\n178: \\t// TracerProvider can provide a tracer, which records spans for distributed tracing.\\n179: \\tTracerProvider tracing.TracerProvider\\n180: \\n181: \\t//===========================================================================\\n182: \\t// Fields you probably don't care about changing\\n183: \\t//===========================================================================\\n184: \\n185: \\t// BuildHandlerChainFunc allows you to build custom handler chains by decorating the apiHandler.\\n186: \\tBuildHandlerChainFunc func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) (secure http.Handler)\\n187: \\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\\n188: \\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\\n189: \\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\\n190: \\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\\n191: \\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\\n192: \\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\\n193: \\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\\n194: \\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\\n195: \\t// DiscoveryAddresses is used to build the IPs pass to discovery. If nil, the ExternalAddress is\\n196: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL112-195\"}",
